Ordblind Ordbog Til brugeren ii

Til brugeren

Hvad er denne ordbog?

Ordblind Ordbog er en dansk–engelsk og engelsk–dansk ordbog med fokus på det ordforråd, der anvendes på PD3-eksamen (B2–C1 niveau). Ordbogen er udformet med dysleksivenlige principper: klare farver, konsekvent layout og en fonetisk vejledning på dansk, så udtalen kan læses direkte uden kendskab til IPA.

Ordvalg

Ordene er udvalgt på baggrund af frekvensdata fra Leipzig Corpora (dansk nyhedssprog, 1 million ord) samt PD3-pensum. Hvert opslag er mærket med et frekvensniveau: grundlæggende (top 500), almindelig (501–2.000), generel (2.001–5.000).

Brug ved eksamen

Ordbogen må anvendes ved eksamen. Ifølge bekendtgørelsen (bilagਃ) må alle ordbøger anvendes — med undtagelse af læseforståelsesprøven. Eksempelssætningerne er medtaget udelukkende som sproglige illustrationer af ordenes anvendelse. Er du i tvivl, bør du rådføre dig med dit sproglcenter inden eksamen.

Opbygning af et opslag

Se de følgende sider for en forklaring af alle symboler, farver og den fonetiske vejledning.

Kilder og licenser

Eksempelssætninger er hentet fra Tatoeba (tatoeba.org) — CC BY 2.0.
Oversættelses- og grammatikdata fra Wiktionary (en.wiktionary.org) — CC BY-SA 3.0.
Frekvensdata fra Leipzig Corpora Collection (wortschatz.uni-leipzig.de) — CC BY.

Ordblind Ordbog — Dansk–Engelsk og Engelsk–Dansk ii
Ordbog DA→EN Introduction i

How to Read an Entry

Every entry follows the same layout. Here is a typical noun entry:

dag en noun core
/daːˀɣ/ · DAG
day
Sikke en dag!
What a day!
time
dag Headword — the Danish word, set large and bold. Colour shows gender (nouns) or part of speech.
en Gender — terracotta = common (fælleskon); slate = neuter (intetkøn). Nouns only. Determines den vs det.
noun Part of speech — noun, verb, adjective, adverb, conjunction…
core Frequency — core top 500   common 501–2000   general 2001–5000   rare 5000+
/daːˀɣ/ IPA — precise phonetic transcription. Grey italic. See pronunciation guide (page iii).
DAG Phonetic guide — plain-English approximation in ALL CAPS on green. Read aloud directly. See sound key on page iii.
kom-me Syllables — where the word breaks, e.g. kom-me. Shown only for multi-syllable words.
day Primary translation — main English meaning.
also: … Alternative translations — other senses or related meanings.
Sikke en dag!
What a day!
 Example — real sentence from Tatoeba corpus (CC-BY). Danish bold; English italic below.
time Thematic tag — for finding words by topic in the index.

False friends ⚠

Some Danish words look identical to English words but mean something completely different. These are flagged with an amber banner — the two most important ones to know:

gift en noun general
/ɡift/ · YIFT
poison
also: venom; married
⚠ False friend — Means poison (noun) or married (adj), NOT a present
Er du gift?
Are you married?
chef en noun common
/ˈsjɛːˀf/ · CHEF
boss; person in charge
⚠ False friend — Means boss or manager, NOT a chef/cook
Jeg er min egen chef.
I’m my own boss.
work

Pronunciation trap 🔊

Entries flagged 🔊 Pronunciation trap contain sounds that are especially unexpected for English speakers — the soft-d, the stød, or unusual vowel combinations. Check the IPA and the pronunciation guide on page iii.

Introduction DA → EN
Ordbog DA→EN Colours & Symbols ii

Colours and Symbols

Gender — nouns

dag en
Common gender (fælleskon) — deep blue headword and badge. Use den as definite article: dagen.

dag, mand, tid, bog, ven, vej, uge, skov, krig, ting, regering, kærlighed

barn et
Neuter gender (intetkøn) — sky blue headword and badge. Use det as definite article: barnet.

barn, hus, land, år, navn, bord, liv, svar, spørgsmål, område

Parts of speech — headword colours and border

mand noun
Noun — deep blue (en) or sky blue (et) depending on gender.
komme verb
Verb — vermillion orange.
stor adjective
Adjective — teal green.
kun adverb
Adverb — rose purple.
og conjunction
Other (preposition, conjunction, phrase, interjection) — medium grey.

Frequency tiers

core
Core — top 500 most frequent words in Danish. Essential exam vocabulary.
common
Common — ranks 501–2,000. High-frequency everyday language.
general
General — ranks 2,001–5,000. Broad active vocabulary.
rare
Rare — rank 5,000+. Specialist, literary, or infrequent.

Special flags

⚠ False friend
False friend — looks like an English word but has a different meaning. gift = poison   chef = boss   blank = shiny   aktuel = current
🔊 Pron. trap
Pronunciation trap — sound is especially unexpected for English speakers. Check the IPA.

Thematic tags

Tags let you find related words in the thematic index even if you cannot spell the Danish word.

emotion body health food drink home nature weather work education bureaucracy transport time money social family culture politics environment technology
Colours & Symbols DA → EN
Ordbog DA→EN Pronunciation Guide iii

Danish Pronunciation

Danish spelling does not reliably predict pronunciation. Silent letters, vowel shifts, and the glot­tal stop (stød) make Danish harder to read aloud than it looks. Each entry gives two layers of help:

ALL-CAPS — a plain-English read-aloud approximation.   /IPA/ — precise phonetic transcription.

Vowels

SpellingIPACodeSounds likeExampleMeaning
a/aː/AH"a" in fatherdagday
e/eː/AY"a" in daysesee
i/iː/EE"ee" in feetviwe
o/oː/OH"o" in gostorlarge
u/uː/OO"oo" in foodhushouse
y/yː/EWRound lips for OO, say EEbytown
æ/ɛː/EH"e" in bedlæseread
ø/øː/UR"ur" in fur (British)grøngreen
å/ɔː/AW"aw" in lawåryear

Key consonants

SymbolIPACodeSounds likeExampleMeaning
d (soft)/ð/-TH-"th" in "the"badebathe
g (soft)/ɣ/-wFades to a soft "w"dagday
r/ʁ/—Back of throat, like French rrødred
ˀ stødˀ(catch)Catch in the throat — glottal stophunddog

The Stød (glottal stop)

Stød is a brief catch or creak in the throat — like the pause in “uh–oh”. In IPA it is written  ˀ  after the affected sound: hund /hunˀ/ (dog), se /seˀ/ (see). The phonetic ALL-CAPS codes do not mark stød — just be aware it exists.

Soft-D

When d appears between vowels or after a vowel at the end of a word, it softens to a fricative — like the th in “the”. Examples: bade (bathe), ved (at/by), sæde (seat). The code writes this as –TH–.

The Danish R

Danish r is uvular — produced at the very back of the throat, like French or German r. After a vowel it often weakens to a schwa /ɐ/ or disappears entirely. The ALL-CAPS codes mostly omit final r sounds as they are inaudible to English ears.

Reading the phonetic codes

The green ALL-CAPS codes use English letter combinations: AH (father), AY (day), EE (feet), OH (go), OO (food), EW (French tu), UR (British fur), AW (law). They are approximations only — always check the IPA for precise pronunciation.

Ordbog DA→EN absolut – advokat 5
A
absolut adjective general
[ɑbsoˈlud] · ABSOLUT · ab-so-lut
absolute, full, complete
also: absolute, nonrelative; absolutely; Absolute
Så absolut!
I'll say!
Absolut ikke.
No way.
acceptere verb general
ACCEPTERE · ac-cep-te-re
to accept
also: to recognize, validate, verify
Jeg kan ikke acceptere din undskyldning.
I do not accept your excuse.
adfærd en noun common
ADFEHRD · ad-færd
behaviour
Din adfærd lader meget tilbage at ønske.
Your behavior leaves much to be desired.
adgang en noun common
[ˈaðˌɡ̊ɑŋˀ] · ADGANG · ad-gang
access, permission, entry
Er der adgang for kørestolsbrugere?
Is there wheelchair access?
Har algerierne adgang til rent vand?
Do Algerians have access to clean water?
A
adresse et noun general
/adrɛsə/ · ADRESSE · adres-se
address, abbreviated adr.
Hvad er Toms adresse?
What's Tom's address?
Fandt du hans adresse?
Did you find his address?
advare verb rare
/advaːrə/ · ADVARE · ad-va-re
to warn (to make someone aware of impending danger)
also: caution
Vi prøvede at advare dem.
We tried to warn them.
Jeg forsøgte at advare dig.
I tried to warn you.
advarsel en noun rare
ADVARSEL · ad-var-sel
warning
also: caution (precept or warning against evil or danger); booking (the issuing of a caution)
Advarsel! Gulvet er vådt!
Caution! Wet floor.
Det er ikke en trussel, det er en advarsel.
That's not a threat, that is a warning.
advokat en noun general
/aðvoˈkad/ · ADVOKAT · ad-vo-kat
lawyer, attorney
Er Tom ikke advokat?
Isn't Tom a lawyer?
Han er en god advokat.
He is a good lawyer.
politics

Ordbog DA→EN afdeling – afholde 6
A
afdeling en noun common
AFDELING · af-de-ling
part (group inside a larger group)
also: section; division (section of a large company); department
Psykiatrisk afdeling er på 3. etage.
Mental health is on the third floor.
Det lokale supermarked har en økologisk afdeling.
The local supermarket has an organic section.
culture
afdød adjective rare
[ˈɑwˌtøðˀ] · AFDURD · af-død
deceased
"Er der nogen der kender navnet på den afdøde?"
"Does anyone know the name of the deceased?"
Den, som har haft størst indflydelse på mit liv, var ingen anden end min nu afdøde mor Mary.
The one who most influenced my life was no other than my late mother Mary.
affald et noun common
AFFALD · af-fald
trash, rubbish, garbage, waste
Der var affald overalt.
Garbage was everywhere.
I dag er det dagen for ikke-brændbart affald.
Today is a non-burnable rubbish day.
afgang en noun general
AFGANG · af-gang
departure
also: resignation, retirement; passage, excretion
Alle vognene i toget blev fyldt op ti minutter før afgang.
All the coaches of the train were packed to capacity ten minutes before it started.
culture
A
afgøre verb general
[ˈɑwˌɡ̊ɶˀɐ] · AFGURRE · af-gø-re
to decide, determine, settle
Jeg tror, det er afgørende.
"I think that's crucial."
Jeg tror at det er afgørende.
"I think that that's crucial."
afgørelse et noun general
AFGURREL-SEH · af-gø-rel-se
decision
also: determination; ruling; verdict
Det ligner en afgørelse.
It looks like a decision.
afgørende adjective common
/ˈavˌɡœrənə/ · AFGURRENDE · af-gø-ren-de
decisive
also: critical, crucial, key; conclusive; final
Jeg tror, det er afgørende.
I think that's crucial.
Jeg tror at det er afgørende.
I think that that's crucial.
afholde verb general
[ˈɑwˌhʌlˀə] · AFHOLDE · af-hol-de
to hold
also: to pay; to prevent, stop, keep from; to abstain
"Mødet afholdes på lørdag kl. 10:30."
The meeting will be held on Saturday at 10.30 a.m.
De olympiske lege afholdes hvert fjerde år.
The Olympic Games are held every four years.

[ˈɑwˌhʌlˀəs] · AFHOLDES · af-hol-des
to be held; to take place; to be conducted
Mødet afholdes på lørdag kl. 10:30.
The meeting will be held on Saturday at 10.30 a.m.
De olympiske lege afholdes hvert fjerde år.
The Olympic Games are held every four years.
afhænge verb rare
[ˈɑwˌhɛŋˀə] · AFHEHNYE · af-hæn-ge
to depend
Det afhænger af vejret.
It depends on the weather.
Det afhænger af konteksten.

AFHEHNYEE · af-hæn-gig
addicted
also: dependent
Er du afhængig?

Tom is dependent on Mary.
afkorte verb rare
AFKORTE · af-kor-te
to shorten
also: to abbreviate
"Havde det afkortet forløbet?"

aflevere verb general
[ˈɑʊ̯le̝ˌʋe̝ːˀɐ] · AFLEVERE · af-le-ve-re
To convey or hand over something.
also: to hand in an assignment
Jeg kan ikke beholde det her ur. Jeg skal aflevere det tilbage til Tom.
I can't keep this watch. I have to give it back to Tom.
body
aflive verb general
[ˈɑʊ̯ːliʋə] · AFLIVE · af-li-ve
to euthanize
Min dyrlæge rådede mig til at lade min hund aflive.
My vet recommended putting my dog to sleep.
aflyse verb common
AFLEWSE · af-ly-se
to cancel
Hun burde aflyse festen.
She should cancel her party.
Vi hørte at vejret var dårligt, så vi besluttede os for at aflyse turen.
We heard it was bad weather, so we decided to cancel the trip.
aflyst adjective common
AFLEWST · af-lyst
cancelled; called off
Mødet blev aflyst.
The meeting was cancelled.

aflyste verb general
AFLEWSTE · af-ly-ste
to past
De aflyste.
They canceled.
Til alles overraskelse aflyste Tom brylluppet.
To everybody's surprise, Tom called the wedding off.
afskaffe verb rare
AFSKAFFE · af-skaf-fe
to disband
also: to abolish
Vi burde afskaffe dødsstraffen.
We should do away with the death penalty.
Vi må afskaffe disse dårlige vaner.
We must do away with such bad customs.
afslutning en noun general
AFSLUTNING · af-slut-ning
end, finale
Handelshindringer blev ophævet efter krigens afslutning.
Trade barriers were lifted after the war ended.
afslutte verb rare
AFSLUTTE · af-slut-te
to finish, end
also: to close, conclude; to finalize, complete; to terminate, exit
Vi kan afslutte det senere.
We can finish it later.
Han vil afslutte arbejdet i morgen.
He will end the work by tomorrow.
A
afsnit et noun general
AFSNIT · af-snit
part, paragraph or passage in a text
also: section; episode; segment
Hvornår sendes næste afsnit?

When will the next episode air?

afstand en noun core
/avstanˀ/ · AFSTAND · af-stand
distance

afsted adverb common
AFSTED · af-sted
af sted

afsætte verb rare
AFSEHTTE · af-sæt-te
to set aside, earmark, allocate
also: to leave behind; to dethrone, overthrow; to sell

aftage verb rare
AFTAYE · af-ta-ge
to decrease, lessen
also: to buy or snap up; to remove
Kærlighed er som månen; når den ikke er i tiltagen, er den i aftagen.
Love is like the moon; when it does not wax, it wanes.

aftale en noun common
/avtalə/ · AFTALE · af-ta-le
arrangement
also: agreement; deal; appointment

aften en noun core
/ˈaftən/ · AFTEN · af-ten
evening
also: night (evening)
I aften lyder godt.

Sikke en smuk aften!
What a beautiful evening!

afvikle verb general
AFVIKLE · af-vik-le
to discontinue an enterprise, especially gradually and in an ordered manner
also: to cause some activity to take place
"På hvilket sprog blev mødet afviklet?"

A
afvise verb general
/avviːˀsə/ · AFVISE · af-vi-se
to reject
also: to deny
Ingen ved hvorfor han afviser min hjælp.
Nobody knows why he turns down my help.
aktion en noun general
AKTION · ak-tion
action
En norsk diplomat havde været fjendligt indstillet over for Sovjetunionen. Så KGB igangsatte en aktion der skulle kompromittere nordmanden. Han havde en russisk kæreste og han blev bedt om at forlade landet straks.
A Norwegian diplomat had been hostile to the Soviet Union, so the KGB initiated a compromising action against the Norwegian. He had a Russian girlfriend and was asked to immediately leave the country.
aktiv adjective general
/aktiːˀv/ · AKTIW · ak-tiv
active
also: top; active voice
Sami var aktiv på Facebook.
Sami was active on Facebook.
Liisa er en aktiv og energisk ung kvinde.
Liisa is an active and energetic young woman.
aktivitet en noun general
AKTIVITET · ak-ti-vi-tet
activity
Dan hvidvasker alle sine penge i lovlige aktiviteter i Canada.
Dan launders all of his money in legal activities in Canada.

relevant, of interest, topical
also: current, present

ALDER · al-der
age
"Alder?" "16."
"Age?" "16."
Tom løj om sin alder.

aldrig adverb common
ALDREE · al-drig
never (at no time)
Aldrig igen!
Never again!
De kom aldrig.
They never came.
alene adjective common
[ˈæle̝ːnə] · ALENE · ale-ne
alone
also: only; just
Er du alene?

Han er alene.
He's alone.

alkohol en noun general
ALKOHOL · al-ko-hol
alcohol
Jeg drikker alkohol.
I drink alcohol.
Alkohol løser ingen problemer.
Alcohol doesn't solve any problems.
allerede adverb core
ALLERETHE · al-le-re-de
already (prior to some time, so soon)
Det regner allerede.
It rains already.
Jeg er allerede rig.
I'm already rich.
time
alligevel adverb common
ALLIYEVEL · al-li-ge-vel
nonetheless, nevertheless
Jeg elsker hende alligevel.
I love her anyway.
Han dukkede alligevel ikke op.
He didn't turn up after all.
almindelig adjective common
ALMINDELEE · al-min-de-lig
ordinary
also: usual; common; general (not specific or particular)
Kolera er ikke almindelig i Japan.
Cholera is uncommon in Japan.
Hun mangler almindelig sund fornuft.
She is lacking in common sense.

altid adverb core
/altid/ · ALTID · al-tid
always
Du ser altid tv.

Tom ryger altid.
Tom is always smoking.
altså adverb core
ALTSAW · alt-så
so; then; well; that is to say
Ih altså!

Jeg tænker, altså er jeg.
I think, therefore I am.
alvor en noun common
[ˈalˌʋɒˀ] · ALVOR · al-vor
seriousness (state of being serious)
also: gravity; earnestness
Det lyder alvorligt.
That sounds serious.
Han er alvorligt syg.
He is severely ill.

alvorlig adjective common
/alvɔrli/ · ALVORLEE · al-vor-lig
serious (important; weighty; not trifling; leaving no room for play)
also: grave; earnest; grievous
Tom var meget alvorlig.
Tom was very serious.
Han lider af en meget alvorlig sygdom.
He is suffering from a very serious illness.
home
alvorligt adjective common
/alvɔrlit/ · ALVORLIGT · al-vor-ligt
seriously, gravely, earnestly, severely, grievously, thoroughly
Det lyder alvorligt.
That sounds serious.
Hun er alvorligt syg.
She is severely ill.
ambulance en noun general
AMBULANCE · am-bu-lan-ce
Q180481 ambulance emergency vehicle
Vi har brug for en ambulance.
We need an ambulance.
Ringede du efter en ambulance?
Did you call an ambulance?
amerikansk adjective general
AMERIKANSK · ame-ri-kansk
American; of or pertaining to the Americas.
also: American; of or pertaining to the United States of America (USA).
Han taler amerikansk.
He speaks American English.
Han er amerikansk jøde.
He's an American Jew.

analyse en noun general

analysis; examination; study

His analysis of the causes of the uprising was correct.
analysere verb rare
ANALEWSERE · ana-ly-se-re
to analyze
Lægen analyserede blodprøven for anæmi.
The doctor analyzed the blood sample for anemia.
anbefale verb rare
ANBEFALE · an-be-fa-le
To recommend
also: To recommend, commend; To recommend, advise
Jeg kan anbefale en god læge.
I can recommend a good doctor.
Kan du anbefale en god ordbog?
Can you recommend a good dictionary?
anbefaling en noun general
ANBEFALING · an-be-fa-ling
recommendation; endorsement
Det var ikke en god anbefaling.
That was not a good recommendation.
Det var en anbefaling, ikke en ordre.

andel en noun general
[ˈanˌd̥eˀl] · ANDEL · an-del
share, part, proportion
Det er en meget høj andel.
That's a very high percentage.
culture
anden adjective core
/ˈanən/ · ANDEN · an-den
other, another, else
also: second
Find en anden.
Find somebody else.
Er der en anden?
Is there someone else?
anderledes adjective common
/ˈɑnɐˌleːðˀəs/ · ANDERLETHES · an-der-le-des
different
also: differently
Gid jeg var anderledes!
I wish I were different.
Du ved jeg er anderledes.
You know I'm different.
angivelig adjective rare
ANYIVELEE · an-gi-ve-lig
alleged, claimed
also: reportedly, purportedly, supposedly; really, actually, in fact, notoriously
Det er angiveligt ikke akut.
It is allegedly not acute.

ANGBCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN angreb – anlæg 13
A
angreb et noun common
[ˈanˌɡ̊ʁɛˀb̥] · ANGREB · an-greb
attack (offense of a battle)
also: assault; raid; charge
Hun angreb fjenden.
She attacked the enemy.
Toms hund angreb Mary.
Tom's dog attacked Mary.
angriber en noun general
/anɡriːbər/ · ANGRIBER · an-gri-ber
attacker (someone who attacks, offensive soccer player)
Vi angriber ved solopgang.
We will attack at sunrise.
Ingen angriber mig ustraffet.
No one attacks me unpunished.
angst adjective general
ANGST
afraid, anxious, alarmed
also: fear, anxiety, alarm, apprehension, dread
Helen skreg af angst.
Helen screamed in fear.
body
anholde verb rare
/anhɔlə/ · ANHOLDE · an-hol-de
to arrest (to seize and take into legal custody)
Han skulle anholdes.
He should be arrested.
body
A
anklage en noun rare
ANKLAYE · an-kla-ge
an accusation
also: a indictment, charge; to accuse
Hun blev falsk anklaget.
She was falsely accused.
Han blev falsk anklaget.
He was accused falsely.
ankomme verb rare
ANKOMME · an-kom-me
To get to a certain place; to arrive.
Han vil ankomme der omkring klokken fem.
He will arrive there about five o'clock.
Hvis hun kommer med fly, burde Sue ankomme meget snart.
If Sue is coming by air, she ought to arrive very soon.
anledning en noun common
ANLEDNING · an-led-ning
cause, occasion, reason
also: occasion, opportunity
Tom har en kort lunte og farer i flint ved den mindste anledning.
Tom's got a short fuse and flies off the handle at the drop of a hat.
Det er mig altid en glæde når jeg finder en anledning til at sige hej til dig.
I am always pleased when I find a reason to say hello to you.
family
anlæg et noun general
ANLEHG · an-læg
a larger facility, plant or construction
also: an installation; the planning and construction of something; predisposition, trait, aptitude
"Der lukkes for vandet i morgen fra kl. 13 til kl. 15 på grund af anlægsarbejde. Husk det nu!"
"The water will be cut off tomorrow from 1 p.m. to 3 p.m. due to construction work. Don't forget."
nature

A
anmelde verb rare
ANMELDE · an-mel-de
to report
also: to announce; to review; to inform, notify
"Hvad sagde anmelderne?"
"What did the critics say?"
Anmelderne var uimponerede.
The critics were unimpressed.
anmeldelse et noun common
[ˈanˌmɛlˀəlsə] · ANMELDEL-SEH · an-mel-del-se
A review, a reception, criticism.
Læs anmeldelsen her.
Read the review here.
ansigt et noun general
[ˈanˌseɡd̥] · ANSIGT · an-sigt
face
Vask dit ansigt.
Wash your face.
Jeg har tabt ansigt.
I lost face.
body
anstrengelse et noun rare
ANSTRENYEL-SEH · an-stren-gel-se
effort, exertion, strain
Alle hans anstrengelser var forgæves.
All his efforts were useless.
Alle mine anstrengelser gav ingen resultater.
My efforts produced no results.
weather transport
A
ansvar et noun common
ANSVAR · an-svar
responsibility the state of being responsible
also: liability an obligation, debt or responsibility owed to someone; blame
Det er dit ansvar.
That's your responsibility.
Det er helt klart Toms ansvar.
It's clearly Tom's responsibility.
ansætte verb rare
/ansɛtə/ · ANSEHTTE · an-sæt-te
to hire, employ, appoint
also: to estimate, assess
Ingen vil ansætte dig.
Nobody's going to hire you.
Ingen vil ansætte jer.
Nobody's going to hire you.
work technology
ansøgning en noun rare
ANSURNING · an-søg-ning
an application; a written request for money, help, asylum, work etc.
Sidste frist for ansøgning er i overmorgen.
The last day to apply is the day after tomorrow.
Den sidste dag for ansøgning er i overmorgen.
The last day to apply is the day after tomorrow.
work bureaucracy money
antal et noun common
ANTAL · an-tal
number (of something); quantity of something countable
Jeg har et stort antal bøger i min bogreol.
I have a large number of books on my bookshelf.
Facebook har efterhånden fået en så vigtig betydning i vores samfund, at et stigende antal arbejdsgivere – og endda nogle psykologer – mener, at folk, der ikke har en profil på det sociale netværk, er "mistænkelige".
Facebook has now been invested with such significance in our society that a growing number of employers — and even some psychologists — are of the opinion that people who don't have a profile on this social network are "suspicious".

arbejde et noun core
/ˈarˌbajdə/ · ARBAYDE · ar-bej-de
work (labour, employment, occupation, job)
also: work; work (effort expended on a particular task); work, to do a specific task (Generally for money)

/ˈɑːbɑjdsˌplas/ · ARBAYDSPLADS · ar-bejds-plads

areal et noun general
/areaːl/ · AREAL · are-al
area, space
also: acreage
Så meget areal nytter noget.

argument et noun rare
ARGUMENT · ar-gu-ment
argument

august en noun common

This offer expires on August 15, 1999.
avis en noun general
/aˈviːˀs/ · AVIS
newspaper
Hvor er din avis?

Han købte en avis.
He bought a newspaper.

bag en noun core
/baːˀɣ/ · BAG
behind, bottom, butt, buttocks
also: seat; behind; behind
Kig bag dig.
Look behind you.
Gem dig bag sofaen!

bagefter adverb general
/baːjɛftər/ · BAYEFTER · bag-ef-ter
behind
also: afterward, afterwards, subsequently; slow
Uret er ti minutter bagefter.
The clock is ten minutes slow.
Bagefter er man altid klogere.
Afterwards you are always smarter.

baggrund en noun common
[ˈb̥ɑwˌɡ̊ʁɔnˀ] · BAGGRUND · bag-grund
background
Se baggrunden her.

hill, rise, slope
also: tray, salver; jaw (of a tool)
Det gik ned ad bakke.
It went downhill.

bakke verb general
/ˈb̥agə/ · BAK-ke · bak-ke
to reverse; to back (a vehicle)
also: to back up; to support (bakke op om)
Han bakkede bilen forsigtigt ind i garagen.
He carefully reversed the car into the garage.
Partiet bakkede op om forslaget.
The party backed the proposal.

a balance sheet

bange adjective common
/baŋə/ · BANYE · ban-ge
afraid, frightened, scared
Vi er bange.

also: knock; rapping sound; a beating

just
also: simply; only, merely; I wish, I hope, if only

child (immature human)

/bɛst/ · BEDST
; best

befinde verb rare
BEFINDE · be-fin-de
to be located
also: to find, to deem, to determine
Vi befinder os i en blindgyde.
"We're in a blind alley."
"På hvilken planet befinder du dig?"
"On what planet are you?"
befolkning en noun general
BEFOLNING · be-folk-ning
population (people living within a political or geographical boundary)
Sveriges befolkning vokser.
The population of Sweden is on the increase.
Landets samlede befolkning er 300 millioner.
The total population of the country is 300 million.
begge pronoun common
[ˈb̥ɛɡ̊ə] · BEGYE · beg-ge
both
Begge lever.
Both are alive.
Begge er døde.

begrænse verb common
BEGREHNSE · be-græn-se
to limit
also: to restrict; to constrain; to curb
Vi har begrænset plads.
We have limited space.
Vi har begrænsede midler.

begynde verb common
/beɡønə/ · BEGEWNDE · be-gyn-de
to start, begin, commence, open
Lad os begynde.

Du må godt begynde.

begyndelse et noun rare
/beɡønəlsə/ · BEGEWNDEL-SEH · be-gyn-del-se
beginning, start, commencement
Al begyndelse er svær.

Alt, der har en begyndelse, har også en ende.
Everything that has a beginning has an end.

to commit
also: create or produce

B
behandle verb general
BEHANDLE · be-hand-le
to treat
Lad være med at behandle mig som et barn.
Don't treat me like a child.
Vi er nødt til at lære at behandle hinanden med respekt.
We have to learn to treat each other with respect.
food
behandling en noun common
BEHANDLING · be-hand-ling
treatment
also: processing; management
Jeg skal i behandling.
I have to receive treatment.
Jeg har brug for palliativ behandling.
I need palliative care.
food
beholde verb general
[b̥eˈhʌlˀə] · BEHOLDE · be-hol-de
to keep, retain, maintain
Du kan beholde bogen.
You may keep the book.
Yanni kan beholde de penge, han har tjent.
Yanni can keep the money he made.
behov et noun common
/behɔv/ · BEHOW · be-hov
need, requirement
Folk har en tendens til at undervurdere deres fremtidige behov.
People have a tendency to underestimate their future needs.
B
behøve verb rare
/beˈhøːˀvə/ · BEHURVE · be-hø-ve
to need
Hun behøver hjælp.
She needs help.
Jeg behøver dem ikke.
"I don't need them."
bekendt adjective common
[b̥eˈkʰɛnˀd̥] · BEKENDT · be-kendt
known
also: well-known, famous; acceptable; acquaintance, friend
Det navn virkede bekendt.
That name rung a bell.
Jeg tog det for givet, at du var bekendt med faren.
I took it for granted that you were aware of the danger.
beklage verb rare
BEKLAYE · be-kla-ge
to apologize for
also: express dissatisfaction about; to complain; express one's sympathy with someone's plight
Vi beklager.
"We're sorry."
"Jeg beklager!"
"I'm sorry."
bekræfte verb rare
BEKREHFTE · be-kræf-te
to confirm
also: to verify; to approve
Jeg kan bekræfte at Tom taler sandt.
I can confirm that Tom is telling the truth.
Jeg kan bekræfte at Tom fortæller sandheden.
I can confirm that Tom is telling the truth.

bekymre verb rare
BEKEWMRE · be-kym-re
to worry (be a source of concern)
also: to worry (be worried)
Hvorfor bekymre sig om Tom?
Why worry about Tom?
Hvorfor bekymre sig om det?
Why worry about it?
emotion
bekymring en noun common
BEKEWMRING · be-kym-ring
concern, worry
Der er ingen grund til bekymring.
There's no cause for concern.
Der er absolut ingen grund til bekymring.
There's absolutely no cause for concern.
emotion
bekæmpe verb general
BEKEHMPE · be-kæm-pe
to fight (physically), to be in armed conflict with
also: to counteract, overcome, destroy, combat (a disease, a pest, a setback, etc)
Algeriet skal bekæmpe korruption.
Algeria needs to fight corruption.
body
belaste verb rare
BELASTE · be-la-ste
To encumber.
Det er voldsomt belastende.
It's very stressful.
B
beløb et noun common
/be løːb/ · BELURB · be-løb
a certain amount of money
Hvad er det samlede beløb?
What's the total?
Er dette beløb med moms, eller skal momsen lægges til?
Is this amount inclusive of GST, or does it have to be added on?
money
ben et noun general
BEN
leg a limb of a human or an animal used for walking; also, by analogy, the legs of a desk
also: bone any part of the skeleton; sinecure a position that requires little to no work but still gives an ample payment; a
Mit ben klør.
My leg itches.
Han har lange ben.
His legs are long.
benytte verb general
/benøtə/ · BENEWTTE · be-nyt-te
to use
Jeg besluttede at benytte lejligheden til at tage hen og besøge ham.
I decided to take the opportunity to go and visit him.
beredskab et noun general
BEREDSKAB · be-red-skab
preparedness, standby, readiness
Alle var i højeste beredskab.
Everyone was on high alert.

Ordbog DA→EN berøre – beslutte 21
B
berøre verb rare
/berøːˀrə/ · BERURRE · be-rø-re
to touch
Det berører mig dybt, sagde hun.
It touches me deeply, she said.
besiddelse en noun general
BESIDDEL-SEH · be-sid-del-se
possession
also: property
Dit afkom skal tage sine fjenders porte i besiddelse, og i din sæd skal alle jordens folk velsignes, fordi du adlød mig.
'Your offspring shall possess their enemies' gates, and in your seed shall be all nations blessed because you obeyed my command thus.'
besked en noun common
/beskeːˀð/ · BESKED · be-sked
message
also: order, instruction
Fik Tom min besked?
Did Tom get my message?
Er der nogen besked?
Can I take a message?
beskeden adjective general
/beskeːˀðən/ · BESKETHEN · be-ske-den
modest, unassuming
also: modest, moderate, humble; plain, simple
Han er en meget beskeden mand.
He is a very modest man.
B
beskrive verb rare
/beskriːˀvə/ · BESKRIVE · be-skri-ve
to describe
Hvordan vil du beskrive dig selv?
How would you describe yourself?
Hvordan ville du beskrive dig selv?
How would you describe yourself?
beskytte verb common
BESKEWTTE · be-skyt-te
to defend, protect
Kan du beskytte mig?
Can you protect me?
Jeg ville beskytte dig.
I wanted to protect you.
beslutning en noun common
BESLUTNING · be-slut-ning
decision
Det var en dum beslutning.
That was a dumb decision.
Denne beslutning er endelig.
This decision is final.
beslutte verb general
[b̥eˈslud̥ə] · BESLUTTE · be-slut-te
to decide (come to a resolution or judgement)
Tom kunne ikke beslutte sig for om hans hund skulle hedde Cookie eller Pochi.
Tom couldn't decide whether to name his dog Cookie or Pochi.

a cost saving (measure that consists of reducing one or more expenses and thereby saving money)

money
bestemt adjective common
[b̥eˈsd̥ɛmˀd̥] · BESTEMT · be-stemt
specific, determinate
also: definite; resolute, determined; certainly, definitely
Din plan vil bestemt lykkes.
Your plan is sure to succeed.
Jeg har intet bestemt at sige.
I don't have anything particular to say.
bestil verb rare
BESTIL · be-stil
to bestille
"Hvad bestiller du?"
"What do you do for a living?"
"Bestilte du bogen?"

bestille verb general
BESTILLE · be-stil-le
to order
also: to undertake, accomplish
Jeg vil gerne bestille en pizza.
I'd like to order a pizza.
Jeg vil gerne bestille det samme.
I'd like to order the same.

bestyrelse et noun general
BESTEWREL-SEH · be-sty-rel-se
management
Symbs nye bestyrelse.
Symb's new board.
bestå verb rare
[b̥eˈsd̥ɔˀ] · BESTAW · be-stå
to consist
also: to persist, to endure, to continue to exist; to pass
Han forventes at bestå eksamen.
It is expected that he will succeed in the examination.
Hvis du arbejder hårdt, vil du bestå din eksamen.
If you work hard, you'll pass your exam.
besøg et noun common
[b̥eˈsøˀj] · BESURG · be-søg
a visit
Kom og besøg os!
Do come and visit us.
Han nød de besøg.
He enjoyed those visits.
social
besøge verb common
[b̥eˈsøˀjə] · BESURYE · be-sø-ge
to visit (to go and meet or see)
Lad os besøge Boston igen.
Let's visit Boston again.
New York er værd at besøge.

B
betale verb common
/beˈtaːˀlə/ · BETALE · be-ta-le
to pay
Jeg vil betale dig.
I'll pay you.
Jeg nægter at betale.
I refuse to pay.
money
beting verb rare
BETING · be-ting
to imp
Tom fik en seks måneders betinget dom.
Tom was given a six-month suspended sentence.
En hund vil altid elske dig betingelsesløst.
A dog will always love you unconditionally.
betingelse et noun rare
BETINYEL-SEH · be-tin-gel-se
condition, prerequisite, requirement
En hund vil altid elske dig betingelsesløst.
A dog will always love you unconditionally.
Jeg ønsker ikke at arbejde under disse betingelser.
"I don't want to work under these conditions."
betjent en noun rare
BETYENT · be-tjent
police officer; constable
Spørg en betjent!
Ask a policeman!
Bliver du betjent?
Is anybody waiting on you?
B
betyde verb common
[beˈtyˀðə] · BETEWDE · be-ty-de
to mean (convey, signify, indicate)
Mammografi kan betyde forskellen mellem liv og død for millioner af kvinder.
Mammography can mean the difference between life and death for millions of women.
betydelig adjective general
BETEWDELEE · be-ty-de-lig
considerable, significant
also: of great importance or significance; considerably, significantly
Fra begrænset til betydelig.
From limited to significant.
betydning en noun common
BETEWDNING · be-tyd-ning
meaning (symbolic value of something)
also: significance; importance; relevance
Har det nogen betydning?
Does it matter?
Musikkens betydning undervurderes.
The importance of music is underrated.
bevare verb general
BEVARE · be-va-re
to preserve, protect
Forsøg at bevare roen!
Try to stay calm.
Gud bevare dronningen.
God save the Queen.

BEVIDST · be-vidst
aware
also: conscious; deliberate; deliberately
Tom afsonede 30 år for bevidst uagtsomt manddrab.
Tom served 30 years for second degree murder.
Det Arabiske Forår var ikke en fejltagelse men en bevidst fejl.
The Arab Spring was not a mistake but an intentional mistake.
bevis et noun rare
BEVIS · be-vis
proof
En påstand er ikke et bevis.
An assertion isn't a proof.
Bevis at P er en delvis ordnet mængde.
Prove that P is a poset.
bevæge verb general
BEVEHYE · be-væ-ge
to move (oneself)
also: to move (something else); to move (someone), to stir someone's emotions
Kan du bevæge ørene?
Can you move your ears?
Kan du bevæge ørerne?
Can you move your ears?
bevægelse et noun general
[b̥eˈvɛˀjəlsə] · BEVEHYEL-SEH · be-væ-gel-se
movement
Åbn aldrig døren på en bil der er i bevægelse.
Never open the door of a car that is in motion.
Heste har tre former for bevægelse: Skridt, trav og galop.
Horses have three modes of locomotion: Walk, trot and gallop.
B
bibliotek et noun general
[bibl(i)oˈtˢeˀɡ] · BIBLIOTEK · bi-bli-o-tek
library
Hvor er det nærmeste bibliotek?
Where's the nearest library?
De byggede dette bibliotek i 2013.
They built this library in 2013.
bidrag et noun general
BIDRAG · bi-drag
A contribution.
also: A subscription.; Input.
Alle bidrag er fradragsberettigede.
All contributions are tax deductible.
bidrage verb common
BIDRAYE · bi-dra-ge
to contribute
also: to contribute to
Den bedste måde at hjælpe os er ved at bidrage på dit eget modersmål.
The best way to help us is to contribute in your own native language.
bil en noun common
/biːl/ · BIL
car, automobile
Bil!
Car!
I bil!
By car!
transport

/bjœrn/
bear

Du er blandt venner.

/bliːə/
to become

also: rock, boulder; notepad; bloc

mere, very

blæst en noun general
/blɛst/ · BLEHST
wind
also: windy weather; blown; barmy

We had our roof blown off.
I udkanten af lysningen ligger stammerne af flere træer, der er blæst omkuld af stormen.

weather

/blɔːˀ/ · BLAW
blue

/bɔrɣə/ · BORYE · bor-ge

borger en noun common

/branˀ/ · BRAND
fire

also: shore; bank

BREMSE · brem-se
brake
also: to brake; a botfly
Lad være med at bremse.

Jeg har bemærket at buschaufførerne ikke kan bremse.

brev et noun general
/breːˀv/ · BREW
letter
also: note; epistle
Her er dit brev.

bringe en noun common
/brenɡə/ · BRINYE · brin-ge
chest
also: to bring; to publish
Ingen ved, hvad morgendagen vil bringe.
No one knows what tomorrow will bring.
Det var et meget ubelejligt tidspunkt at bringe emnet op.

[ˈb̥ʁid̥isɡ̊] · BRITISK · bri-tisk

use, application

BRUYER · bru-ger

Do you use Skype?

She uses Botox.
bryde verb general
/bryːðə/ · BREWDE · bry-de
to break; to violate
also: to break; to break, violate; to break
Tom forsøgte at bryde isen.

[ˈb̥uð] · BUD
command; commandment
also: message; errand (send bud = send word, send for someone); bid (at auction); offer

The ten commandments are the basis of many laws.

/byˈɕɛt/ · BUDYET · bud-get

Do you have a budget?
Vi rejser på et stramt budget.

BUDSKAB · bud-skab
message
also: news (new information of interest)
Han ønsker at komme i fængsel for at sprede budskabet.

bund en noun general
[ˈb̥ɔnˀ] · BUND
bottom
Der er lidt vin tilbage i glassets bund.
There is a little wine left in the bottom of the glass.
Da der ikke fandtes fingeraftryk, stod politiet på bar bund.
Without fingerprints, the police had nothing to go on.

/bus/ · BUS

There's our bus.

[b̥uˈtˢiɡ̊] · BUTIK · bu-tik
store, shop

[ˈb̥yˀ] · BEW

byde verb general
[ˈb̥yːðə] · BEWDE · by-de
to order, command, bid

Han har ikke meget at byde på.

byge en noun rare
BEWYE · by-ge
a shower of rain, hail or snow

I was caught in a shower on my way to the station.

bygge verb common
/byɡə/ · BEWGYE · byg-ge
to build, construct

headquarters, place whence organizations are administrated

dele verb common
DELE · de-le
to divide
also: to share; to split

[ˈd̥eˀls] · DELS
partly

/ˌdemʌnsdʁɑˈɕoˀn/ · DEMONSTRATION · de-mon-stra-tion
demonstration
Maria, der lige er blevet udnævnt til National koordinator mod voldelig ekstremisme, deltog i en demonstration, hvor man rituelt afbrændte det elbonske flag.
Maria, who has just been appointed as National Coordinator against violent extremism, participated in a demonstration in which the Elbonian flag was ritually burned.
dengang adverb common
DENGANG · den-gang
back then, at that time
also: back then, when, at that time (where)...
Hvad lavede I dengang?
What were you guys doing then?
Tom var dengang tretten.
Tom was thirteen then.
body time
D
denne pronoun core
[ˈd̥ɛnə] · DENNE · den-ne
this; this one
also: the latter, the lastly mentioned
Denne vej!
Right this way.
Anvend denne.
Use this.
dens pronoun general
DENS
its, possessive form of den
Dens navn er Tom.
Its name is Tom.
Dens farve er rød.
Its color is red.
bureaucracy
derefter adverb common
DEREFTER · der-ef-ter
after that, thereafter, afterwards
Hvad kommer der derefter?
What's next?
Derefter mistede hun bevidstheden.
Then, she lost consciousness.
deres pronoun core
[ˈd̥ɛɒ̯̽s] · DERES · de-res
their
Det er deres valg.
It's their choice.
De bøger er deres.
Those books are theirs.
Danish–English Dictionary  ·  Tatoeba (CC-BY)  ·  Leipzig (CC-BY) enetverbadjadvother DA → EN
ABCDEREFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN derfor – desto 37
D
derfor adverb core
/ˈd̥eːɐ̯.foɐ̯/ · DERFOR · der-for
therefore
also: for this, for that, for it
Det er derfor Tom kom.
That's why Tom came.
Derfor tog jeg det op.
That's why I brought it up.
derfra adverb general
DERFRA · der-fra
from there, thence
Hvordan kom du ud derfra?
How did you get out of there?
Forholdene blev bare ved med at gå ned ad bakke derfra.
Things just kept going downhill from there.
derimod adverb common
DERIMOD · der-i-mod
however
also: in opposition hereto
Ukraine sidder ikke med ved bordet, men landet er derimod på menuen.
Ukraine is not at the negotiation table, it's on the menu.
Alle de her egern er blevet vaccineret tre gange og taler om den fjerde vaccination. Tom er derimod vaccinenægter.
All these squirrels are triple vaccinated and are talking about the fourth vaccination. Tom, however, is a vaccine refuser.
dermed adverb common
[ˈdɛˀɐ̯ˈmɛð] · DERMED · der-med
thus
I sit essay »Esperanto: et europæisk eller et asiatisk sprog« påviste Claude Piron lighederne mellem esperanto og kinesisk og aflivede dermed forestillingen om at esperanto kun var et europæisk orienteret sprog.
In his essay "Esperanto: European or Asiatic language" Claude Piron has shown the similarities between Esperanto and Chinese, thereby putting to rest the notion that Esperanto is purely eurocentric.
D
dertil adverb general
DERTIL · der-til
moreover; in addition; to that end; for that purpose
also: there; to that place (directional)
Ring til mig, når du er kommet dertil.
When you get there, call me.
Det tager mindst en time at komme dertil.
It takes an hour to get there, at least.
derude adverb general
DERUTHE · der-u-de
out there
Jeg tror, der er nogen derude.
I think there's someone out there.
Hvad laver han derude? Inviter ham indenfor!
What's he doing out there? Invite him inside!
derudover adverb common
DERUTHOVER · der-u-d-over
in addition, on top of what has just been mentioned
Derudover blev de slået.
In addition, they were beaten.
desto conjunction general
DESTO · de-sto
the (forming the parallel comparative with )
Jo før, desto bedre.
The sooner, the better.
Jo kortere, desto bedre.
The shorter the better.
bureaucracy

D
desuden adverb common
DESUTHEN · des-u-den
furthermore, moreover
Desuden kan jeg flyve.
Moreover, I can fly.
desværre adverb common
/desvɛrə/ · DESVEHRRE · desvær-re
regrettably, unfortunately
Desværre!
Unfortunately!
detalje et noun rare
DETALYE · de-tal-je
a detail, a circumlocution, a specific
Jeg vil have detaljer.
I want specific information.
Djævlen ligger i detaljen.
The devil is in the details.
dets pronoun general
DETS
its, possessive form of
Dets navn er Tom.
Its name is Tom.
Man kan kende et menneske på dets venner.
You can know a man by his friends.
bureaucracy
D
diagram et noun rare
DIAGRAM · di-a-gram
diagram
Jeg vil gerne se på det diagram.
I'd like to look at that chart.
dialog en noun common
[diaˈloˀ] · DIALOG · di-a-log
dialogue
En frugtbar dialog er ikke kun godt for USA og Rusland, men godt for hele verden.
A productive dialogue is not only good for the United States and good for Russia, but it is good for the world.
digital adjective general
/diɡiˈtæˀl/ · DIYITAL · di-gi-tal
digital representing discrete values
also: digital of or relating to computers or the Information Age
Udviklingen indenfor digital teknologi skrider frem i et halsbrækkende tempo.
Digital technology is advancing at a breakneck pace.
digitale adjective common
DIYITALE · di-gi-ta-le
digital
I den digitale tidsalder er håndskrift langsomt ved at blive en glemt kunstart.
In the digital age, handwriting is slowly becoming a lost art.

your, thy (singular; one owner)

direkte adjective common
/dirɛktə/ · DIREKTE · di-rek-te
direct
also: immediate; straightforward, no-nonsense; outright, downright
De gik direkte i seng.
They went straight to bed.
Det er en direkte ordre.
That's a direct order.
direktør en noun core
/di.ɐ̯əɡˈtøˀɐ̯/ · DIREKTURR · di-rek-tør
director

DISKUSSION · dis-kus-sion
an argument, a discussion
Tom og jeg havde en lang diskussion om det.
Tom and I had a long discussion about that.

diskutere verb general
DISKUTERE · dis-ku-te-re
to discuss
Lad os ikke diskutere det.

Some people hate to argue.
diverse adjective general
DIVERSE · di-ver-se
various, sundry, miscellaneous, incidental.
also: Capable of various forms; multiform.
Diverse værktøj var stjålet.
Various tools were stolen.
weather
division en noun general
DIVISION · di-vi-sion
league
also: department, section, branch
Det må 1. division ikke.
The 1st Division must not.

DOG
however
also: though
Så sagtn dog farten!

Og dog bevæger den sig!

dokumentere verb rare

to document (something)
I alt seks dokumenterede tilfælde.
A total of six documented cases.
dollar en noun general
DOLLAR · dol-lar
a dollar monetary unit
Han havde 50 dollar.
He had fifty dollars.
40 dollar for syv dage.
40 dollars for seven days.
dom en noun common
[ˈd̥ʌmˀ] · DOM
sentence
also: conviction; judgement; verdict
Tom fik en seks måneders betinget dom.
Tom was given a six-month suspended sentence.
Jeg drømte en drøm i nat om silke og fornemt stof eller om lighed og retfærdig dom.
I had a dream last night, about silk and fine cloth or about equality and fair trial.
dommer en noun general
/ˈdɔmɐ/ · DOMMER · dom-mer
judge
also: magistrate; justice; referee
Dommeren er ikke god.
The referee is not good.
Dommeren dømte ham til et års fængsel.
"The judge sentenced him to one year's imprisonment."

drab et noun general
DRAB
homicide
Sami blev dømt for drab på en juveler.
Sami was convicted of killing a jeweler.
Disse meningsløse drab skal ikke gå ustraffede hen.
These senseless killings will not go unpunished.
dreje verb rare
/drajə/ · DRAYE · dre-je
to turn
also: to twist, twirl; to revolve, rotate, pivot; to dial
Jeg så hans bil dreje til højre.

dreng en noun common
/ˈdrɛŋˀ/ · DRENG
boy, lad
Er du en dreng?
Are you a boy?
Jeg er en dreng.
I am a boy.
drift en noun general
/dreft/ · DRIFT
operation, running
also: service; drive, urge, desire; drove

Tatoeba was out of service.

DEWGTEE · dyg-tig
skilled

/dyːˀɐ̯/ · DEWR

/dyrkə/ · DEWRKE · dyr-ke
to engage in

Marys læge rådede hende til at dyrke motion.
Mary's doctor advised her to exercise.

dække verb general
DEHKKE · dæk-ke
to cover
also: to veil; to set (the table)
Jeg vil dække bord.
I'll set the table.
Tom bad Mary dække ham ind.
Tom asked Mary to cover for him.
dække et noun general
[ˈd̥ɛɡə] · DEHKKE · dæk-ke
cover; covering; layer
also: tyre tread; tread (dæk is more common for tyre)

A cover of snow lay over the fields.
Borddækket var pænt arrangeret.
The table cover was neatly arranged.

[ˈd̥øˀ] · DUR
to die

Die!

døgn et noun common
/døjn/ · DURN
day - the 24 hour period stretching from midnight to midnight.
also: day - a measurement of time, equaling 24 hours.

A day has 86,400 seconds.
Jeg vil give dig et døgn til at tænke over det.

[ˈd̥œmə] · DURMME · døm-me
to convict
also: sentence; judge; form an opinion on, especially a negative one
At dømme efter hans accent, så er han fra Osaka.

/dœːr/ · DURR
door
Jeg dør.
I'm dying.
Ingen dør.
Nobody dies.
home

[ˈd̥ɒːli] · DAWRLEE · dår-lig
bad, unwell, poor

Bad hair day.

Do you feel sick?

power

effective, producing a large effect

It's 99.9% effective.

Efterforskningen er ikke engang kommet ét skridt videre.
"The investigation hasn't progressed even one step."

efterfølgende adjective common
EFTERFURLYENDE · ef-ter-føl-gen-de
afterwards, thereafter, at a later time
Den mistænkte gerningsmand døde efterfølgende af sine kvæstelser.
The suspected perpetrator later died from his injuries.

efterhånden adverb common
EFTERHAWNDEN · ef-ter-hån-den
pointing out a change that has happened gradually or in small intervals; over time
Jeg har efterhånden fået nok af socialretfærdighedskrigere.
I'm getting fed up with SJWs.
Jeg har efterhånden fået nok af krigere for social retfærdighed.
I'm getting fed up with SJWs.
efterlyse verb rare
EFTERLEWSE · ef-ter-ly-se
to search for, look for
also: to issue a warrant for someone's arrest, to issue an appeal for
Nu efterlyses kandidater.
Candidates are now being sought.

eftermiddag en noun common
EFTERMIDDAG · ef-ter-mid-dag
afternoon (part of the day between noon and evening)
God eftermiddag!
Good afternoon!
Vi ses i eftermiddag.
I'll see you this afternoon.
time culture
E
eftersom conjunction rare
EFTERSOM · ef-ter-som
because, since
Jeg spiste ikke brødet, eftersom der var mug på skorpen.
I didn't eat the bread because there was mold on the crust.
Det er nemt for min søster og mig at shoppe sammen, eftersom vi begge er vintertyper.
It's easy for my sister and me to go shopping together, seeing as how we're both "winters."
efterspørgsel en noun general
[ˈɛfdɐˌsbɶɐ̯səl] · EFTERSPURRGSEL · ef-ter-spørgsel
demand

There is a great demand for gasoline.
efterår et noun general
/ˈɛfdɐˌɒːˀ/ · EFTERAWR · ef-ter-år
autumn, fall
Det er efterår.
It is autumn.
Et køligt efterår er på vej.

/ˈɑjən/ · EYEN
own
also: private; peculiar; odd
Jeg er min egen chef.
I'm my own boss.
Det var Toms egen skyld.

to own

Menneskeforstand er det stærkeste våben man kan eje.

[ˈɑːjənˌd̥ʌmˀ] · AYENDOM · ejen-dom
property

owner

[ˈɛɡ̊sd̥ʁɑ] · EKSTRA · ek-stra
extra (beyond what is due, usual, expected, or necessary)
also: extra

ENDDA · end-da
even

ende en noun common
/ɛnə/ · ENDE · en-de

Hun vandt i sidste ende.

Vi er nået til vejs ende.
We reached the end of the road.
endelig adjective common
/ˈɛnəli/ · ENDELEE · en-de-lig
final, eventual
also: finite; finally, eventually; finally, at last
Endelig!
Finally!
Endelig kom bussen.
The bus finally arrived.
endnu adverb core
/eˈnu/ · ENDNU · end-nu
yet
Endnu en gang.

Is he still sleeping?
ene adjective common
ENE
alone
also: lonely; juniper
Det ene øje er udspilet.
One eye is dilated.
Det ene øje er dilateret.
One eye is dilated.

only, sole

enig adjective common
/eːni/ · ENEE
united

OK.

enighed en noun general
[ˈeːniˌheðˀ] · ENIGHETH · enig-hed
agreement, consensus

ENKEL · en-kel
single, singular
also: simple
Min metode er overraskende enkel, men effekten er stor.
My method is surprisingly simple, but the impact is big.
Min metode er forbløffende enkel, men meget virkningsfuld.
My method is surprisingly simple, but highly effective.
E
enkelt adjective common
ENKELT · en-kelt
single, individual, not immediately surrounded or followed by other similar specimens or cases
also: for use by no more than one person; one-way; special, particular, occurring once or only a few times and therefore particularly prominent
En enkelt til Boston, tak!
A one-way ticket to Boston, please.
Jeg har set ham en enkelt gang.
I have seen him once.
enorm adjective general
[eˈnɒˀm] · ENORM
enormous
also: enormously, extremely, very
Denne bil har en enorm motor.
This car has a huge engine.
Esperanto er en enorm udvidelse af min horisont.
Esperanto is a huge expansion of my horizon.
enten conjunction common
/ɛntən/ · ENTEN · en-ten
either
Han er enten fuld eller skør.
He is either drunk or mad.
Svar venligst enten »ja« eller »nej«.
Please answer yes or no.
erfaring en noun common
ERFARING · er-fa-ring
experience
Han mangler erfaring.
He lacks experience.
Han har stor erfaring.
He has ample experience.
Danish–English Dictionary  ·  Tatoeba (CC-BY)  ·  Leipzig (CC-BY) enetverbadjadvother DA → EN
ABCDERHFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN erhverv – euro 49
E
erhverv et noun general
ERVERW · er-hverv
profession
also: professional (relating to a profession)
Prince Edward Islands vigtigste erhverv er turisme, kartoffelavl og fiskeri.
Prince Edward Island's main industries are tourism, potato farming and fishing.
erhvervsliv et noun general
ERVERVSLIW · er-hvervs-liv
the business sector, industry
Især i erhvervslivet.
Especially in business.
transport
erkende verb rare
[aɐ̯ˈkʰɛnˀə] · ERKENDE · er-ken-de
to acknowledge, admit, concede
also: to recognize, realize, discover, understand
Tom erkender at han blev besejret.
Tom acknowledges that he was defeated.
erklære verb rare
ERKLEHRE · er-klæ-re
to declare
also: to proclaim; to state; to declare; to set aside a portion of memory for
Tom vil erklære national nødsituation.
Tom will declare a national emergency.
E
erstatning en noun general
[aɐ̯ˈsd̥ad̥neŋ] · ERSTATNING · er-stat-ning
substitute, replacement
also: compensation
De havde ikke rigtig kaffe, så de drak erstatningskaffe.
"They didn't have real coffee, so they drank ersatz."
etablere verb general
ETABLERE · etab-le-re
to establish
USA og Cuba ønsker at etablere diplomatiske forbindelser efter et halvt århundredes fjendskab.
The United States and Cuba wish to establish diplomatic relations after a half century of enmity.
etape et noun general
ETAPE · eta-pe
stage
Ikke kun etapen.
Not just the stage.
euro en noun general
/øvro/ · EURO · eu-ro
euro currency
Lån mig tyve euro.
Lend me twenty euros.
Mary gav ham ti euro.
Mary gave him ten euros.

ABCDEURFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN europæisk – falsk 50
E
europæisk adjective general
[œwʁoˈpʰɛˀisɡ̊] · EUROPEHISK · eu-ro-pæ-isk
European
Hvad får dig til at tro at Kaliningrad er en europæisk by?
What makes you think Kaliningrad is a European city?
I sit essay »Esperanto: et europæisk eller et asiatisk sprog« påviste Claude Piron lighederne mellem esperanto og kinesisk og aflivede dermed forestillingen om at esperanto kun var et europæisk orienteret sprog.
In his essay "Esperanto: European or Asiatic language" Claude Piron has shown the similarities between Esperanto and Chinese, thereby putting to rest the notion that Esperanto is purely eurocentric.
eventuel adjective general
EVENTUEL · even-tu-el
that might or might not exist or be relevant; any
Hertil kommer eventuelle bøder.
In addition, any fines.
evne en noun general
/ɛvnə/ · EVNE · ev-ne
ability
also: power; capacity; to be able to
Jeg ville ønske, jeg havde evnen til at gøre det.
I wish I had the ability to do that.
Det drejer sig ikke om hendes evner, men om hendes karakter.
The point at issue is not her ability but her character.
F
faglig adjective general
[ˈfɑwli] · FAGLEE · fag-lig
professional, academic, technical
Både fagligt og socialt.
Both professionally and socially.
F
faktisk adjective common
/faktisk/ · FAKTISK · fak-tisk
actual, real
also: factual; actually, as a matter of fact, in fact, really
De vandt faktisk.
They did win.
Min kat er faktisk sky.
The fact is that my cat is shy.
fald et noun common
/falˀ/ · FALD
fall
also: decrease, decline; case; event, case
Hovmod står for fald.
Arrogance precedes the fall.
I så fald har du ret.
In that case, you are right.
falde verb common
FALDE · fal-de
to fall
also: to drop, to descend
Prøv at falde til ro.
Try and calm down.
Priserne ser ud til at falde.
Prices seem to be going down.
falsk adjective rare
/falsk/ · FALSK
false
also: fake; counterfeit; forged, spurious
Hun synger falsk.
She sings out of tune.
Tom synger falsk.
Tom sings off key.

family

fange verb rare
/faŋə/ · FANYE · fan-ge
to catch, to capture

Vi skal fange løven levende.

fange en noun rare
/faŋə/ · FANYE · fan-ge
prisoner; captive; convict
also: detainee
Fangen flygtede fra fængslet om natten.

fans en noun general

fans; supporters
Det Europæiske Melodigrandprix har millioner af fans rundt om i verden.

/fanˈtasdisɡ/ · FANTASTISK · fan-ta-stisk
incredible, amazing, fantastic

Fantastic!
Det er fantastisk.

far en noun common
/fɑː/ · FAR
father, dad
Far er høj.
Dad is tall.
Godnat, far.

fare en noun common
/ˈfaːrə/ · FARE · fa-re
danger, hazard

Undgå fare.

FARLEE · far-lig
dangerous
also: perilous
Du er farlig.

fart en noun general
/farˀt/ · FART
speed
also: trip; journey; trade.
Han cyklede med jævn fart.
He rode his bicycle at a uniform speed.
Politibilen kørte med forholdsvis høj fart.
The police car was driving at fairly high speed.
farve en noun rare
FARVE · far-ve
color (US), colour (UK)
also: color, human skin tone; dye, colourant used for hair
Tom har farve-tv.
Tom has a color TV.
Dens farve er rød.
Its color is red.
farvel interjection general
FARVEL · far-vel
goodbye, farewell
Farvel.
Farewell!
Farvel!
Farewell!
fase en noun common
FASE · fa-se
phase
also: state at a particular time in a system that is in periodic oscillations; homogeneous component of a heterogeneous mixture; to phase (, / in, out)
Det er ikke en fase, mor.
It's not a phase, Mom.
Observationen af tyngdebølger vil starte en ny fase i studiet af universet.
The observation of gravitational waves will start a new phase in the study of the universe.
F
fast adjective common
/fast/ · FAST
firm
also: solid; tight; fixed
Tom sov fast.
Tom was fast asleep.
Har du fast kæreste?
Do you have a steady girlfriend?
fat et noun common
FAT
grip; hold; grasp (tage fat = to take hold; få fat i = to get hold of)
Få fat i en læge!
Call a doctor!
Lad os få fat i et juletræ.
Let's go get a Christmas tree.
februar en noun common
FEBRUAR · fe-bru-ar
february (the second month of the Gregorian calendar)
Februar har kun 28 dage.
In February there are only 28 days.
Februar er årets anden måned.
February is the second month of the year.
fedt et noun general
[ˈfed̥] · FEDT
fat
also: grease; lard
Svinekød indeholder meget fedt.
Pork has a lot of fat.
Det var fedt at møde dig i virkeligheden!
It was awesome to meet you in real life!

fejl adjective common
FAYL
wrong

Du tog fejl.
You were mistaken.

fejre verb general
/fajrə/ · FAYRE
to celebrate
Har vi noget at fejre?

Lad være at fejre det for tidligt.
Don't celebrate too early.
felt et noun rare
FELT
field (the practical part of something)
also: field; an outlying area, as opposed to e.g. the lab, office or barracks; field; sphere, province
Han kom op i det røde felt.

Han røg op i det røde felt.
He lost his temper.

fem adjective core
[ˈfɛmˀ] · FEM
five
Klokken er fem.

Jeg har fem katte.

femte adjective general
[ˈfɛmˀd̥ə] · FEMTE · fem-te
fifth

ferie en noun common
FERIE · fe-rie
holiday
also: vacation; recess
God ferie!
Enjoy your holidays.
Vi er på ferie.

fest en noun common
/ˈfɛsd/ · FEST

a festival

/fin/ · FIN
fine
also: choice, classy; delicate; fashionable

FINANSLOW · fi-nans-lov

"Hvad er finansloven?"

/fenə/ · FINDE · fin-de
to find
also: to think, consider; to exist - see
Vi må finde den.
We must find it.

[ˈfenəs] · FINDES · fin-des
to exist
Der findes guder.
Gods exist.
Rambo findes ikke.
Rambo doesn't exist.

/fiːrə/ · FIRE · fi-re
four
also: to lower something fixed to a rope or something similar
Klokken er fire.
It's four o'clock.
I har fire hunde.
You have four dogs.

FIRMA · fir-ma
a company, a firm
Det firma er gået fallit.
That company went bankrupt.

fisk en noun general

fjerde adjective common
[ˈfjɛːɐ] · FYERDE · fjer-de
fourth
Ugens fjerde dag er torsdag.
Thursday is the fourth day of the week.
Torsdag er den fjerde dag i ugen.
Thursday is the fourth day of the week.
fjern adjective rare
/fjɛrn/ · FYERN
distant
also: remote; faraway, far-off
Fjern venligst dette.
Please take this away.
I morgen kommer en fjern fætter.
Tomorrow a distant cousin is coming.
fjerne verb common
FYERNE · fjer-ne
to remove, take away
also: to retreat, remove oneself (from a certain setting)
Tom så Mary i det fjerne.
Tom saw Mary in the distance.
Vi så tårnet i det fjerne.
We saw the tower in the distance.
flag et noun general
FLAG
flag (cloth)
also: flag (true-false variable)
Dette er et flag.
This is a flag.
Her er Japans flag.
Here's Japan's flag.

flere adjective rare
[ˈfleːɐ]
more
also: several; multiple; others
Aalborg Academy of Science lyver op til flere gange.
flertal et noun common
/flertal/ · FLERTAL · fler-tal
majority
also: plural number
Flertal af hest er heste.
The plural of horse is horses.
»Heste« er flertal af »hest«.
"Horses" is the plural of "horse."
flest adjective common
FLEST
most in relation to number, used with countable nouns
Er der flest får i Wales eller i New Zealand?
Are there more sheep in Wales or in New Zealand?
flok en noun general
[ˈflʌɡ] · FLOK
flock
Ulve jager i flok.
Wolves hunt in a pack.
Tom så en flok springbukke i det fjerne.
Tom saw a herd of springbok in the distance.

great; fine; handsome; impressive

fly et noun general
[ˈflyˀ] · FLEW
airplane, aeroplane
also: to flee; to shun; to hand, give
Jeg så et fly.
I saw a plane.
Dette fly er hans.

/fløktnenɡ/ · FLEWGTNING · flygt-ning
refugee

That boat was full of refugees from Cuba.
Der er mange syriske flygtninge i Algeriet.
There are many Syrian refugees in Algeria.
flytte verb common
/fløtə/ · FLEWTTE · flyt-te
to move, remove
also: to move; move, move away
Lad os flytte sengen.
Let's move the bed.
Vi er nødt til at flytte.
We have to move.

flyve verb general
/flyːvə/ · FLEWVE · fly-ve
to fly
also: to rush, dash
Jeg kan flyve.
I can fly.
En fugl kan flyve.

fod en noun general
[ˈfoˀð] · FOD
foot
also: the base or lower part of something, e.g. a page or a mountain; foot, unit of measure, in Denmark 31.4 cm until 1907, equal to 12 (inches); foot, a rhythmical unit
Min fod klør.
My foot's itching.
fodbold en noun common
/foðbʌld/ · FODBOLD · fod-bold
football: the sport association football; soccer
also: a football: the ball with which association football is played; soccer ball
Ser Tom fodbold?

Han hader fodbold.
He hates soccer.

/ˈfoːɡus/ · FOKUS · fo-kus
focus, focal point

Hold fokus.

folk et noun core
FOLK
people, persons
also: one, people; a people, a nation (not necessarily politically or geographically united); crew
Folk er får.
People are sheep.
Folk er sjove.
People are funny.
folketingsmedlem et noun general
FOLKETINGSMEDLEM · fol-ke-tings-med-lem
a member of the Danish Parliament, Folketinget
Hun har været folketingsmedlem for SF.
She has been a member of parliament for SF.
politics
foran adverb common
[ˈfɒːˈanˀ] · FORAN · for-an
ahead, in front
also: in front of
Han gik foran mig.
He walked in front of me.
Tom sad foran Mary.
Tom sat in front of Mary.
body
forbedre verb general
FORBEDRE · for-bed-re
to improve, better
also: to improve oneself
Firmaet prøver at forbedre sit image.
The company is trying to improve its image.
Du er nødt til at forbedre din kysseteknik.
You need to improve your kissing technique.
F
forberede verb general
[ˈfɒːbeˌʁɛðˀə] · FORBERETHE · for-be-re-de
to prepare (make ready for a specific future purpose)
also: to prepare oneself
Vi må forberede os på det værste.
We need to prepare for the worst.
Jeg begyndte på at forberede mig.
I started to prepare myself.
forbi adverb common
FORBI · for-bi
finished
Sommeren er forbi.
Summer is gone.
forbindelse et noun core
FORBINDEL-SEH · for-bin-del-se
connection
also: contact person; a compound
Jeg har et sidste råd i forbindelse med håndtryk: Glem ikke at smile.
I have one last piece of advice related to handshakes: Don't forget to smile.
Hvor har du været? Jeg har prøvet at komme i forbindelse med dig hele dagen.
Where were you? I've been trying to reach you all day.
forbrug et noun general
FORBRUG · for-brug
consumption; expenditure; use
Japan har et stort forbrug af papir.
Japan consumes a lot of paper.

forbud et noun common
[ˈfɒːˌb̥uð] · FORBUD · for-bud
ban
also: prohibition; harbinger
Rygning forbudt.
No smoking.

Strictly forbidden.
forbudt adjective general
[fɒˈb̥ud̥] · FORBUDT · for-budt
forbidden, prohibited
Rygning forbudt.
No smoking.
Fodring forbudt!
Don't feed the animals.
forbund et noun rare
FORBUND · for-bund
association, union, society
Forbundsregeringen reddede bankerne.
The federal government bailed out the banks.
Tom er meddeler for forbundspolitiet.
Tom is an informant for the FBI.
fordel en noun common
/fordeːl/ · FORDEL · for-del
advantage, merit
also: front part, anterior part
Det er en fordel at kunne bruge en computer.
It is an advantage to be able to use a computer.
Hun forlod ham til fordel for en yngre mand.
She dumped him for a younger man.
F
fordele verb general
/fordeːlə/ · FORDELE · for-de-le
to distribute
also: divide, share out; to spread
Hvordan ville du fordele pengene?
How would you apportion the funds?
Hvordan ville du fordele midlerne?
How would you apportion the funds?
fordi conjunction core
/fɔˈdiːˀ/ · FORDI · for-di
because
Fordi de ser fjernsyn.
Because they're watching television.
Tak fordi du er min ven.
Thank you for being my friend.
forebygge verb general
FOREBEWGYE · fore-byg-ge
to prevent, avoid
Hvilke fremskridt har der været med hensyn til at forebygge og behandle miltbrand-infektion?
What advances have there been in preventing and treating anthrax infection?
foredrag et noun general
FOREDRAG · fored-rag
lecture; talk; presentation
Nogle få klappede efter hans foredrag.
A few people clapped after his lecture.

F
foregå verb general
FOREGAW · fo-re-gå
to happen, to transpire
also: to influence or train others; to precede
"Hvad foregår der?"
"What's going on?"
Se, hvad der foregår.
"See what's going on."
technology
foreløbig adjective common
FORELURBEE · fo-re-lø-big
so far, preliminary
also: so far
Foreløbig uden held.
Preliminary unsuccessful.
forening en noun general
FORENING · for-e-ning
union; association; society
Dansk Psykolog Forening »
Danish Psychological Association »
forenkle verb rare
FORENKLE · for-enk-le
to simplify
Med fare for at forenkle, vil jeg nævne fire råd til nutidens topchefer.
At the risk of simplifying, I would like to mention four pieces of advice for today's top executives.
F
foreslå verb rare
FORESLAW · fo-re-slå
to suggest (ask for without demanding)
also: propose (to suggest a plan or course of action); move (to propose formally for consideration and determination, in a deliberative assembly)
"Hvad foreslår du?"
"What do you suggest?"
Jeg foreslår at du får en snak med Tom.
I suggest you have a talk with Tom.
forestille verb general
FORESTILLE · fo-re-stil-le
to depict, represent
also: to play a specific character or role (as in a play); to give the appearance of; to introduce (people) to each other, present people (to one another)
Det kunne jeg forestille mig.
I could imagine that.
Kan du forestille dig at være frugtar?
Can you imagine being a fruitarian?
foretage verb general
FORETAYE · fo-re-ta-ge
to undertake, perform
Hvor kan vi foretage et telefonopkald?
Where can we make a phone call?
Hvor kan man foretage et telefonopkald?
Where can we make a phone call?
bureaucracy
foretrække verb rare
/ˈfɔrəˌtrɛkə/ · FORETREHKKE · fo-re-træk-ke
to prefer
Jeg ville foretrække en kat frem for en hund.
I would rather have a cat than a dog.

F
forfatter en noun general
/fɔˈfatɐ/ · FORFATTER · for-fat-ter
writer
also: author
Han er forfatter.
He's an author.
forgæves adjective general
/fɔrɡɛːvəs/ · FORGEHVES · for-gæ-ves
vain, futile
also: vainly, in vain
I døde ikke forgæves.
You didn't die in vain.
Du døde ikke forgæves.
You didn't die in vain.
forhandle verb general
FORHANDLE · for-hand-le
to negotiate
also: to trade (with); to sell
Jeg er ikke bemyndiget til at forhandle.
I am not authorized to negotiate.
forhandling en noun rare
FORHANDLING · for-hand-ling
a negotiation
also: the negotiation of a product
Forhandlingerne er i fuld gang.
The negotiations are in full swing.
Forhandlingerne er i fuld sving.
The negotiations are in full swing.
F
forhindre verb common
FORHINDRE · for-hin-dre
to prevent
Berlinmuren blev opført for at forhindre hjerneflugt.
The Berlin Wall was built to prevent brain drain.
Bomuldsvanter vil forhindre babyen i at kradse sig i ansigtet.
Cotton mittens will prevent the baby from scratching his own face.
forhold et noun core
FORHOLD · for-hold
relation
also: relationship; proportion; condition
Dette forhold beviser hans uskyld.
This fact proves his innocence.
Min far billiger ikke vores forhold.
My father does not approve of our relationship.
forholde verb general
FORHOLDE · for-hol-de
to forhold, withhold (improperly), keep from (not tell someone)
also: to behave, conduct oneself; to relate to
Canada vil styrke forholdet til pålidelige allierede.
Canada wants to strengthen its relationships with reliable allies.
Canada vil styrke forholdet til troværdige allierede.
Canada wants to strengthen its relationships with trustworthy allies.
forhåbentlig adverb general
[fʌˈhɔˀb̥ənd̥li] · FORHAWBENTLEE · for-hå-bent-lig
hopefully
Forhåbentlig!
Hopefully!
Du er forhåbentlig klar over at ingen er over loven.
I hope that you're aware nobody is above the law.

Ordbog DA→EN forhånd – forlænge 61
F
forhånd en noun general
FORHAWND · for-hånd
in advance; beforehand; forehand (tennis)
Gør dig klar på forhånd.
Get ready in advance.
Gør jer klar på forhånd.
Get ready in advance.
forkert adjective common
/fɔˈkeːˀrd/ · FORKERT · for-kert
wrong
also: wrongly, wrong, erroneously
Forkert!
Wrong!
Forkert.
Wrong.
forklar verb rare
FORKLAR · for-klar
to imp
Forklar mig, hvad det betyder.
Explain to me what this means.
forklare verb general
FORKLARE · for-kla-re
to explain
Må jeg forklare?
May I explain?
Lad mig forklare.
Let me explain.
F
forklaring en noun common
FORKLARING · for-kla-ring
explanation
Tak for din forklaring.
Thanks for your explanation.
Køber du den forklaring?
Do you buy that explanation?
forlade verb common
FORLATHE · for-la-de
to leave
also: to give up, quit, end an employment; to forgive
Jeg vil forlade landet.
I'm going to leave the country.
Du må ikke forlade mig!
You mustn't leave me!
forlæng verb rare
FORLEHNG · for-læng
to imp
"Bliver det forlænget?"
“Will it be extended?”
forlænge verb general
FORLEHNYE · for-læn-ge
to prolong, lengthen
also: to multiply the denominator and enumerator of a fraction with the same number
"Bliver det forlænget?"
“Will it be extended?”

forløb et noun general
FORLURB · for-løb
course, progress, process
also: a route
Læs om forløbet her.
Read about the process here.
education
form en noun common
/fɔrm/ · FORM
shape
also: mould; tin
Du er i dårlig form.
You're in bad shape.
Han er i ret god form!
He's quite fit!
formand en noun core
[ˈfɒːˌmanˀ] · FORMAND · for-mand
chairman, head
Han er formand for PLO.
He is the chairman of the PLO.
body
F
formentlig adverb common
FORMENTLEE · for-ment-lig
presumably
Babyen har formentlig slugt sutten.
The baby has presumably swallowed the pacifier.
Spædbarnet har formentlig slugt sutten.
The baby has presumably swallowed the pacifier.
formiddag en noun common
/ˈfɒːmeˌdaːˀɣ/ · FORMIDDAG · for-mid-dag
forenoon, morning (about 9 am to 12 noon)
Klokken er ti om formiddagen.
"It's 10 a.m."
Posten kommer om formiddagen.
The mail arrives before noon.
time
formål et noun common
FORMAWL · for-mål
purpose, cause (for doing something)
Hvad er deres formål?
What's their purpose?
Hvad var deres formål?
What was their goal?
fornuftig adjective rare
FORNUFTEE · for-nuf-tig
reasonable, sensible
Jeg er fornuftig.
I'm reasonable.
Hr. White er en fornuftig mand.
Mr White is a man of reason.

forpligtelse et noun rare
FORPLIGTEL-SEH · for-plig-tel-se
an obligation
also: a commitment
Så jeg synes, vi har en forpligtelse.
So I think we have a commitment.
forretning en noun general
FORRETNING · for-ret-ning
business
also: firm; enterprise; shop; store; trade
Hans forretning vokser hurtigt.
His business is growing rapidly.
Lad os komme til sagen og tale forretning.
Let's get down to brass tacks and talk business.

forrige adjective general
FORRIYE · for-ri-ge
previous
also: last; foregoing
Forrige nat skrev jeg et brev.
I wrote a letter last night.
Jeg købte en ny computer i forrige måned.
I bought a new computer last month.
forringe verb rare
FORRINYE · for-rin-ge
to make worse; to deteriorate, diminish, degrade
FOA frygter, at kvaliteten forringes.
FOA fears that the quality will deteriorate.

forsikring en noun rare
FORSIKRING · for-sik-ring
insurance
also: assurance
Er det dækket af min forsikring?
Is that covered by my insurance?
forskel en noun common
/fɒːˌsɡɛlˀ/ · FORSKEL · for-skel
difference (separating one thing from another)
Er der en forskel?
Is there a difference?
Men der er en lille forskel.
But there is a small difference.
forskellig adjective rare
FORSKELLEE · for-skel-lig
different
Min mening er forskellig fra Toms.
My opinion is different from Tom's.
Vi er af forskellig alder. Han er ældre end mig.
We are different ages. He is older than me.
forsker en noun general
/fɔrskər/ · FORSKER · for-sker
researcher, scientist occupied with research
Han er en stor forsker.
He's a great scientist.
nature

FORSNING · forsk-ning
research

nature

FORSLAG · for-slag
proposal, suggestion
Nogen forslag?
Any suggestions?
Jeg har et forslag.
I've got a suggestion.
forstyrre verb rare
FORSTEWRRE · for-styr-re
to disturb, upset, disrupt
Jeg ønsker ikke at forstyrre jer, mens I arbejder.
I don't want to bother you guys while you're working.
forstå verb common
[fʌˈsd̥ɔˀ] · FORSTAW · for-stå
to understand
Tom ville forstå.
Tom would understand.
Jeg kan forstå det.
I can understand it.
F
forståelse et noun general
FORSTAWEL-SEH · for-stå-el-se
understanding, comprehension
Tak for din forståelse.
Thank you for your understanding.
forsvarlig adjective rare
FORSVARLEE · for-svar-lig
justifiable, defensible
also: proper, decent, sizable
"Hvad er forsvarligt?"
“What is justifiable?”
forsvinde verb rare
[fʌˈsvenˀə] · FORSVINDE · for-svin-de
to vanish, to disappear
Tom så Mary forsvinde ind i mørket.
Tom watched Mary disappear into the darkness.
Lad os forsvinde herfra før Tom ser os.
Let's get out of here before Tom sees us.

forsøg et noun common
FORSURG · for-søg
an attempt, try, effort
also: an experiment
Ja, forsøg igen.
Yes, try again.
Forsøg en gang til.

forsøge verb common
/fɔrsøːɡə/ · FORSURYE · for-sø-ge
to try
also: to attempt
Lad os forsøge!
Let's try!
Du skal forsøge at holde dig vågen.
You have to try to stay awake.
fortid en noun general
FORTID · for-tid
the past (period of time before the present time)
Jeg ved intet om hans fortid.
I don't know anything about his past.
Vi kan ikke ignorere Toms fortid.
We can't ignore Tom's past.
time
fortryde verb rare
/fɔrˈtryːˀðə/ · FORTREWDE · for-try-de
to regret
Det kommer du til at fortryde!
You'll regret this.
Tom kommer til at fortryde det.
Tom is going to regret that.
fortsat adverb core
[ˈfɒːd̥ˌsad̥] · FORTSAT · fort-sat
continuing, continuous
also: constantly
Den mistænkte er fortsat på fri fod.
The suspect remains at large.
Den mistænkte går fortsat frit omkring.
The suspect remains at large.
F
fortsætte verb common
[ˈfɒːd̥ˌsɛd̥ə] · FORTSEHTTE · fort-sæt-te
to continue
Vi kan ikke fortsætte uden Tom.
We can't continue without Tom.
Det kan ikke fortsætte sådan her.
It cannot continue like this.
fortælle verb common
/fɔˈtɛlˀə/ · FORTEHLLE · for-tæl-le
to tell, to report
also: to narrate
Jeg vil fortælle sandheden.
I'm going to tell the truth.
Lad mig fortælle dig fakta.
Let me tell you the facts.
fortælling en noun general
FORTEHLLING · for-tæl-ling
a story or tale
also: telling, narration
Er hans fortælling sand?
Is his story true?
Din fortælling er kedelig.
Your story is boring.
forud adverb general
[ˈfɒˌuðˀ] · FORUD · for-ud
ahead of, before, prior to, preceding
also: to be ahead of time, be diligent, industrious; the direction one is looking or walking towards
Jeg betalte forud.
I paid in advance.
Jeg har betalt forud.
I paid in advance.
body

forudsætning en noun rare
FORUDSEHTNING · for-ud-sæt-ning
assumption, premise

Det giver blot nogle andre forudsætninger.
It just provides some other prerequisites.
forvent verb rare
FORVENT · for-vent
to imp
Forvent det uventede.
Expect the unexpected.
Forvent ikke at andre tænker for dig!
Don't expect others to think for you!
forvente verb general
/fʌvɛnˀtə/ · FORVENTE · for-ven-te
to expect
also: to demand
Du kan ikke forvente at jeg altid tænker på alting.
You can't expect me to always think of everything!
Nu ved jeg hvad jeg kan forvente, og kan handle derefter.
Now I know what's in store for me and can act accordingly.
forventning en noun general
FORVENTNING · for-vent-ning
expectation
also: the state of being expectant
Mary har urealistiske forventninger.
Mary has unrealistic expectations.
Hun levede op til vores forventninger.
She lived up to our expectations.
F
forælder en noun rare
/fɔˈɛlˀdɐ/ · FOREHLDER · for-æl-der
parent
"\"Hvad bekymrer dig som forælder?"

family
forældre en noun rare
parents
Af den grund bør unge og deres forældre læse den.
forår et noun general
/ˈfɒːˌɒːˀ/ · FORAWR · for-år
spring
Hvor er forår?
Where is spring?
Livets forår er kort.
The springtime of life is short.

[ˈfʁɑnˀsɡ̊] · FRANSK
French
also: French (language)
Lær fransk.
Learn French.

fraråde verb rare
FRARAWDE · fra-rå-de
to discourage, dissuade, advise against
Det frarådes at bruge denne funktion.
This feature is deprecated. We advise you not to use it.
Mine forældre frarådede mig at rejse alene.
My parents discouraged me from traveling alone.
fravælge verb rare
FRAVEHLYE · fra-væl-ge
to opt-out, deselect
Det får to klubber til at fravælge europæisk deltagelse.
This causes two clubs to opt out of European participation.
fred en noun general
/freð/ · FRED
peace
Giv os fred!
Give us peace!
Vi ønsker fred.
We want peace.
fredag en noun core
/ˈfreːˀda/ · FRETHAG · fre-dag
Friday
Glædelig fredag!
Happy Friday!
Er det fredag i dag?
Is today Friday?
time
F
frem adverb core
/frɛm/ · FREM
forward, toward
Pligt frem for alt.
Duty above all.
Jeg ser frem til det.
I look forward to it.
fremgang en noun general
FREMGANG · frem-gang
progress
Det er en rutinemæssig fremgangsmåde.
"It's routine procedure."
fremgå verb rare
FREMGAW · frem-gå
to appear (from), be indicated (by)
Cookies kan indeholde nødder eller jordnødder, selv om det ikke fremgår af ingredienslisten.
Cookies may contain nuts or peanuts, even if not included in the ingredient list.
technology
fremhæve verb rare
FREMHEHVE · frem-hæ-ve
to accentuate, to stress
also: to emphasize, to highlight, to accentuate, to stress
Den stribede kjole fremhæver hendes slanke figur.
Her striped dress accentuates her slimness.

F
fremlægge verb rare
FREMLEHGYE · frem-læg-ge
to present, exhibit (a proposal, result etc.)
Selve lovforslaget fremlægges tirsdag.
The bill itself will be presented on Tuesday.
fremme verb general
FREMME · frem-me
to promote, further
also: invigoration (to cause something to prosper); at the front of something or in a place that is in front of you; in use, accessible, or visible; (not hidden or tucked away)
Vi har lige fået informationer om at fjenden ligger i baghold på vejen to mil længere fremme.
We've just received intelligence that the enemy is waiting in ambush two miles down the road.
fremover adverb common
FREMOVER · frem-over
from now on; going forward; henceforth
Vær mere forsigtig med dine penge fremover!
From now on, be more careful with your money.
Kardinal Robert Francis Prevost, af amerikansk og peruviansk nationalitet, er netop blevet valgt til pave og vil fremover hedde Leo XIV (2025-05-08).
Cardinal Robert Francis Prevost, of American and Peruvian nationality, has just been elected Pope and will adopt the name Leo XIV (08.05.2025).
fremstille verb general
FREMSTILLET · frem-stil-let
to produce; to manufacture; to make
Smørret er fremstillet af komælk.
The butter is made from cows' milk.
John har en bil der er fremstillet i Japan.
John has a car made in Japan.
F
fremtid en noun common
/ˈfʁamˌtiðˀ/ · FREMTID · frem-tid
future
also: future tense
Tænk på din fremtid.
Think about your future.
Skål for din fremtid!
Here's to your future.
fremtidig adjective rare
FREMTITHEE · frem-ti-dig
future
Præsentér mig for din fremtidige kone.
Introduce me to your future wife.
Folk har en tendens til at undervurdere deres fremtidige behov.
People have a tendency to underestimate their future needs.
fri verb common
/friː/ · FRI
to propose
also: to free; vacant, unoccupied; to available
Jeg er fri.
I'm free.
Arbejde gør fri.
Work makes free.
frihed en noun general
/ˈfriːhed/ · FRIHETH · fri-hed
freedom
also: liberty; exemption; leisure
Folket elsker frihed.
The people love freedom.
Overvågning er frihed.
Surveillance is liberty.

fri

/frivilˀi/ · FRIVILLEE · fri-vil-lig

/frøkt/ · FREWGT
fear, fright

Denne frygt er forståelig.

frygte verb rare
[ˈfʁ̥œɡ̊d̥ə] · FREWGTE · fryg-te
to fear, to be afraid (of)
also: to dread
Der er intet at frygte.
There is nothing to fear.
Jeg har intet at frygte.
I've nothing to fear.
emotion

/fuːˀl/ · FUGL

fuld

[ˈfɔnˀ] · FUND
find
also: bargain; discovery
Den gamle bog der er et sandt fund.
That old book is a real find.
fungere verb general
FUNYERE · fun-ge-re
to function
Intet syntes at fungere.
Nothing seemed to work.
Vores plan kommer til at fungere fint.

FEWLDE · fyl-de
volume
also: whole, abundant, complete; wealth, abundance

De er nogle hårde fyre.

/fyːˀsisk/ · FEWSISK · fy-sisk
physical
also: physically

Sami abused Layla physically.
Der er intet fysisk galt med ham.
There's nothing physically wrong with him.
fælles adjective common
[ˈfɛlˀəs] · FEHLLES · fæl-les
common, communal
also: shared, jointly owned
Vi har et fælles mål.
We have a common goal.
I to har en masse til fælles.
You two have a lot in common.
fællesskab et noun common
[ˈfɛlˀə(s)ˌsɡ̊æˀb̥] · FEHLLESSKAB · fæl-les-skab
a community
also: a solidarity
Vores fællesskab.

fængsel et noun common
/fɛŋˀsəl/ · FEHNGSEL
prison
Han er i fængsel.
He's in prison.
Tom er i fængsel.
Tom is in prison.
family
F
færdes verb general
[ˈfaɐ̯d̥əs] · FEHRDES · fær-des
to go around, travel
"Mennesker færdes ikke her.\""
"People don't move around here.\""
