færdig adjective common
FEHRDEE · fær-dig
finished, completed
also: skillfulness, tendency, habit (added as a prefix or suffix to nouns, verbs and adjectives).; I will debate this no further
Er du færdig?
Have you finished?
Du er færdig.
Your number is up.
færge en noun rare
[ˈfaɐ̯ˀw] · FEHRG · færg
ferry
Den færge forliste ud for Chiles kyst.
That boat wrecked off the coast of Chile.
føde en noun rare
/føːðə/ · FURDE · fø-de
food
also: nourishment; diet; to bear, give birth
De spiser sunde fødevarer.
They eat healthy foods.
"Er dit fødeland Kasakhstan?"

F
fødselsdag en noun general
[ˈføsl̩sˌd̥æˀ] · FURDSELSDAG · fød-sels-dag
birthday
Det er Toms fødselsdag.
It's Tom's birthday.
De glemte min fødselsdag.
They forgot my birthday.
time
føle verb general
[ˈføːlə] · FURLE · fø-le
to feel, sense
also: to feel
Jeg vil føle mig ensom når du er gået.
I'll be lonely after you've gone.
Tom kan få folk til at føle sig værdsat.
Tom can make people feel appreciated.
emotion
følelse et noun general
[ˈføːləlsə] · FURLEL-SEH · fø-lel-se
feeling
Jul er ikke en årstid; det er en følelse.
Christmas isn't a season, it's a feeling.
Han kunne vise sin følelse med musik i stedet for med ord.
He could show his feeling with music instead of words.
emotion
følge verb core
[ˈføljə] · FURLYE · føl-ge
to follow
also: to be a logical consequence of
Lad os følge Tom.
Let's follow Tom.
Må jeg følge dig hjem?
Can I walk you home?
F
følge en noun core
[ˈføljə] · FURLYE · føl-ge
consequence; result; effect
also: retinue; entourage; succession; sequence
Det kan have alvorlige følger for din sundhed.
It can have serious consequences for your health.
education
før adjective core
[ˈfœ̝ɐ̯ˀ] · FURR
before
also: before, until; before, prior, sooner; stout, portly
Før dagbog!
Keep a diary.
Før en dagbog.
Keep a diary.
føre et noun common
/føːrə/ · FURRE · fø-re
state of the roads
also: to lead, guide; to wage; to lead
Jeg kan føre dig til Tom.
I can take you to Tom.
Han kan ikke føre en samtale.
He can't hold a conversation.
transport
førende adjective general
FURRENDE · før-en-de
leading; foremost; prominent
Mary er verdens førende egernekspert.
Mary is the world’s leading expert on squirrels.
Tyskland er førende i verden indenfor denne industri.
Germany is the global leader in this industry.

først adverb core
/fœrst/ · FURRST
first, firstly
also: at first; not until, not till; only, as late as
Herrer først.
Gentlemen first.
Damerne først!
Ladies first.
første adjective core
/fœrstə/ · FURRSTE · før-ste
first
also: the former
Vi er de første.
We're the first.
Det er ikke første gang.
It's not the first time.
få et noun core
[ˈfɔˀ] · FAW
few
also: to get; to have; at random; haphazardly
Må jeg få det?
Can I have it?
Må jeg få den?
Can I have it?

gade et noun general
[ˈɡ̊æːðə] · GATHE · ga-de
street (a paved part of road, usually in a village or a town)
Denne gade er mørk.
This street is dark.
Hvad hedder denne gade?
What's this street called?
health nature transport
G
gal et noun rare
/ɡaːˀl/ · GAL
crazy, mad, insane
also: mad, angry; wrong; bad
Er du gal?
Are you mad?
gammel adjective common
/ɡaməl/ · GAMMEL · gam-mel
old
Du er gammel.
You are old.
Hun er gammel.
She is old.
gang en noun core
/ɡ̊anɡ̊/ · GANG
the act of walking, a walk
also: a time (an occurrence, an incidence); a way or path to walk on, either outdoors or indoors (a corridor)
Kom i gang!
Get started.
Endnu en gang.
Once again.
ganske adjective common
/ˈkænskə/ · GANSKE · gan-ske
whole, complete
also: quite (wholly, completely); quite (to a great extent or degree); quite (to a moderate extent or degree)
Du er ganske klog.
You're quite smart.
Mary er ganske attraktiv.
Mary is quite attractive.

garanti en noun general
/ɡɑrɑnˈtiˀ/ · GARANTI · ga-ran-ti
a guarantee (anything that assures a certain outcome)
also: a warranty
På dette tv-apparat er der to års garanti.
This TV set has a two year guarantee.
gave et noun general
[ˈɡ̊æːʋə] · GAVE · ga-ve
gift, present
also: gift (a talent or natural ability)
Tak for din gave.
Thank you for your present.
Han gav mig en gave.
He gave me a present.
gavn en noun general
[ˈɡ̊ɑwˀn] · GAVN
gain, benefit
Nogle mennesker insisterer på, at tv gør mere skade end gavn.
Some people insist that television does more harm than good.
generation en noun general
YENERATION · ge-ne-ra-tion
generation organisms or devices born or designed at the same time
Velkommen til den næste generation af autonome køretøjer.
Welcome to the next generation of autonomous vehicles.
G
generel adjective rare
/ɡenəˈrɛlˀ/ · YENEREL · ge-ne-rel
general
Øgede CO2-niveauer bidrager væsentligt til generel mangel på næringsstoffer i vores afgrøder.
Increased CO₂ levels are significantly contributing to a general lack of nutrients in our crops.
Øgede CO₂-niveauer bidrager væsentligt til generel mangel på næringsstoffer i vores afgrøder.
Increased CO₂ levels are significantly contributing to a general lack of nutrients in our crops.
generelle adjective general
YENERELLE · ge-ne-rel-le
generel
Så de generelle retningslinjer gælder.
So the general guidelines apply.
gengæld en noun common
/ɡɛn.ɡɛlˀ/ · YENGEHLD · gen-gæld
return, reciprocation
also: retaliation
Denne tabletcomputer har en lav vægt, men til gengæld en høj pris.
This tablet is low weight but high price.
gennem preposition core
/(i)ɡɛnˀəm/ · YENNEM · gen-nem
through
also: through, during; by means of, through, by
Vi gik gennem skoven.
We walked through the forest.
Tom løb gennem skoven.
Tom ran through the woods.

[ˈɡɛnəmˌføˀʌ] · YENNEMFURRE · gen-nem-fø-re
to process, to fulfill, to put into effect
Hun vil gennemføre sine planer uanset omkostningerne.
She will carry out her plans, regardless of the cost.
health
gennemsnit et noun general
YENNEMSNIT · gen-nem-snit
average, mean, arithmetic mean
Jeg sover i gennemsnit seks timer om dagen.
I sleep six hours a day on average.
I gennemsnit går jeg i biografen en gang om ugen.
On the average, I go to the movies once a week.
gerne adverb core
/ˈɡɛɐ̯nə/ · YERNE · ger-ne
with pleasure; gladly, willingly, readily
Jeg kommer gerne!
I'll be happy to come!
Jeg vil gerne komme.
I want to come.

gerningsmand en noun general
YERNINGSMAHN · ger-nings-mand
perpetrator; offender; culprit
En tyverialarm gik i gang, da gerningsmanden smadrede et vindue.

gevinst en noun general
/ɡevenst/ · YEVINST · ge-vinst
prize
also: winnings; profit; advantage
Det er en stor gevinst.

gift en noun general
/ɡift/ · YIFT
poison (substance harmful to a living organism)

Er Tom gift?

body family

[ˈɡ̊iˀ] · YIVE · gi-ve
to give

GLOBAL · glo-bal
global; worldwide; international
Som I allesammen ved, er vi midt i en global pandemi.

glæde en noun common
/ɡlɛːdə/ · GLEHDE · glæ-de

She was crying with joy.

[ˈɡ̊oˀð] · GOD
good
God nat.
Good night.

technology
godt adjective core
/ɡɔt/ · GODT
well
also: pretty; a little more than; a little less than
Godt!
Good!
Sov godt!
Sleep well!
grad en noun common
[ˈɡ̊ʁɑˀð] · GRAD
degree
also: degree (180th of pi); academic degree
Sami var i den grad kugleskør.
Sami was definitely fucked up in the head.
Du kan stole på ham til en vis grad, men ikke helt.

gram et noun general
/ɡram/ · GRAM
irate

Cook 300 grams of rice.
Afvej 15 gram kaffebønner.
Weigh out 15 grams of coffee beans.

ABCDEFGRAHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZÆØÅ

G
gratis adjective common
GRATIS · gra-tis
gratis, free (obtainable without payment)
Er den gratis?
Is it free?
Er det gratis?
Is it free?
grov adjective general
/ɡrɔv/ · GROW
coarse
also: rough; crude; rude
I en grov, men effektiv misinformationsoperation er det lykkedes angriberen at narre verden til at tro at forsvarerne bevidst havde nedskudt et fly med krigsfanger om bord.
In a crude but effective disinformation operation, the aggressor managed to trick the world into believing that the defenders had deliberately shot down an airplane with prisoners of war on board.
grund en noun core
[ɡ̊ʁɔnˀ] · GRUND
shallow
also: reason (a cause); motive (incentive to act; a reason); site, plot
Skibet gik på grund.
The ship ran aground.
Alt sker af en grund.
Everything happens for a reason.
grunde verb general
GRUNDE · grun-de
to prime
also: to ground; to found; to base
Tom må have haft sine grunde.
Tom must've had his reasons.
Jeg kunne give tusind grunde til at vi skulle gøre det.
I could give one thousand reasons why we should do this.
G
grundig adjective general
GRUNDIGT · grun-digt
thorough; careful; methodical
Jeg lover at læse rapporten grundigt.
I promise to read the report carefully.
grundlag et noun general
/ɡrɔnlaɡ/ · GRUNDLAG · grund-lag
basis
also: foundation; grounding
Romulus grundlagde Rom.
Romulus founded Rome.
Han grundlagde virksomheden.
He founded the company.
grundlæggende adjective general
GRUNDLEHGYENDE · grund-læg-gen-de
basic, fundamental
also: seminal; basically
Meter, kilogram, sekund, ampere, kelvin, mol og candela er det metriske systems grundlæggende enheder.
Meter, kilogram, second, ampere, kelvin, mole, and candela are the fundamental units of the metric system.
gruppe et noun common
[ˈɡ̊ʁub̥ə] · GRUPPE · grup-pe
A group, a unit, a team.
also: A community.; A side of a debate or discussion.; A caucus.
Vi har en god gruppe af frivillige.
We have a good group of volunteers.
Tom spillede basketball med en gruppe venner.
Tom was playing basketball with a group of friends.

G
grænse en noun general
[ˈɡ̊ʁansə] · GREHNSE · græn-se
border (the line or frontier area separating regions)
also: boundary; limit; to border
Hvor er den canadiske grænse?
Where's the Canadian border?
Yanni kørte længere østpå til den algeriske grænse.
Yanni drove further east to the Algerian border.
grøn adjective common
/ɡrœnˀ/ · GRURN
green
Dugen er grøn.
The tablecloth is green.
Kjolen er grøn.
The dress is green.
gul adjective rare
/ɡuːˀl/ · GUL
yellow
Solen er som stjerne en såkaldt gul dværg.
As a star, the sun is a so-called yellow dwarf.
Denne blomst er gul, men alle de andre er blå.
This flower is yellow, but all the others are blue.
gulv et noun rare
/ɡɔl/ · GULW
floor
Enhver væg i hans lejlighed er dækket af bøger fra gulv til loft.
Every wall of his apartment is covered from floor to ceiling with books.
home

gymnasium et noun common
[gymˈnæˀsɔm] · GEWMNASIUM · gym-na-si-um
upper secondary school; sixth-form college
also: gymnasium (3-year pre-university programme ending with studentereksamen)
Han gik på et gymnasium i København.
He attended a gymnasium (upper secondary school) in Copenhagen.
gæld en noun general
/ɡɛlˀ/ · GEHLD
debt, debts
Han kom i gæld.
He ran into debt.
Jeg står i gæld til dig.
I'm indebted to you.
gælde verb general
/ɡɛlə/ · GEHLDE · gæl-de
to hold good, be valid
also: to be effective, be in force; to be good for, be regarded as, pass for; to apply to, concern
Reglerne gælder for alle.
The rules apply to everyone.
Denne regel gælder også for dig.
This rule applies to you, too.
gæst en noun general
/ɡɛst/ · GEHST
visitor, guest
Tom er min gæst.
Tom is my guest.
Han er en uønsket gæst.
He's an unwanted guest.

/hals/ · HALS

[ˈhalˀ] · HALW
half

It's half past eight.

Croatia had a good first half.

HANDEL · han-del
commerce, trading
also: trade
Handel hjælper lande med at udvikle sig.
Trade helps nations develop.

/hanlə/ · HANDLE · hand-le
to act (to do something)
also: trade, shop
Vi må handle hurtigt.
We must act quickly.
handling en noun general
HANDLING · hand-ling
action, act
also: the plot, storyline (of a movie etc.)
Vi kræver handling!
We demand action.

/hasj/ · HASH
, hashish Not used anymore to denote dried herbs.

/haw/ · HAW
sea, ocean

[ˈhæːðə]
to past

/ˈhaːvə/

H
havn en noun common
/havn/ · HAVN
harbour, haven
Han hævdede, at vandet i atomkraftværkets havn i Fukushima ikke længere er radioaktivt.
He claimed that the water in the harbor of the nuclear power plant in Fukushima is no longer radioactive.
I Danmark kan man se vikingeskibe — i kopier, naturligvis — i Roskilde Havn i bunden af Roskilde Fjord.
Viking ships — in copies, naturally — are seen in Roskilde Harbor at the bottom of Roskilde Fjord, Denmark.
hedde verb rare
[ˈheðə] · HEDDE · hed-de
to be called
also: to be named; to be said, claimed
Hvad skal dit barn hedde?
What will you name the baby?
Tom kunne ikke beslutte sig for om hans hund skulle hedde Cookie eller Pochi.
Tom couldn't decide whether to name his dog Cookie or Pochi.
hel adjective common
/heːˀl/ · HEL
whole, entire, complete, full
also: whole, intact, undamaged; the hour, top of the hour; entire ()
En hel del er sket siden da.
A great deal has happened since that time.
Tom spiste en hel pose skumfiduser.
Tom ate a whole bag of marshmallows.
helbred et noun general
/ˈhɛlˌbʁɛð/ · HELBRED · hel-bred
health
Hvordan er Toms helbred?
How's Tom's health?
Vin er godt for dit helbred.
Wine is good for your health.
health
H
held et noun general
[ˈhɛlˀ] · HELD
luck, fortune
Ønsk mig held og lykke.
Please wish me luck.
Jeg ønsker dig held og lykke.
I wish you well.
heldigvis adverb common
[ˈhɛldiˈviˀs] · HELDIGVIS · hel-dig-vis
fortunately, luckily
Heldigvis var vejret godt.
Fortunately, the weather was good.
Heldigvis har jeg grønne fingre.
Fortunately, I have a green thumb.
heller adverb common
HELLER · hel-ler
either
Heller ikke mig!
Neither am I!
hellere adverb general
HELLERE · hel-le-re
rather
Vi må hellere vente.
We'd better wait.
Du må hellere komme.
You'd better come.

helst adverb common
/hɛlst/ · HELST
preferably; most of all; by preference
Spørg hvem som helst.
Ask anybody.
Hvem som helst kan gøre det.

helt en noun core
/hɛlˀt/ · HELT
hero
Helt sikkert!
Certainly!
Jeg er en helt.
I'm a hero.
hen adverb common
[ˈhɛn] · HEN
used with a verb, indicating a movement towards or to something
Hen er min lærer.
They're my teacher.
Hvor skal du hen?
Where are you going?
hendes pronoun core
[ˈhenəs] · HENDES · hen-des
, meaning her
Hendes far døde.
Her father died.
Jeg købte hendes.
I bought hers.
H
henholdsvis adverb common
HENHOLDSVIS · hen-holds-vis
respectively
De blev henholdsvis 24 og 21 år.
They were 24 and 21 years old, respectively.
hensigt en noun rare
/ˈhense̝ɡd/ · HENSIGT · hen-sigt
intention
Det er min hensigt.
That's my intention.
Dette er min hensigt.
This is my intention.
hensyn et noun common
HENSEWN · hen-syn
care, concern, consideration, regard, respect
Meg er naturbegavet med hensyn til sprog.
Meg has a natural gift for languages.
Vi bliver nødt til at tage hensyn til hendes dårlige helbred.
We must allow for her poor health.
hente verb common
HENTE · hen-te
to fetch, retrieve
also: to download
Jeg vil hente en.
I'll get one.
Kan I hente hjælp til ham?

henvendelse et noun general
HENVENDEL-SEH · hen-ven-del-se
contact, inquiry
"'Tak for din henvendelse."
“Thank you for your enquiry.
henvendelser en noun common
HENVENDELSER · hen-ven-del-ser
enquiries; requests; applications; contacts
"Har I fået nogle henvendelser?"
“Have you had any inquiries?”
henvise verb rare
HENVISE · hen-vi-se
to refer (to)
also: to link
"Henviser du til mig?"
"Are you referring to me?"
henvisning en noun general
HENVISNING · hen-vis-ning
reference
also: referral
Det kræver en henvisning fra lægen.
It requires a referral from the doctor.
H
herefter adverb general
HEREFTER · her-ef-ter
hereafter
Jeg gad vide hvad de vil gøre herefter.
I wonder what they'll do next.
herhjemme adverb general
HERHYEMME · her-hjem-me
here at home; in this country
I Sverige er det mere almindelig at et barn får tre fornavne end et enkeltnavn, men herhjemme er dette et mere usædvanligt fænomen.
In Sweden it is more common for a child to have three first names than one, but here, at home, this is a more unusual phenomenon.
hermed adverb rare
HERMED · her-med
hereby
"Det gør vi hermed!"
"We do!"
herunder adverb common
HERUNDER · her-un-der
included herein
also: under here; while or during something just mentioned is happening; less or fewer than the thing just mentioned
Det armenske folkemord efterlod halvanden million døde, herunder mænd, kvinder og børn.
The Armenian Genocide left a total of one million and five hundred thousand deaths, including men, women and children.

hidtil adverb common

So far, hitherto.
Det er hidtil rekord.
That's a record so far.
hinanden pronoun core
/hinanən/ · HINANDEN · hin-an-den
each other
I har hinanden.
You've got each other.
Vi har hinanden.
We've got each other.
historie et noun common
/histoːˀriɘ/ · HISTORIE · hi-sto-rie
history
also: story; affair, business
Din historie er sjov.
Your story is funny.
Fortæl os en historie!
Tell us a story!
historisk adjective general
HISTORISK · hi-sto-risk
historic
also: historical
Dette er et historisk øjeblik.
This is an historical moment.

hjem et noun core
[ˈjɛmˀ] · HYEM
home
Gå hjem!
Go home!

Come home!
home

HYEMLURS · hjem-løs
homeless (lacking a permanent residence)
Er du hjemløs?
Are you homeless?
Han er hjemløs.

home
hjemme adverb common
/jɛmə/ · HYEMME · hjem-me
at home, home
I er hjemme.
You're at home.
Bliv hjemme.
Stay home.
home
hjemmebane et noun general
HYEMMEBANE · hjem-me-ba-ne
home ground, home field
Så udtalt er hjemmebanefordelen.
That's how pronounced the home-field advantage is.
home

H
hjemmefra adverb common
HYEMMEFRA · hjem-me-fra
from home; working from home
Da jeg tog hjemmefra, sov min mor.
When I left home, my mother was sleeping.
Da jeg tog hjemmefra, lå min mor og sov.
When I left home, my mother was sleeping.
hjemmeside et noun common
/jɛməsiːðə/ · HYEMMESITHE · hjem-mesi-de
homepage
also: web site
Denne hjemmeside er ubrugelig.
This website is useless.
YouTube er ikke en god hjemmeside.
YouTube is not a good website.
home
hjerte et noun general
/jɛrtə/ · HYERTE · hjer-te
heart
Jeg har et hjerte.
I have a heart.
Du har intet hjerte.
You have no heart.
body culture
hjælp en noun core
[ˈjɛlˀb̥] · HYEHLP
help, assistance, rescue, support, aid
also: help! (cry of distress)
Hjælp!
Help!
Hjælp Tom!
Help Tom.
H
hjælpe verb core
HYEHLPE · hjæl-pe
to help
also: to assist; to aid; to help each other, see hjælpes ad
Kan vi hjælpe?
Can we help?
Vil du hjælpe?
Do you want to help?
hold et noun common
[ˈhʌlˀ] · HOLD
team
also: class; distance, side; truth
Hold mund!
Shut up!
drink
holde verb core
[ˈhʌlə] · HOLDE · hol-de
to hold
also: to keep; to last; to stop
Jeg vil holde ud.
I will endure.
Vil du holde kæft?
Would you shut up?
holdning en noun general
[ˈhʌlneŋ] · HOLDNING · hold-ning
posture, positioning of body
also: opinion
Min holdning til ham er ændret.
My attitude towards him has changed.
Han ændrede pludselig sin holdning.
He changed his attitude suddenly.

hos preposition core
[hɔs] · HOS
at X's abode
also: in X's view; as X expresses it in their writings
Bliv her hos os.
Stay here with us.
Tom køber æg hos os.
Tom buys eggs from us.
hospital et noun general
/hɔspitaːl/ · HOSPITAL · ho-spi-tal
hospital
Dette er et hospital.
This is a hospital.
Det er ikke et hospital.
It isn't a hospital.
hotel et noun general
HOTEL · ho-tel
hotel
Hvor er dit hotel?
Where's your hotel?
Der er et hotel dér.
There's a hotel there.
hoved et noun rare
/ˈhoːəð/ · HOVED · ho-ved
head
also: mind; person
Hans hoved gør ondt.
His head hurt.
Han brugte sit hoved.
He used his head.
body
H
hovedstad en noun general
[ˈhoːð̩ˌsd̥að] · HOVEDSTAD · ho-ved-stad
capital city

What's the capital of the United States?
Ukraines hovedstad er Kyiv.
The capital of the Ukraine is Kiev.
hul et noun common
/hol/ · HUL
hole
also: gap; blank; leak
Grav et dybt hul.
Dig a deep hole.
Der er et stort hul.
There's a big hole.
hund en noun general
[hunˀ] · HUND
dog
also: hound; hundred
Min hund gør.
My dog is barking.
Min hund gǿr.
My dog is barking.
hundrede et noun general
/hunrədə/ · HUNDRETHE · hund-re-de
hundred
also: hundredth; a unit of about one hundred
Hundrede procent.

hurtig adjective general
/horti/ · HURTEE · hur-tig
quick, rapid, fast
Er din bil hurtig?
Is your car fast?
Din bil er hurtig.
Your car is fast.
hurtigt adjective core
HURTIGT · hur-tigt
rapidly, expeditiously
Kom her hurtigt.
Come here quickly.
Gør det hurtigt.
Do it quickly.
hus et noun common
[ˈhuˀs] · HUS
house
also: building; block of flats, cottage; shell
Har I et hus?
Do you have a house?
Har du et hus?
Do you have a house?
home
husk verb rare
/husk/ · HUSK
to remember
Husk dette!
Remember this!
Husk at skylle ud.

H
huske verb common
HUSKE · hu-ske
to remember
Kan du huske os?
Do you remember us?
Kan I huske Tom?
Do you guys remember Tom?
hvem pronoun common
/vɛmˀ/ · VEM
who
also: who, that
Hvem?
Who?
Hvem ved?
Who knows?
hver pronoun core
/ˈvɛːˀr/ · VER
every, each
also: everyone
Jeg løber hver dag.
I run every day.
Jeg bader hver dag.
I bath every day.
hverdag en noun common
[ˈvæɐ̯ˌdæˀ] · VERDAG · hver-dag
weekday, working day
Er du tilfreds med din hverdag?

work time

H
hverken adverb common
/vɛrkən/ · VERKEN · hver-ken
neither
Tom ved hverken ud eller ind.
Tom is at his wits' end.
Jeg ved hverken ud eller ind.
I'm at my wits' end.
hvid en noun general
/ˈviːˀð/ · VID
white
also: blank; penny, cent/dime (han ejer ikke en hvid = he has not got a penny/cent/dime)
Er den hvid?
Is it white?
Den var hvid.
It was white.
hvilken pronoun common
/ˈvelɡən/ · VILKEN · hvil-ken
what, which, the
also: who, whom, which, whatever, whichever
Hvilken jungle!
What a jungle!
Hvilken lettelse!
What a relief!
hvilket pronoun rare
/ˈvelɡəð/
which; that; what (neuter relative pronoun)
Af dem var 1084 positive, hvilket svarer til 1,35 procent af prøverne.
H
hvis pronoun core
VIS
whose (of whom, of which)
Kom hvis du kan.
Come if you can.
Hvis hue er det?
Whose cap is this?
hvoraf adverb general
VORAF
of which; from which; whereof
Spækhuggeren dræbte tre mennesker, hvoraf to var dens trænere.
The killer whale killed three people, two of which were his trainers.
Storhertugdømmet Luxembourg har 563.000 indbyggere, hvoraf 46 % er udlændinge.
The Grand-Duchy of Luxembourg has a population of 563,000 of which 46% are foreigners.
hvordan adverb core
[ʋʌˈd̥an] · VORDAN · hvor-dan
how
Hvordan går det?
How are you doing?
Jeg ved hvordan.
I know a way.
hvorfor adverb common
/ˈvofɔ/ · VORFOR · hvor-for
wherefore, why
also: for this reason
Hvorfor?
Why?
Hvorfor mig?

H
hvorvidt conjunction common
VORVIDT · hvor-vidt
whether (used to introduce a clause that contains an indirect question, or that expresses uncertainty or doubt)
Vi er i tvivl om hvorvidt han har valgt den rette kurs.
We have some doubt as to whether he has chosen the right course.
hygge et noun general
/hykə/ · HEWGYE · hyg-ge
cosiness
also: to have a good time; to make it comfortable, cozy; to have a good time, enjoy oneself
Kom, lad os hygge os!
Come, let's chill!
Hygge er et nationalt ikon for os.
Coziness is a national icon for us.
hænge verb general
[ˈhɛŋə] · HEHNYE · hæn-ge
to hang
Hvorfor vil Tom hænge ud med os?
Why does Tom want to hang out with us?
Tom kan lide at hænge ud med Mary.
Tom likes hanging out with Mary.
hærværk et noun general
HEHRVEHRK · hær-værk
vandalism
Nu er det ramt af hærværk.
Now it is affected by vandalism.
H
hæve verb general
/hɛːvə/ · HEHVE · hæ-ve
to raise, to elevate, to lift
also: to rise; to draw, to withdraw, to cash (money); to suspend, to end, to lift
Gær får dej til at hæve.
Yeast makes dough rise.
Jeg vil gerne hæve penge.
I'd like to withdraw money.
høj adjective common
/hɔjˀ/ · HURY
high
also: tall; loud
Er han høj?
Is he tall?
Tom er høj.
Tom is tall.
højre adjective common
/hɔjrə/ · HURYRE · høj-re
right
Rul dit højre ærme op.
Roll up your right sleeve.
Jeg drejede til højre.
I turned right.
højst adverb general
/hɔjst/ · HURYST
very, most, highly, extremely
also: at most
Tom er højst sandsynligt hjemme.
Tom is most likely at home.
Du tror højst sandsynligt at Tom er døv.
You probably think Tom is deaf.

/ˈhøːʁə/ · HURRE · hø-re
to hear
also: to learn; to belong to; to belong under, come under

/hɔːb/ · HAWB
hope (belief that something hoped for can happen)
Vi har håb.

Det gav mig håb.
That gave me hope.

håbe verb general
/hɔːbə/ · HAWBE · hå-be
to hope

hånd en noun common
/hɔnˀ/ · HAWND
hand

body

HAWNDBOLD · hånd-bold

body

handling

H
hård adjective common
[hɒˀ] · HAWRD
hard
also: tough; severe, harsh
Hun er hård ved ham.
She is hard on him.
Den var hård som sten.
It was as hard as rock.
hårdest adjective general
HAWRDEST · hår-dest
supd
Gyldendal bliver hårdest ramt.
Gyldendal is hit hardest.
I
ide en noun general
ITHE
idé
Var det din ide?
Was it your idea?
Hvis ide var det?
Whose idea was it?
idet conjunction general
ITHET
as it is the case that, since, because
also: at the same time, just then
De bor til leje, idet stuehuset af Torupfonden.
They live for rent, as the farmhouse of Torupfonden.
I
idræt en noun general
[ˈiˌdʁad̥] · IDREHT
sports
also: sport; physical education
Ulykker sker hver dag i denne idrætsgren.
Accidents happen every day in this sport.
idé en noun common
[iˈd̥eˀ] · IDÉ
idea
Det er en skør idé.
This is a crazy idea.
Alle lo af min idé.
Everyone laughed at my idea.
ifølge preposition core
/iføljə/ · IFURLYE · iføl-ge
according to
also: in accordance with; pursuant to; as per
Ifølge Tom boede Mary i Boston.
According to Tom, Mary used to live in Boston.
Ifølge hende så kommer han ikke.
According to her, he isn't coming.
iføre verb rare
[ˈiˌføˀʌ] · IFURRE · ifø-re
to clothe, put on, don
also: to equip, provide with
Og hun har netop øje for, hvad andre bør iføre sig.
And she just has an eye for what others should wear.

ABCDEFGHIGEJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN igen – imidlertid 92
I
igen adverb core
IYEN
again
Aldrig igen!
Never again!
Vi ses igen.
We will meet again.
igennem preposition core
/iɡɛnˀəm/ · IYENNEM · igen-nem
through
Mennad er helt igennem led.
Mennad is completely nasty.
Jeg ved hvad du går igennem.
I understand what you're going through.
ignorere verb rare
[injoˈʁɛˀɐ] · INORERE · ig-no-re-re
to ignore
Du kan ikke ignorere Tom.
You can't ignore Tom.
Jeg kan ikke bare ignorere Tom.
I can't just ignore Tom.
ihjel adverb general
/ijɛl/ · IHYEL
death
Slå dem ihjel.
Kill them.
Slog I ham ihjel?
Did you kill him?
food
I
ild en noun general
/ilˀ/ · ILD
fire
also: ardor
Har du ild?
Do you have a light?
Ild brænder.
Fire burns.
imellem adverb common
/imɛləm/ · IMELLEM · imel-lem
between, between them
also: among them; between; betwixt
Det sker en gang imellem.
That happens sometimes.
Ja det sker en gang imellem.
Yes, it happens from time to time.
imens adverb general
/iˈmɛnˀs/ · IMENS
meanwhile
Sluk varmen imens.
Meanwhile, turn off the heat.
imidlertid adverb common
[iˈmið̠ˠˀlʌˌtið̠ˠˀ] · IMIDLERTID · imid-ler-tid
however
also: though
Tango blev imidlertid altid mobbet af andre naboers katte og kom hjem fuld af sår.
Tango was, however, always bullied by other neighbors' cats, and would come home covered with wounds.

I
imod adverb common
[iˈmoˀð] · IMOD
against
also: a variant of
Vi er imod det.
We oppose that.
indbrud et noun common
INDBRUD · ind-brud
burglary
Tom skød indbrudstyven i benet.
Tom shot the burglar in the leg.
Jeg troede, du var en indbrudstyv.
I thought you were a burglar.
indbygger en noun rare
[ˈenˌb̥yɡ̊ɐ] · INDBEWGYER · ind-byg-ger
inhabitant
Byen har mange indbyggere.
The city has a large population.
Mange indbyggere i USA taler spansk.
Many inhabitants of the United States speak Spanish.
inde adverb common
INDE · in-de
inside, indoors
Tiden er inde.
The time has come.
Hun blev buret inde.
She was thrown behind bars.
I
indeholde verb rare
INDEHOLDE · in-de-hol-de
to contain; to include; to hold
Kan indeholde nødder.
May contain nuts.
Dette glas kan indeholde to liter varmt vand.
This jar can hold two liters of hot water.
inden adverb core
INDEN · in-den
inner, interior
also: before; at a point reached earlier; before
Jeg betaler dig inden mandag.
I will pay you before Monday.
Cookie er inden under bordet.
Cookie is under the table.
indendørs adverb general
INDENDURRS · in-den-dørs
indoors
also: indoor, indoors
Gå indendørs når det tordner!
When thunder roars, go indoors!
Tom går sommetider med solbriller indendørs.
Tom sometimes wears sunglasses indoors.
home
indenfor adverb common
INDENFOR · in-den-for
inside
also: within
Vi gik indenfor.
We went inside.
Lad os få det indenfor.
Let's get it inside.

ABCDEFGHINDJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN indflydelse – indrette 94
I
indflydelse et noun general
/enflyːˀðəlsə/ · INDFLEWDEL-SEH · ind-fly-del-se
influence
Din ven har en dårlig indflydelse på dig.
Your friend has a bad influence on you.
Min far har stor indflydelse på rådhuset.
My father has a lot of clout at city hall.
indføre verb common
INDFURRE · ind-fø-re
to introduce, bring something new into effect
also: to import; to insert; to state, assert (in a book or other written format)
Mange amerikanere ønskede at indføre guldmøntfoden.
Many Americans wanted a gold standard.
Mange amerikanere ønskede at indføre guldstandarden.
Many Americans wanted a gold standard.
indgå verb general
INDGAW · ind-gå
to be or become a part of; to join
also: to join a joint (legally binding) agreement; be received
Min klient er villig til at indgå en aftale.
My client is willing to make a deal.
Langt om længe har de to indianerstammers høvdinge besluttet at indgå fred.
At long last, the two chiefs of the Indian tribes have decided to make peace.
culture
indhold et noun general
INDHOLD · ind-hold
content
Det er sponsoreret indhold.
It's sponsored content.
Han tømte beholderen for dens indhold.
He emptied the container of its contents.
I
indkøb et noun general
INDKURB · ind-køb
shopping, procurement
Jeg tog på indkøb.
I went shopping.
Jeg går på indkøb.
I'm going shopping.
indlægge verb rare
INDLEHGYE · ind-læg-ge
to admit (a patient into hospital)
also: to insert, install
Hun ville ikke indlægges.
She did not want to be admitted.
health
indre et noun general
/endrə/ · INDRE · in-dre
interior
also: mind; inner, internal
Tom bor i den indre by.
Tom lives downtown.
indrette verb rare
INDRETTE · ind-ret-te
to furnish for some purpose
also: to design
I bilen er indrettet tre stationer.
Three stations are arranged in the car.

ABCDEFGHINDJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZÆØÅ

I
indrømme verb rare
[ˈenˌʁɶmˀə] · INDRURMME · in-drøm-me
to admit, confess, recognize
Jeg må indrømme at du har ret.
I must admit, you're right.
indsats en noun common
INDSATS · ind-sats
effort, exertion
also: stake, pledge (item or money offered during a gamble, game or bet)
Tak for din indsats.
Thank you for your efforts.
Jeg tager hatten af for hendes indsats.
I take my hat off to her for her effort.
indstille verb general
INDSTILLE · indstil-le
to set, tune, adjust
also: prepare somebody for; make somebody realise or accept; discontinue, suspend, cease, stop, cancel; recommend, propose, advice, set up (for approval)
Stolens højde kan indstilles.
"The chair's height is adjustable."
USA og Cuba er indstillet til at reparere deres forhold efter et halvt århundredes fjendskab.
The USA and Cuba are set to mend their relationship after half a century of hostilities.
indtil conjunction core
/ˈenˀte/ · INDTIL · ind-til
until, till
also: until; up to; till
Jeg venter indtil 14:30.
I'll wait until 2:30.
Vent her indtil han kommer.
Be waiting here until he comes.
health
I
indtryk et noun general
INDTREWK · ind-tryk
impression
Tom gjorde indtryk på kejserinden.
Tom impressed the empress.
Forsøger du stadig at gøre indtryk på mig?
Are you still trying to impress me?
indtægt en noun rare
[ˈenˌtˢɛɡ̊d̥] · INDTEHGT · ind-tægt
income
Min indtægt er dobbelt så stor som din.
My income is twice as large as yours is.
information en noun general
INFORMATION · in-for-ma-tion
(a piece of)
Dette er nyttig information.
This is useful information.
Vær venlig at sende os mere information.
Please send us more information.
ingen pronoun core
/ˈenɡən/ · INYEN · in-gen
no
also: no one, nobody, nothing, neither, none
Ingen løj.
Nobody lied.
Ingen dør.
Nobody dies.

ABCDEFGHINIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN initiativ – interessere 96
I
initiativ et noun general
INITIATIW · ini-ti-a-tiv
initiative
Ingen tager initiativ.
No one takes the initiative.
inspiration en noun general
INSPIRATION · in-spira-tion
inspiration
EU-regler er inspiration.
EU rules are inspiration.
inspirere verb rare
INSPIRERE · in-spi-re-re
to inspire
Alle mulige typer kvinder inspirerer mig.
All kinds of women inspire me.
"Hvad inspirerede dig til at skrive denne sang?"
"What inspired you to write this song?"
institution en noun general
INSTITUTION · in-sti-tu-tion
institution
Og i institutioner.
And in institutions.
I
intensiv adjective general
INTENSIW · in-ten-siv
intensive
To på intensiv, sagde hun.
Two in intensive care, she said.
interessant adjective general
/entʁəˈsanˀd/ · INTERESSANT · in-ter-es-sant
interesting
also: of interest; interestingly
Interessant.
Interesting.
Det er interessant.
It's interesting.
interesse et noun common
/entərrasə/ · INTERESSE · in-ter-es-se
interest
Politik har ikke min interesse.
I'm not interested in politics.
Tom har ikke interesse for politik.
Tom is not interested in politics.
interessere verb rare
[entˢʁ̥ɐˈseˀɐ] · INTERESSERE · in-ter-es-se-re
to hold someone's interest
also: be interested
Hvornår begyndte du at interessere dig for at lære sprog?
How did you become interested in studying languages?
Siden hvornår er du begyndt at interessere dig for politik?
Since when do you care about politics?

ABCDEFGHINTJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN international – involvere 97
I
international adjective general
[ˈentˢɐnaɕoˈnæˀl] · INTERNATIONAL · in-ter-na-tio-nal
, having to do with more nations
also: , having to do with relations between two or more nations; , foreign, having to do with another nation than your own
Glædelig international kvindedag!
Happy International Women's Day!
Interlingua er et moderne redskab for international kommunikation.
Interlingua is a modern tool for international communication.
interview et noun common
INTERVIEW · in-ter-view
interview; press interview
Hvordan gik hans interview?
How did his interview go?
intet et noun rare
nothingness
Alligevel ser intet ud til for alvor at have ændret sig.
investere verb general
INVESTERE · in-ve-ste-re
to invest
also: to spend (money, resources, time etc.)
Kina investerer milliarder af dollars i Algeriet.
China is investing billions of dollars in Algeria.
I
investering en noun rare
INVESTERING · in-ve-ste-ring
investment
Dels var det en god investering.
On the one hand, it was a good investment.
invitere verb rare
INVITER · in-vi-ter
to invite; to have over; to ask over
Hvad laver han derude? Inviter ham indenfor!
What's he doing out there? Invite him inside!
invitere verb rare
INVITERE · in-vi-te-re
to invite
Tillad mig at invitere dig.
Allow me to invite you.
Vil du invitere hende til festen?
Are you going to invite her to the party?
social
involvere verb rare
INVOLVERE · in-vol-ve-re
to include, embrace, integrate
also: to involve (someone into something)
Jeg ville ikke involvere dig.
I didn't want you to get involved.
Jeg vidste, at jeg aldrig skulle have ladet mig involvere i din tåbelige plan.
I knew I should never have gotten involved with your hair-brained scheme.
Danish–English Dictionary  ·  Tatoeba (CC-BY)  ·  Leipzig (CC-BY) enetverbadjadvother DA → EN
ABCDEFGHISOJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN isolation – jord 98
I
isolation en noun general
ISOLATION · iso-la-tion
The act of isolating.
Han sidder i isolation.
He's in solitary confinement.
isolere verb rare
ISOLERE · iso-le-re
to isolate
also: to insulate; lag (cover, e.g. pipes, with felt strips or similar material)
Landsbyen er isoleret fra resten af verden.
The village is insulated from the world.
Vi har isoleret huset med 100 mm tykke EPS-plader.
"We've insulated the house with expanded polystyrene foam panels, 100 millimetres thick."
især adverb common
[iˈsɛːˀɐ̯] · ISEHR
especially, particularly
Han er især glad for hunde.
He is especially fond of dogs.
Jeg elsker alle bær, især jordbær.
I love all berries, especially strawberries.
culture
italiensk adjective rare
[itˢalˈjɛˀnsɡ̊] · ITALIENSK · ita-li-ensk
Italian
Hun er italiensk.
She is Italian.
Taler du italiensk?
Do you speak Italian?
J
jagt en noun general
YAGT
hunting, shooting
also: hunt, pursuit
Tom og Mary er gået på jagt.
Tom and Mary have gone hunting.
Jagt er forbudt i nationalparker.
Hunting is banned in national parks.
januar en noun common
/ˈjanuˌɑˀ/ · YANUAR · ja-nu-ar
January
Det er allerede januar.
It's already January.
Den 1. januar er nytårsdag.
The first of January is New Year's Day.
job et noun common
[ˈd̥jʌb̥] · YOB
a task (at home or at work)
Find et job!
Find a job.
Tom har et sejt job.
Tom has a cool job.
home work
jord en noun general
[ˈjoɐ̯ˀ] · YORD
earth
also: Earth ; see also; dirt, soil; ground (the natural surface)
Tom ejer denne jord.
Tom owns this land.
Vi kan blive tre hundrede år, men når vi så hører op at være til her, bliver vi kun skum på vandet, har ikke engang en grav hernede mellem vore kære. Vi har ingen udødelig sjæl, vi får aldrig liv mere, vi er ligesom det grønne siv, er det engang skåret over, kan det ikke grønnes igen! Menneskene derimod har en sjæl, som lever altid, lever, efter at legemet er blevet jord; den stiger op igennem den klare luft, op til alle de skinnende stjerner! Ligesom vi dykker op af havet og ser menneskenes lande, således dykker de op til ubekendte dejlige steder, dem vi aldrig får at se.
We sometimes live for three hundred years, but when we cease to exist here, we become only foam on the surface of the water and have not even a grave among those we love. We have not immortal souls, we shall never live again; like the green seaweed when once it has been cut off, we can never flourish more. Human beings, on the contrary, have souls which live forever, even after the body has been turned to dust. They rise up through the clear, pure air, beyond the glittering stars. As we rise out of the water and behold all the land of the earth, so do they rise to unknown and glorious regions which we shall never see.
culture
Danish–English Dictionary  ·  Tatoeba (CC-BY)  ·  Leipzig (CC-BY) enetverbadjadvother DA → EN

journalist en noun general
YOURNALIST · jour-na-list
a , a reporter
Hun er journalist.
She is a journalist.
Tom er journalist.
Tom is a journalist.
jul en noun common
[ˈjuˀl] · YUL
Christmas
God jul!
Merry Christmas!
Det er jul!
It's Christmas.
juli en noun common
/ˈjuːˀli/ · YULI
July
Vi har boet her siden juli.
We've been living here since July.
Sommerferien begynder i juli.
The summer vacation begins in July.
juni en noun core
/ˈjuːˀni/ · YUNI · ju-ni
June

She is expecting a baby in June.
Hun venter barn til juni.

justitsminister en noun general
YUSTITSMINISTER · ju-stits-mi-ni-ster
minister of justice, attorney general
Det oplyser Justitsministeriet.

kaffe et noun general
/ˈkɑfə/ · KAFFE · kaf-fe
coffee
Kaffe?

kalde verb general
KALDE · kal-de
to call, to refer to
Du kan kalde mig Tom.
You can call me Tom.
Lad os kalde det en dag.
Let's call it a day.
kamp en noun common
KAMP
battle, struggle (violent)
also: endeavour, struggle (non-violent); game, match (of e.g. ball games)
De døde i kamp.
They died in battle.
Det var en tæt kamp.

kampagne en noun general
KAMPANE · kampag-ne
campaign; drive; push
Som dagene gik, tog vores kampagne til i fart.
As the days passed, our campaign grew in momentum.
kandidat en noun general
KANDITHAT · kan-di-dat
candidate (someone who is up for consideration for a title, office, etc.)
also: a person who holds a master's degree
Denne kandidat er væsentligt bedre end sin forgænger.
This candidate is significantly better compared to his predecessor.
work
kapacitet en noun general
KAPACITET · ka-pa-ci-tet
capacity
Så vi har god kapacitet.
So we have good capacity.
karakter en noun general
/karakteːr/ · KARAKTER · ka-rak-ter
character
also: mark; grade
Hun er en kvinde med en stærk karakter.
She's a woman of strong character.
Det drejer sig ikke om hendes evner, men om hendes karakter.
The point at issue is not her ability but her character.
K
karriere en noun general
KARRIERE · kar-ri-e-re
career; profession
Det her bliver kronen på værket i min karriere.
This will be the crowning achievement of my career.
Skandalen var tæt på at ødelægge hendes karriere.
The scandal nearly wrecked her career.
kasse et noun rare
KASSE · kas-se
box
also: a rectangular cuboid
Hvad koster en kasse øl?
How much does a case of beer cost?
Hvordan laver man en kasse?
How do you make a box?
kaste en noun general
[ˈkʰasd̥ə] · KASTE · ka-ste
caste
also: to throw, to chuck; to drop; to cast
Jeg begyndte at kaste op.
I started to vomit.
Det er din tur til at kaste.
It's your turn to throw.
ked adjective general
[ˈkʰeðˀ] · KED
tired (of), sorry (about).
Jeg er ked af det.
I'm feeling blue.

K
kende en noun general
/ˈkɛnə/ · KENDE · ken-de
know
also: recognise as one’s own; to feel; a little bit
Du må lære dem at kende.
You must get to know them.
Du skal lære dem at kende.
You must get to know them.
kendskab et noun general
KENDSKAB · kend-skab
knowledge (awareness)
Jeg har noget kendskab til kemi.
I have some acquaintance with chemistry.
Hvordan fik du kendskab til Tatoeba-projektet?
How did you learn about the Tatoeba Project?
kendt adjective common
[ˈkʰɛnˀd̥] · KENDT
known
also: famous
Har du kendt ham længe?
Have you known him for a long time?
Han er kendt verden over.
He's famous around the world.
kigge verb common
/kikə/ · KIGYE · kig-ge
to look
also: to glance; to peek, peep, keek; to peer
Tom vil kigge på det.
Tom will look at it.
Jeg lover ikke at kigge.
I promise not to look.

kilde en noun rare
/kilə/ · KILDE · kil-de
spring, fountain, source
also: source, fountain, fount; to tickle
Hold op med at kilde mig!
Stop tickling me!
Appelsiner er en god kilde til vitamin C.
Oranges are a good source of vitamin C.
kilo et noun general
KILO · ki-lo
kilogram; kilo
Tom købte tre kilo æbler.
Tom bought three kilograms of apples.
Hvor mange kilo vejer du?
How many kilograms do you weigh?
kilometer en noun common
KILOMETER · ki-lo-me-ter

We ran for 10 kilometers.
Det er cirka 8 kilometer væk.

[ˈkʰinɛsisɡ̊] · KINESISK · ki-ne-sisk

[ˈkʰl̥æːjə] · KLAYE · kla-ge
complaint
also: to complain
Jeg ville klage.

Vi har fået en klage.

klar adjective core
[ˈkʰl̥ɑˀ] · KLAR
clear
also: bright; plain; distinct
Jeg er klar.

Vi var klar.

klasse et noun common
KLASSE · klas-se
class

They were in Joe's class last year.
Mary og jeg går i klasse sammen.
Mary and I are in the same class.

klassisk adjective general
[ˈkʰl̥asisɡ̊] · KLASSISK · klas-sisk
classic, exemplary, simple, harmonious
also: classical, belonging to Greco-Roman antiquity; classical; classical
Synes du om klassisk musik?
Do you care for classical music?
Det er et klassisk Nigeriabrev.
It's a classic Nigerian 419 scam.
body education
klima et noun general
KLIMA · kli-ma
climate
also: mood, atmosphere, vibe
Den ø har tropisk klima.
That island has a tropical climate.
Dette er et forfærdeligt klima.
This is a terrible climate.
environment
klog adjective rare
/klɔːʊ̯/ · KLOG
sensible
also: wise; intelligent; sagacious
Tom er klog.
Tom has brains.
Han var klog.
He was smart.
klokke et noun rare
/klɔkə/ · KLOKKE · klok-ke
bell
also: time, hour, o'clock
Får det ikke en klokke til at ringe?
Doesn't that ring a bell?
Det navn fik en klokke til at ringe.
That name rung a bell.

a club (organisation)
Denne klub har halvtreds medlemmer.

knap en noun common
/knap/ · NAP
scant, scarce
also: brief, concise; hardly, scarcely; just under
Knap flasken op.
Open the bottle.

kniv en noun general
/kniːv/ · NIW
knife
Har I en kniv?
Do you have a knife?

Do you have a knife?
kokain en noun general
KOKAIN · ko-kain
cocaine
Tom tager kokain.
Tom does blow.
Tom er afhængig af kokain.
Tom is addicted to cocaine.

kold adjective rare
/kɒl/ · KOLD
cold (having a low temperature)
also: cold (unfriendly, emotionally distant or unfeeling)
Den væg er kold.
That wall is cold.
Maden bliver kold.
The food is getting cold.
weather
kollega en noun general
[ˈkʰole̝ːka] · KOLLEGA · kol-le-ga
colleague

Min nye kollega er en robot.

[ˈkʰʌmə] · KOMME · kom-me
to come
also: to get; To appear, to manifest itself; come; to arrive
Du skal komme.
You must come.

He must come.
kommentar en noun common
/koməntar/ · KOMMENTAR · kom-men-tar
comment (spoken remark)
also: note, annotation; commentary; narration
Ingen kommentar.
No comment.
Jeg mente det som en seriøs kommentar.
I meant it as a serious comment.

K
kommunal adjective rare
KOMMUNAL · kom-mu-nal
of or pertaining to municipalities ()
Han henviser til kommunaldirektøren.
He refers to the municipal manager.
kommune et noun core
KOMMUNE · kom-mu-ne
municipality
I nogle kommuner.
In some municipalities.
kompensation en noun general
KOMPENSATION · kom-pen-sa-tion
a compensation
Alle bør have fuld kompensation.
Everyone should be fully compensated.
kompetence et noun rare
KOMPETENCE · kom-pe-ten-ce
competence, competency
Så tjek ved den kompetence.
Then check on that competence.
K
koncert en noun general
/kɔnsɛrt/ · KONCERT · kon-cert
concert
also: recital; concerto
Sikken fantastisk koncert!
What a great concert!
Hvilken fantastisk koncert!
What a great concert!
kone et noun general
[ˈkʰoːnə] · KONE · ko-ne
wife (married woman)
also: woman (frequent in compounds)
Toms kone døde.
Tom's wife passed away.
Min kone ryger.
My wife smokes.
konflikt en noun general
KONFLIKT · kon-flikt
conflict (clash or disagreement)
Tom var ikke en konfliktsøgende person.
"Tom wasn't a confrontational person."
Mary er landets førende ekspert på konflikten i Syrien.
"Mary is the country's foremost expert on the conflict in Syria."
konklusion en noun rare
KONKLUSION · kon-klu-sion
conclusion
Det tog os tre timer at komme til denne konklusion.
It took us three hours to come to this conclusion.
I denne tid med postfaktuel politik er det nemt at håndplukke data og komme til hvilken som helst konklusion man ønsker.
In this era of post-truth politics, it's easy to cherry-pick data and come to whatever conclusion you desire.

ABCDEFGHIJKONLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN konkret – kontakte 105
K
konkret adjective common
KONKRET · kon-kret
concrete
also: characterized by concretism; concrete noun
”Ikke så konkret.
"Not so concrete.
konkurrence et noun general
KONKURRENCE · kon-kur-ren-ce
competition (action of competing)
Det sovjetiske folk er ikke bange for fredelig konkurrence med kapitalismen.
The Soviet people are not afraid of peaceful competition with capitalism.
konkurs adjective general
/kɔnɡkurs/ · KONKURS · kon-kurs
bankrupt
also: insolvent; failure; bankruptcy
Leonids firma var ved at gå konkurs.
Leonid's company was about to go bankrupt.
Leonids virksomhed var ved at gå konkurs.
Leonid's company was about to go bankrupt.
money
konsekvens en noun general
/kɔnsəkvɛns/ · KONSEKVENS · kon-se-kvens
consequence (that which follows something on which it depends; that which is produced by a cause)
also: consistency (reliability or uniformity; the quality of being consistent)
"Det vil få store konsekvenser!"
"Consequences will never be the same!"
"Konsekvenserne vil aldrig blive det samme!"
"Consequences will never be the same!"
K
konservativ adjective rare
/konsɛrvatiːv/ · KONSERVATIW · kon-ser-va-tiv
conservative
Biden er konservativ.
Biden is a conservative.
Jeg synes, at din mor har opdraget dig på en meget konservativ måde.
I think that your mother has brought you up in a very conservative manner.
konstatere verb general
KONSTATERE · kon-sta-te-re
to ascertain, establish, determine
Ingen personer konstateret ramt.
No people found affected.
kontakt en noun common
[kɔnˈtɑɡd] · KONTAKT · kon-takt
A contact person
also: Contact; A socket, power outlet.
Hold dig i kontakt.
Please keep in touch.
Jeg er i kontakt med Tom.
I'm in touch with Tom.
family
kontakte verb general
KONTAKTE · kon-tak-te
to contact
Hvordan kan jeg kontakte dig?
How can I contact you?
Vi bliver nødt til at kontakte Toms forældre.
We need to contact Tom's parents.

K
kontant adjective rare
KONTANT · kon-tant
cash; in cash; blunt; direct
Sami betaler altid kontant.
Sami always pays in cash.
Du ønsker at betale kontant?
You want to pay in cash?
kontor et noun general
[kʰɔnˈtˢoɐ̯ˀ] · KONTOR · kon-tor
office
Han er på sit kontor.
He is at his office.
Tom har ikke et kontor.
Tom doesn't have an office.
work
kontrakt en noun general
KONTRAKT · kon-trakt
contract
also: agreement (written)
I stedet for at vente på en kontrakt, kan vi klare det over telefonen.
Instead of waiting for a contract, we can handle it over the phone.
kontrast en noun rare
KONTRAST · kon-trast
contrast; difference
Den røde rose skabte en fin kontrast til hendes hvide kjole.
The red rose made a nice contrast to her white dress.
K
kontrol en noun common
KONTROL · kon-trol
control
also: controller
Branden er under kontrol.
The fire is under control.
Brandene kom ud af kontrol.
The fires got out of control.
korrekt adjective general
KORREKT · kor-rekt
correct
Korrekt!
Correct!
Svaret er korrekt.
The answer is correct.
kort et noun core
/kɔrt/ · KORT
short
also: brief; briefly; curtly
Tom er kort.
Tom is short.
Det var kort.
It was short.
koste verb common
KOSTE · ko-ste
to cost; to have a price; to be for sale
also: to boss around
Det vil koste 30 euro.
That will cost thirty euros.
Det vil koste tredive euro.
That will cost thirty euros.
money

ABCDEFGHIJKRALMNOPQRSTUVWXYZÆØÅ

K
kraft en noun common
[ˈkʰʁ̥ɑfd̥] · KRAFT
strength
also: force; power, continuous energy
A-kraft er dyrt og farligt.
Atomic energy is expensive and dangerous.
Forordningen vil træde i kraft næste år.
This regulation will take effect next year.
kraftig adjective general
KRAFTEE · kraf-tig
powerful
also: strong; robust; sturdy
Sasha havde en kraftig smerte i sin højre hånd.
Sasha had a sharp pain in his right hand.
Alice vendte tidligt hjem fra arbejde med en kraftig hovedpine.
Alice returned home early from work with a severe headache.
krav et noun common
/kraːˀv/ · KRAW
a requirement
also: a demand
Jeg har et krav til.
I have one more demand.
krave et noun rare
KRAVE · kra-ve
collar
Du har en plet på kraven.
Your collar has a stain on it.
Tom brækkede kravebenet i en motorcykelulykke.
Tom broke his collarbone in a motorcycle accident.
K
krig en noun general
[ˈkʰʁ̥iˀ] · KREE
war
Undgå krig.
Avoid war.
Vi er i krig.
We're at war.
kriminalitet en noun general
KRIMINALITET · kri-mi-na-li-tet
crime, criminality
Det er her kriminaliteten sker.
This is where crime happens.
kriminel adjective rare
KRIMINEL · kri-mi-nel
criminal
Denne mand er kriminalbetjent, ikke en kriminel.
This man is a police officer in the crime squad, not a criminal.
krise en noun common
KRISE · kri-se
crisis
Europa er i krise.
Europe is in crisis.
Landet er i krise.
The country is in crisis.

ABCDEFGHIJKRILMNOPQRSTUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN kritik – krænkelse 108
K
kritik en noun common
KRITIK · kri-tik
criticism
also: critique
Din kritik er ubegrundet.
Your criticism is unfounded.
Kritik af den rene fornuft er den tyske filosof Immanuel Kants hovedværk.
Critique of Pure Reason is German philosopher Immanuel Kant's chief literary work.
kritisere verb rare
KRITISERE · kri-ti-se-re
to criticise (UK) / criticize (US)
Vær ikke bange for at kritisere Israel.
Don't be afraid of criticizing Israel.
Enhvert fjols kan kritisere, fordømme og klage — og det gør de fleste fjolser. Men det kræver karakter og selvkontrol at være forstående og tilgivende.
Any fool can criticize, condemn and complain—and most fools do. But it takes character and self-control to be understanding and forgiving.
kritisk adjective common
KRITISK · kri-tisk
critical
Situationen er kritisk.
The situation is critical.
Nu er han indlagt på hospitalet i kritisk tilstand.
Now he is in the hospital in critical condition.
krone en noun rare
[ˈkʰʁ̥oːnə] · KRONE · kro-ne
crown
also: , crown; heads; top
Slå plat eller krone.
Flip a coin.
Lad os slå plat og krone!
Let's toss a coin.
K
krop en noun general
/krɔp/ · KROP
body
also: trunk, torso; carcass, carcase; body, fuselage
Over min døde krop!
Over my dead body.
Hun har en perfekt krop.
She has a perfect body.
kryds en noun general
/krys/ · KREWDS
cross
also: crossroads, intersection; croup; loin
Dette kryds er farligt.
This intersection is dangerous.
kræft en noun general
[ˈkʁ̥æfd̥] · KREHFT
cancer
Du har kræft.
You have cancer.
Jeg har kræft.
I have cancer.
krænkelse et noun rare
KREHNKEL-SEH · kræn-kel-se
violation, infringement, harassment, affront
"Er du klar til krænkelses-fastelavn?"
"Are you ready for infringement fasting?"

Ordbog DA→EN kræve – kurs 109
K
kræve verb general
/krɛːvə/ · KREHVE · kræ-ve
to demand
also: to require; to claim
"Vi kræver handling!"
We demand action.
Islamisterne kræver mere.
Islamists are demanding more.
kultur en noun common
KULTUR · kul-tur
culture
Ungdommen har sin egen kultur.
Youth has its own culture.
Man kan ikke adskille sprog og kultur.
You can't separate language from culture.
kun adverb core
[ˈkʰɔn] · KUN
only
Tag kun ét.
Take only one.
Tag kun én.
Take one only.
kunde en noun general
/konə/ · KUNDE · kun-de
customer
also: shopper, client, patron
Du er en god kunde.
You are a good customer.
Hun var den sidste kunde.
She was the last customer.
K
kunne verb rare
[ˈkʰunə]
to be able, can
also: to be allowed, may; could, would, might; to know
Aalborg Zoo satser på at kunne åbne 8. juni.
kuns adverb rare
KUNS
kun
"Hvad er kunst?"
"What is art?"
Hun er kunstlet.
"She's artsy."
kunstner en noun general
/ˈkonˀs(d)nɐ/ · KUNSTNER · kunst-ner
An artist.
Tom er kunstner.
Tom is an artist.
Mary er kunstner.
Mary is an artist.
culture
kurs en noun general
KURS
course
also: price, quotation, exchange rate
Skibet ændrede kurs.
The ship changed its course.
Vi er i tvivl om hvorvidt han har valgt den rette kurs.
We have some doubt as to whether he has chosen the right course.
education

kvadratmeter en noun common
KVADRATMETER · kva-drat-me-ter
square metre, or square meter (US) (SI unit of area measurement)
Metrotoget var så fyldt at der stod omkring fem mennesker per kvadratmeter.
The subway was so full that there were around five people per square meter.
kvalitet en noun general
KVALITET · kva-li-tet
quality
also: a quality, a property
Det her er brandy af lav kvalitet.
This is poor quality brandy.
Det her er brændevin af lav kvalitet.
This is poor quality brandy.
kvart adjective rare
[ˈkʰʋ̥ɑːd̥] · KVART
a quarter of, quarter-
also: quarter; fourth; quarto
Klokken er kvart i to.
It's a quarter to two.
Klokken er kvart i tre.
It's a quarter to three.

kvartal et noun general
[kʰʋ̥ɑˈtˢæˀl] · KVARTAL · kvar-tal
a quarter

The next quarter could be even worse.

kvinde et noun common
/ˈkvenə/ · KVINDE · kvin-de
A woman.
Jeg er kvinde.
I'm a woman.

She's a woman.
kvindelig adjective general
KVINDELEE · kvin-de-lig
female (of humans)
also: the corpse of a woman
Den kvindelige lærer var omgivet af sine studerende.
The teacher was surrounded by her students.
Merkel var Tysklands første kvindelige kansler og den første leder, der var opvokset i det tidligere Østtyskland.
Merkel was Germany’s first female chancellor and its first leader to have been raised in the former East Germany.
kæmpe en noun common
KEHMPE · kæm-pe
giant
also: warrior; plantain (species)
Det er et kæmpe træ.
It is a huge tree.
Jeg vil kæmpe til døden.
I will fight to the death.
kær et noun rare
/kɛːˀr/ · KEHR
pond, pool
also: marsh, fen; dear; adorable, cute
Hun er mig kær.
She is dear to me.

købe verb common
[ˈkʰøːb̥ə] · KURBE · kø-be
to buy

køkken et noun general
[ˈkʰøɡŋ̩] · KURKKEN · køk-ken
a kitchen
also: cuisine (style or method of cooking); a galley (kitchen on an aircraft)

I kølvandet fulgte 20 medarbejdere.

/køːrə/ · KURRE · kø-re
to drive; to ride

KURREKORT · kø-re-kort

[ˈkʰøːɐnə] · KURRENDE · kø-ren-de
to participle

[ˈkʰøːɐˌtˢʌj] · KURRETURY · kø-re-tøj

/kør(ˀ)səl/ · KURRSEL · kør-sel
driving
Uagtsom kørsel forårsager ulykker.

Careful driving prevents accidents.

lade en noun common
[ˈlæːðə] · LATHE · la-de
barn (building)
also: let; leave; have
Du kan lade os gå.
You could let us go.

I'll let you decide.
land et noun common
/lanˀ/ · LAND
country
also: country, countryside
Det er vores land.
That is our country.
Taiwan er et land.
Taiwan is a country.
landbrug et noun general
LANDBRUG · land-brug
agriculture
Mange algeriere er beskæftiget i landbruget.
Many Algerians are employed in agriculture.
Priserne på landbrugsejendomme var faldet femoghalvtreds procent.
Farm prices had fallen fifty-five percent.

lang adjective core
/lanɡ/ · LANG
long (having great distance)
Vejen er lang.
The way is long.
Sikken lang dag!
What a long day!

langs adverb common
/læŋˀs/ · LANGS
along (by the length of)
Vi sejlede langs kysten.

The road follows the river.

LANGSOMT · lang-somt
slow

Speak slowly!
Drik langsomt!

LASTBIL · last-bil
truck (vehicle designed for carrying cargo)
also: lorry (motor vehicle)

lav adjective general
/ˈlaːˀv/ · LAW
low
also: short; shallow; humble; inferior; base
Min puls er lav.
My pulse is low.
Priserne er lave denne uge.
The prices are low this week.
lav et noun rare
/ˈlaːˀv/ · LAW
guild; trade association (historical)
also: lichen (the plant)
Skomagernes lav regulerede håndværket i middelalderen.
The shoemakers' guild regulated the craft in the Middle Ages.
work culture
lave verb core
/ˈlaːvə/ · LAVE · la-ve
to make, create, construct, produce
also: to cook, prepare; to do; to repair, mend, fix
Han vil lave kaffe.
He'll make coffee.
Jeg bad ham lave te.
I asked him to make tea.
food
led et noun common
LED
joint movable connection between the bones in a body or a minor exterior part of the
also: link movable part of a thing or a plant; link, part element in a line of thought or a course of events; generation in a family tree
Han er led.
He is nasty.
Tom er led.
Tom is nasty.
L
lede verb general
/ˈleːðə/ · LETHE · le-de
to manage, run
also: to head, direct; to lead, guide; to conduct
Hun har indvilliget i at lede partiet.
She has consented to take the leadership of the party.
Hvem har sat den gamle mand til at lede?
Who put that old guy in charge?
ledelse et noun common
[ˈleːðəlsə] · LETHEL-SEH · le-del-se
leadership, management
also: administration, board, committee
"Er der god nok ledelse?"
"Is there good enough leadership?"
leder en noun common
/leːdər/ · LETHER · le-der
leader, manager, head

ledig adjective rare
/ˈleːð̞i/ · LETHEE · le-dig
unoccupied
also: unemployed; single, not in a committed relationship (especially marriage)

leg en noun rare
[ˈlɑjˀ] · LAY
play, game
also: spawning (fish)
Kom ud og leg!
Come out and play.
Leg ikke med maden!
Don't play with your food.
lej verb rare
/lɑj/ · LAY
to rent; to hire
also: to let; to lease
Kan jeg leje en cykel her?
Can I rent a bicycle here?
Tom lejede en SUV.
Tom rented an SUV.
lejlighed en noun common
[ˈlɑjliˌheðˀ] · LAYLIGHETH · lej-lig-hed
apartment, flat
also: opportunity; occasion
Lejlighed gør tyv.
Opportunity makes a thief.
Tom har en lejlighed.
Tom has an apartment.
home culture
let en noun common
[ˈlɛd̥] · LET
light
also: easy; slight; mild
Svensk er let.
Swedish is easy.
Han tog det let.
He took it lightly.
L
lette en noun general
/lɛtə/ · LETTE · let-te
Lett, Latvian
also: lighten; relieve; facilitate
Det er lette penge.
It's easy money.
Der er ingen lette løsninger.
There's no silver bullet.
leve verb common
[ˈleːʋə] · LEVE · le-ve
to live, be alive
also: to live off or live on; to still exist (not extinct or abolished); to appear present and lively
Lad mig leve.
Let me live.
Jeg vil leve.
I want to live.
levende adjective general
LEVENDE · le-ven-de
alive
also: live; living, vivid, lively
Tom er levende.
Tom is alive.
Tokyo er levende.
Tokyo is bustling with life.
levere verb common
/leverə/ · LEVERE · le-ve-re
to supply, furnish
also: to provide; to contribute; to produce
Hvor lang tid tager det at levere en pizza?
How long does it take to deliver a pizza?
Det er vanskeligt at levere en god oversættelse uden hjælp fra en korrekturlæser.
It is difficult to provide a good translation without the help of a proofreader.

ABCDEFGHIJKLIDMNOPQRSTUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN lide – ligne 115
L
lide verb general
[ˈliːðə] · LITHE · li-de
to suffer
also: To have some disease or similar condition.; See; to approach
Tom vil lide.
Tom will suffer.
Kan du lide te?
Do you like tea?
lidt adverb core
/let/ · LIDT
a little, slightly
also: a bit, a little; liked
Sov lidt.
Get some sleep.
Vent lidt.
Wait a bit.
lige en noun core
[ˈliːə] · LIYE · li-ge
like, match
also: straight, not bent; equal; even
Lige meget!
Whatever.
Vent lige der.
Wait right there.
ligefrem adjective general
LIYEFREM · li-ge-frem
blunt, frank, straightforward
also: even, exactly
Han var ikke ligefrem kærestemateriale.
He wasn't exactly boyfriend material.
Kvinderne kaster sig ikke ligefrem om halsen på mig.
Women aren't exactly throwing themselves at me.
L
ligeledes adverb common
[ˈliːəˌleːðˠəs] · LIYELETHES · li-ge-le-des
likewise
Naturligvis kan du ikke sige at det intet er værd, fordi det ikke er perfekt, ligeledes betyder det ikke at GTD er nyttesløst.
Of course you can't say that it's worth nothing because it isn't perfect, so that doesn't mean that GTD is useless.
ligesom adverb common
LIYESOM · li-ge-som
sort of, kind of, as it were
Jeg er meget ligesom dig.
I'm a lot like you.
Gør det igen, ligesom før.
Do it again, just like before.
ligge verb common
[ˈleɡ̊ə] · LIGYE · lig-ge
to lie
also: to be; to stand; to sit
Lad ikke kufferten ligge der.
Don't leave your suitcase there.
Lad ikke din kuffert ligge der.
Don't leave your suitcase there.
ligne verb rare
[ˈliːnə] · LINE · lig-ne
to resemble, look like
also: take after; be like; to assess
Når man begynder at ligne sit pasbillede, burde man tage på ferie.
When you're beginning to look like the photo in your passport, you should go on a holiday.

line
also: file

life
also: waist, middle; bodice

lokale et noun core
/lokaːlə/ · LOKALE · lo-ka-le
room
also: hall; office

lov en noun common
/ˈlɔv/ · LOW
law

L
love noun general
/ˈlɔːvə/ · LOVE
trust, faith
also: to promise; to praise; palm of the hand
Love laves altid af de rige mod de fattige.
Laws are always made by the rich against the poor.
Smith hævder at ingen internationale love kan anvendes i denne sag.
Smith argues that no international laws can be applied to this case.
lovlig adjective rare
LOVLEE · lov-lig
lawful
also: legal
Skilsmisse er lovlig i Algeriet.
Divorce is legal in Algeria.
luft en noun general
[ˈlɔfd̥] · LUFT
air

Air is invisible.
Dækket taber luft.
The tire leaks air.
lufthavn en noun general
LUFTHAVN · luft-havn
An airport
Jeg havde aldrig før set Roms lufthavn så tom.
I had never seen Rome airport so empty.

lukke et noun common
[ˈlɔɡ̊ə] · LUKKE · luk-ke
fastening, lock
also: cover; closure, flap, tap,; to close
Må jeg lukke vinduet op?
May I open the window?
Glem ikke at lukke døren.
Don't forget to close the door.
lyde en noun general
LEWDE · ly-de
flaw, blemish
also: to sound, to be realized; to sound (some way); to obey
Ved at lære dit sprogs lyde får du adgang til ord.
By learning the sounds of your language, you gain access to words.
Det kan lyde mærkeligt, men han er pludselig forsvundet.
This might sound strange, but he has suddenly disappeared.
politics
lykkes verb general
/løkəs/ · LEWKKES · lyk-kes
to be a success, be successful, succeed in
also: to succeed in
Du fortjener at lykkes.
You deserve to succeed.
Jeg håber, at jeg lykkes.
I hope I succeed.
lys et noun common
[ˈlyˀs] · LEWS
light, bright
also: fair, blond; cheerful, sunny; high-pitched
Du har lys på.
You left your lights on.
Månen er så lys!
The moon's so bright.

/ˈlydə/ · LEWTTE · lyt-te
to listen

supd

lære verb common
[ˈlɛːɐ] · LEHRE · læ-re
to teach
also: to learn
Jeg vil lære.

lære en noun common
[ˈlɛːˀɐ] · LARE · læ-re

/ˈlɛːsə/

[ˈløˀb̥] · LURB

also: race; barrel (of a firearm); course, channel (of a river or stream)

He ran.
løbe verb common
/løːbə/ · LURBE · lø-be
to run
also: to curdle
Han hader at løbe.

løbende adjective common
LURBENDE · lø-ben-de
ongoing; current; continuous; running
Han kom løbende.

Drengen kom løbende.

løfte et noun general
LURFTE · løf-te
promise, pledge, vow
Det er et løfte.
That's a promise.
Han brød sit løfte.
He broke his promise.
lørdag en noun core
/ˈlœɐ̯da/ · LURRDAG · lør-dag
Saturday

I dag er det lørdag.

[ˈløːsə] · LURSE · lø-se
to solve
also: to untie; to loose, release
Jeg skal løse problemet.
I am going to solve the problem.
Det vil ikke løse noget.
That won't solve anything.
løsning en noun common
LURSNING · løs-ning
an answer (solution)
also: a solution, resolution; loosening, slackening, releasing; a solution
Har du en løsning?

lån et noun general
[ˈlɔˀn] · LAWN
loan

låne verb general
[ˈlɔːnə] · LAWNE · lå-ne
to borrow
also: to lend

/mað/ · MAD
food
also: a slice of bread with something on top.

food
magt en noun general
[ˈmɑɡ̊d̥] · MAGT
power

Visdom er magt.
Wisdom is power.
mail en noun common
MAIL
email

Did you read my email?
maj en noun core
/maj/ · MAY

Maj kommer efter april.
May comes after April.
Jeg ankommer den 23. maj.

M
mand en noun core
/manˀ/ · MAND
man
also: husband
Mand over bord!
Man overboard!

Tom is a man.
mandag en noun core
/ˈmanˀda/ · MANDAG · man-dag
Monday

It's Monday.
Det er kun mandag.
It's only Monday.
time
mandlig adjective general
MANDLEE · mand-lig
male, manly
I dag bliver en kvinde som er automekaniker set som "sej", mens en mandlig frisør af mange automatisk bliver stemplet som "bøsse".
Nowadays, a woman who is an auto mechanic is "cool", while a man hairdresser is automatically titled as "gay" by many.
mange adjective core
[ˈmɑŋə] · MANYE · man-ge
many; a lot of; numerous
Mange tak.
Thanks!
M
mangel en noun general
MANYEL · man-gel
lack, want, absence
also: defect, flaw, deficiency, shortcomings; disadvantage, drawback; shortage, scarcity
Der er mangel på arbejdere.
There is a lack of workers.
Planterne døde af mangel på vand.
The plants died for lack of water.
mangle verb rare
/manɡlə/ · MANGLE · mang-le
to lack
also: to want; to need; to be missing
Dette manglede.
This was missing.
Jeg mangler ord.
Words fail me.
markant adjective common
MARKANT · mar-kant
significant, prominent, notable, marked
also: markedly, significantly
På markant kortere tid.
In significantly less time.
marked et noun general
/markəd/ · MARKED · mar-ked
market
also: fair; emporium
USA er et godt marked for japanske produkter.
The USA is a good market for Japanese products.
Vi er nødt til at finde et nyt marked for disse produkter.
We have to find a new market for these products.

marts en noun core
/marts/ · MARTS
March

March is over.

/masə/ · MASSE · mas-se
mass, pulp
also: multitude, crowd, mob; many, loads, a lot, lots; mass

massiv adjective general
MASSIVT · mas-sivt
massive; solid; substantial
Denne statue er lavet af massivt guld.
This statue is made of solid gold.
Den 7. oktober 2023 gennemførte Hamas et massivt overraskelsesangreb på Israel.
On October 7, 2023, Hamas led a massive surprise attack on Israel.
materiale et noun general
/matriaːlə/ · MATERIALE · ma-te-ri-a-le
material

Jeg har ikke samlet nok materiale til at kunne skrive en bog.

mave en noun rare
/maːvə/ · MAVE · ma-ve
stomach, tummy, belly
also: abdomen, abdominal cavity; belly; potbelly, paunch
Min mave rumler.
My stomach is rumbling.
Ryg ikke på en tom mave!
Don't smoke on an empty stomach.
medarbejder en noun common
[ˈmɛðɑːˌb̥ɑjˀd̥ɐ] · METHARBAYDER · me-d-ar-bej-der
employee
Han er ikke vores mest produktive medarbejder.
He isn't our most productive employee.
Det er svært at erstatte en medarbejder, der er så kvalificeret som han er.
It is difficult to replace an employee that is as qualified as he is.
work

[ˈmɛðˌd̥eːˀlə] · MEDDELE · med-dele

to result in, lead to, bring about, cause

medie et noun general
/meːdiə/ · METHIE · me-die

member a person that is part of a group or organisation

Du er ikke medlem af familien.

MELD
to imp

Jeg ønsker ikke at melde mig selv.

MELDING · mel-ding
announcement
also: report; message, indication
Meldingen var klar.

between

environment
M
milliard en noun general
MILLIARD · mil-li-ard
billion

"Why does someone need a billion dollars?" "No one needs a billion dollars."
million en noun common
[miliˈoˀn] · MILLION · mil-li-on

Ordbogen indeholder cirka en halv million ord.

/miːn/
mine
Alle børnene boede hos min mor.

also: at least

minimum

time

Misforstå mig ikke!

Men misforstå mig ikke.

to lose

modern

Hun er en moderne pige.

modsat adjective general
/ˈmoðsæd̥/ · MODSAT · mod-sat
opposite
also: oppositely

to contradict

[ˈmoðˌsd̥anˀ] · MODSTAND · mod-stand

modsætning en noun general
MODSEHTNING · mod-sæt-ning
contrast; contradiction; opposition (i modsætning til = unlike; in contrast to)
I modsætning til i går, er det slet ikke varmt i dag.
In contrast to yesterday, it isn't hot at all today.
Livet er ikke kort – i modsætning til den offentlige mening.
Life is not short, contrary to public opinion.
modtage verb common
MODTAYE · mod-ta-ge
to receive, get, accept
Jeg ser frem til at modtage dit svar snarest.
I look forward to receiving your reply as soon as possible.

/moːɐ̯/ · MOR
mother
also: raw humus; Moor; to have fun
Mor jer!
Have fun!

Hello, Mum!

morgen en noun core
/ˈmɔr.ən/ · MORYEN · mor-gen
morning (the part of the day after midnight and before midday)

motorvej en noun rare
MOTORVAYE · mo-tor-ve-je
motorway; highway; freeway

possible, feasible

MUNDBIND · mund-bind

museum
Du bør besøge dette museum.

/musik/ · MUSIK · mu-sik

[ˈmɛŋˀd̥ə] · MEHNGDE · mæng-de

Jeg lagde ikke mærke til ham.

[ˈmɔːðə]

determined; having and pursuing a goal
Hvad skulle have været en målrettet militær operation, endte med at være en reel nedslagtning af civile.

måned en noun common
/ˈmɔːnəð/ · MAWNED · må-ned
month

/mɔskeː/ · MAWSKE · må-ske
perhaps, possibly, maybe

/ˈmɔtə/ · MAWTTE · måt-te

also: must, have to, got to; can, may; may

I had to hide.

nabo en noun general
/naːbo/ · NABO · na-bo
neighbour
Jeg er din nabo.
I'm your neighbor.
Min nabo er læge.
My neighbour is a doctor.
nat en noun common
/nad̥/ · NAT
night (period between sunset and sunrise)
God nat.
Good night.
Mød mig i nat.
Meet me tonight.
weather
national adjective general
[naɕoˈnæˀl] · NATIONAL · na-tio-nal
, having to do with a particular country in opposition to other nations
also: , having to do with the whole and not only single parts of it; patriotic, having positive feelings for one's own nation
Tom vil erklære national nødsituation.
Tom will declare a national emergency.
Maria, der lige er blevet udnævnt til National koordinator mod voldelig ekstremisme, deltog i en demonstration, hvor man rituelt afbrændte det elbonske flag.
Maria, who has just been appointed as National Coordinator against violent extremism, participated in a demonstration in which the Elbonian flag was ritually burned.
culture
N
natur en noun common
/natuːr/ · NATUR · na-tur
nature
also: disposition; temper; countryside
Han har en blid natur.
He has a mild nature.
Helen er af natur optimist.
Helen is by nature an optimist.

NATURLEE · na-tur-lig
natural
Hun er en naturlig skønhed.
She's a natural beauty.
Jordens måne er en naturlig satellit.
The earth's moon is a natural satellite.
naturligvis adverb common
/natuːrliviːs/ · NATURLIGVIS · na-tur-lig-vis
of course
also: naturally; obviously
Naturligvis.
Naturally.
Du har naturligvis ret.
You're right, of course.
education
navn et noun common
[ˈnɑwˀn] · NAVN
name (word or phrase indicating a particular person, place, class or thing)
also: reputation
Er dit navn Tom?
Is your name Tom?
Mit navn er Tom.
My name is Tom.

N
nede adverb common
/neːdə/ · NETHE · ne-de
down (at a lower place or position)
Bliv nede!
Stay down!
Serveren er nede.
The server is down.
nederlag et noun general
NETHERLAG · ne-der-lag
(a) defeat
USA's kvindelandshold i fodbold led et chokerende 3-0-nederlag til Sverige.
The U.S. women’s soccer team suffered a shocking 3-0 defeat to Sweden.
food
negativ et noun general
NEGATIW · ne-ga-tiv
negative
Vær ikke så negativ.
Don't be so negative.
Min saldo er negativ.
My balance is negative.
nej interjection common
/nɛj/ · NAY
no
also: oh, oh no
Nej.
No.
Nej!
No!
N
nem adjective common
NEMT · nemt
easy; simple; straightforward
Det er nemt.
This is easy.
Det er meget nemt.
It's very easy.
nemlig adverb core
/ˈnɛmli/ · NEMLEE · nem-lig
that is, that is to say
also: namely, viz.; because, as; right, exactly
Kan du klare dig uden bilen i morgen? Jeg skal nemlig bruge den.
Can you do without the car tomorrow? I need it.
net adjective rare
/nɛt/ · NET
visually pleasing and proper; well-groomed
also: , web; tote bag
De fleste edderkopper spinder net.
Most spiders weave webs.
Både tjenere og frøkener gik og pyntede det. På en gren hang de små net, udklippet af kulørt papir; hvert net var fyldt med sukkergodt; forgyldte æbler og valnødder hang, som om de var vokset fast, og over hundrede røde, blå og hvide smålys blev stukket fast i grenene.
The servants, and the young ladies also, decked out the tree. On one branch they hung little nets, cut out of colored paper; every net was filled with sweetmeats; golden apples and walnuts hung down as if they grow there, and more than a hundred little candles, red, white, and blue, were fastened to the different boughs.
home
netop adverb core
NETOP · net-op
precisely [this]; [this] very
also: just, just now
Jeg har netop spist.
I just ate.
Det er netop problemet.
That's just the problem.
Danish–English Dictionary  ·  Tatoeba (CC-BY)  ·  Leipzig (CC-BY) enetverbadjadvother DA → EN
ABCDEFGHIJKLMNETOPQRSTUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN netværk – nordjysk 133
N
netværk et noun general
NETVEHRK · net-værk
network
Google bruger et neuralt netværk til at oversætte sætninger.
Google uses a neural network to translate sentences.
Hackere finder nye måder at trænge ind i private og offentlige netværk.
Hackers find new ways of infiltrating private or public networks.
niveau et noun common
/nivo/ · NIVEAU · ni-veau
level
Vi er alle på forskellige niveauer.
We are all at different levels.
Øgede CO2-niveauer bidrager væsentligt til generel mangel på næringsstoffer i vores afgrøder.
Increased CO₂ levels are significantly contributing to a general lack of nutrients in our crops.
nogen pronoun core
[ˈnoən] · NOYEN · no-gen
some
also: somebody, someone, something; any; anybody, anyone, anything
Nogen lo.
Somebody laughed.
Så I nogen?
Did you guys see anybody?
nogenlunde adjective general
NOYENLUNDE · no-gen-lun-de
reasonably well, alright
also: somewhat, to some degree
»Det er nogenlunde det samme.
It is somewhat similar with the European Union.
family
N
nogensinde adverb common
/ˈnoːɡənˈsenə/ · NOYENSINDE · no-gen-sin-de
ever, at some time
Har du nogensinde røget?
Have you ever smoked?
Har du nogensinde snorket?
Have you ever snored?
time
nok en noun core
[ˈnʌɡ̊] · NOK
tap
also: yardarm, peak; something curved', related to (a) key, protruding pin-/wedge-shaped part of an object, pin that engages in a corresponding hole, outer end of a spar, e.g. a boom on a ship, end of a quay/pier Den_Danske_Ordbog; enough
Det er nok.
That's enough.
Nok er nok!
Enough is enough.
nord en noun common
/noːr/ · NORD
north
also: toward the north, northwards
Hvor er nord?
Where's the North?
Tom kommer fra nord.
Tom comes from the north.
nordjysk adjective rare
NORDYEWSK · nord-jysk
North Jutlandic, North Jutish
also: Danish dialects spoken in Northern Jutland
Og det nordjyske håndtryk.
And the North Jutland handshake.

ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOROPQRSTUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN nordlig – nyde 134
N
nordlig adjective rare
NORDLEE · nord-lig
northern
also: northerly
Det nordlige Algeriet har middelhavsklima.
Northern Algeria has a Mediterranean climate.
Store Bjørn er et stjernebillede på den nordlige stjernehimmel.
Ursa Major is a constellation in the northern sky.
normal en noun general
[nɒˈmæˀl] · NORMAL · nor-mal
a normal value or quantity
also: line or surface that is perpendicular to another line or surface; ordinary, usual, typical; usually, for the most part
Din puls er normal.
Your pulse is normal.
Hold op med at betragte mig som en "normal" person!
Stop seeing me as a "normal" person!
norsk adjective rare
/nɔrsk/ · NORSK
Norwegian
Jeg elsker norsk!
I love Norwegian!
Toms kone er norsk.
Tom's wife is Norwegian.
november en noun common
NOVEMBER · novem-ber
November
Sidste måned var november, ikke sandt?
Last month was November, right?
Niels Bohr døde den 18. november 1962.
Niels Bohr died the 18th of November 1962.
N
nummer et noun common
[ˈnɔmˀɐ] · NUMMER · num-mer
number
also: track
Hvem er nummer ét?
Who's number one?
Ring til dette nummer.
Call this number.
nuværende adjective common
NUVEHRENDE · nu-væ-ren-de
current, present
Dilma Rousseff er Brasiliens nuværende præsident.
Dilma Rousseff is the current President of Brazil.
Kvinden der sidder derovre, er hans nuværende kone.
The woman sitting over there is his present wife.
ny et noun core
/nyː/ · NEW
new
also: fresh; recent; novel
Den er ny.
It is new.
Er du ny her?
Are you new here?
nyde verb general
/nyːdə/ · NEWDE · ny-de
to enjoy, relish
also: have, take; indulge in; to receive
Jeg forsøger at nyde livet.
I'm trying to enjoy life.
Det tror jeg, jeg ville nyde.
I think I wouldn't enjoy doing that.
emotion

ABCDEFGHIJKLMNYHOPQRSTUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN nyhed – nærme 135
N
nyhed en noun general
NEWHETH
A report of current events, news
also: Novelty, new thing
Jeg har en dårlig nyhed.
I have bad news.
Vi havde ikke hørt denne nyhed.
We hadn't heard this news.
nylig adjective general
/nyːli/ · NEWLEE · ny-lig
recent
also: recently; lately; newly
Jeg har ikke set Tom for nylig.
I haven't seen Tom lately.
Har du hørt fra hende for nylig?
Have you heard from her recently?
nytår et noun general
/nytɔr/ · NEWTAWR
New Year
Godt Nytår!
Happy New Year!
Godt nytår!
Happy New Year!
time
nægte verb rare
NEHGTE · næg-te
to contest, dispute
also: to decline, refuse; to prohibit, forbid
Jeg ville nægte at gøre det.
I'd refuse to do that.
N
næppe adverb general
NEHPPE · næp-pe
probably not (referring to likelihood)
also: hardly (referring to quantity)
Vi undslap med nød og næppe.
We had a narrow escape.
Jeg undslap med nød og næppe.
I had a narrow escape.
nær adjective common
/nɛːr/ · NEHR
near
also: close; nearly
Hun stod nær vinduet.
She was standing near the window.
Jeg var nær ved at dø.
I nearly died.
nærhed en noun rare
/nɛrhed/ · NEHRHETH · nær-hed
closeness, nearness, proximity
also: imminence
De bor i nærheden.
They live nearby.
Jeg bor i nærheden.
I live nearby.
nærme verb rare
NEHRME · nær-me
to bring closer
also: to approach
Fra og med 1950 begynder de europæiske lande indenfor rammerne af Det Europæiske Kul- og Stålfællesskab at nærme sig hinanden økonomisk og politisk for at sikre varig fred.
As of 1950, the European Coal and Steel Community began to unite European countries economically and politically in order to secure lasting peace.
Fra og med 1950 begynder de europæiske lande inden for rammerne af Det Europæiske Kul- og Stålfællesskab at nærme sig hinanden økonomisk og politisk for at sikre varig fred.
As of 1950, the European Coal and Steel Community began to unite European countries economically and politically in order to secure lasting peace.
