ABCDEFGHIJKLMNÆROPQRSTUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN nærmere – nødvendig 136
N
nærmere adjective common
NEHRMERE · nær-me-re
(more) precise, thorough, detailed
also: precisely; thoroughly
Kom nærmere.
Come closer.
Kom nærmere!
Come closer.
nærmest adjective common
NEHRMEST · nær-mest
almost
also: approximately
Hvilken planet er nærmest Solen?
Which planet is nearest to the Sun?
Merkur er den planet der er nærmest Solen.
Mercury is the planet nearest to the sun.
næste en noun core
/nɛstə/ · NEHSTE · næ-ste
neighbour
also: next; the following; to sew together
Hvad er næste stop?
What is the next stop?
næsten adverb common
/nɛstən/ · NEHSTEN · næ-sten
nearly
also: almost; all but; practically
Næsten tre.
Nearly three.
Den er næsten ny!
It's almost new!
N
nævn et noun rare
/nævn/ · NEHVN
board
Nævn ikke krigen!
Don't mention the war!
Nævn én ting jeg er god til.
Name one thing I'm good at.
nævne verb general
NEHVNT · næv-nt
to mention; to name; to cite
Fik jeg nævnt at jeg er skør?
Did I mention I'm crazy?
Jeg har nævnt det tidligere overfor dig.
I have mentioned it to you before.
nødt adjective common
[ˈnøˀd̥] · NURDT
forced to; obliged
Jeg er nødt til at gå.
I have to go.
I er nødt til at vente.
You have to wait.
nødvendig adjective general
[nøðˈʋɛnˀd̥i] · NURDVENDEE · nød-ven-dig
necessary, essential
also: inevitable
Ingen forudgående erfaring er nødvendig.
No prior experience is necessary.

ABCDEFGHIJKLMNØDOPQRSTUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN nødvendighed – offer 137
N
nødvendighed en noun rare
NURDVENDIGHETH · nød-ven-dig-hed
necessity, need
At vide hvordan man bøjer udsagnsord, er en absolut nødvendighed.
Knowing how to conjugate verbs is an absolute necessity.
nødvendigvis adverb general
[nøðˈʋɛnˀd̥iʋiˀs] · NURDVENDIGVIS · nød-ven-dig-vis
necessarily
Store mænd er ikke nødvendigvis stærke mænd.
Big men are not necessarily strong men.
Det du ser med øjnene er nødvendigvis ikke sande.
Things that you see with your eyes are not necessarily true.
nøje adverb general
NURYE · nø-je
carefully; precisely; closely
Tom kontrollerede nøje at der var lukket for gassen.
Tom carefully checked that the gas was turned off.
Jeg ved at I har fulgt situationen i Algeriet meget nøje.
I know you have been following the situation in Algeria very closely.
nøje verb general
[ˈnʌjə] · NURYE · nø-je
to make do; to settle for; to be content with
Nu kan jeg nøjes med fire«
Now I can settle for four'
N
nå interjection common
/nɔːˀ/ · NAW
oh; well; I see
also: really?; is that so? (surprised or sceptical response)
Nå, jeg må gå nu.
Well, I have to go now.
Nå, det vidste jeg ikke.
Oh, I didn't know that.
O
offentlig adjective common
[ˈʌfənd̥li] · OFFENTLEE · of-fent-lig
public
Er der en offentlig strand her i nærheden?
Is there a public beach around here?
Dette er en offentlig gade, ikke en væddeløbsbane!
This is a public street, not a racetrack.
offentlighed en noun rare
OFFENTLIGHETH · of-fent-lig-hed
general public, public
also: publicity
Biblioteket er midlertidig lukket for offentligheden.
The library is temporarily closed to the public.
Biblioteket er midlertidigt lukket for offentligheden.
The library is temporarily closed to the public.
offer et noun rare
OFFER · of-fer
sacrifice
also: victim
Tom bliver det næste offer.
Tom will be the next victim.
Tom var ikke det eneste offer.
Tom wasn't the only victim.

Ordbog DA→EN officiel – omkostning 138
O
officiel adjective rare
OFFICIEL · of-fi-ci-el
official
EU har 24 officielle sprog.
The EU has 24 official languages.
"Har du set den officielle rapport?"
"Have you seen the official report?"
bureaucracy
ofte adverb common
/ɔf.tə/ · OFTE · of-te
often, frequently
Flyver I ofte?
Do you fly frequently?
Flyver du ofte?
Do you fly frequently?
oktober en noun common
OKTOBER · ok-to-ber
October
Det skete i oktober.
That happened in October.
Tom døde 20. oktober.
Tom died October 20th.
omfang et noun common
OMFANG · om-fang
extent, scope, size, range, reach, volume, circumference, girth
Stort i omfang og i indhold.
Large in scope and in content.
O
omfatte verb rare
OMFATTE · om-fat-te
To include
also: To involve
Toms gøremål omfatter blandt andet at rive blade sammen.
"Tom's chores include raking the leaves."
omfattende adjective common
OMFATTENDE · om-fat-ten-de
comprehensive
also: extensive; thorough; wide
»Det er omfattende.
"It is extensive.
omgang en noun common
[ˈʌmˌɡ̊ɑŋˀ] · OMGANG · om-gang
turn
also: round; dealings; handling
Tom gav en omgang.
Tom bought drinks for everybody.
Vært, en omgang til!
Landlord, another round!
omkostning en noun rare
OMKOSTNING · om-kost-ning
costs
Omkostningerne var røget i vejret.
Costs were through the roof.
Hun vil gennemføre sine planer uanset omkostningerne.
She will carry out her plans, regardless of the cost.
money

ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOMKPQRSTUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN omkring – onsdag 139
O
omkring adverb core
OMKRING · om-kring
about
Omkring!
About face!
Jeg ser mig omkring.
I'm looking around.
område et noun common
OMRAWDE · om-rå-de
area, region, territory (physically/geometrically)
also: area
Han er en autoritet på sit område.
He's an authority in his field.
Hun er en autoritet på sit område.
She's an authority in her field.
omstændighed en noun rare
OMSTEHNDIGHETH · om-stæn-dig-hed
circumstance
Denne omstændighed beviser hans uskyld.
This fact proves his innocence.
Denne omstændighed beviser hendes uskyld.
This fact proves her innocence.
omtale verb rare
OMTALE · om-ta-le
to mention
also: to refer to as, to call (by a name), to describe as
Sagen fik meget omtale.
The case received a lot of publicity.
O
omvendt adjective general
OMVENDT · om-vendt
reverse, opposite
also: on the other hand, conversely
Fentanyl kommer hovedsageligt ind i Canada fra USA, og ikke omvendt.
Fentanyl largely enters Canada from the U.S., rather than the other way around.
Britiske videnskabsmænd har godtgjort at hvis du vender det japanske flag omvendt, får du det japanske flag.
British scientists have established that if you turn the flag of Japan upside down, you get the flag of Japan.
ondt adjective general
ONDT
pain; hurt; evil; ill (gøre ondt = to hurt; have ondt i = to have a pain in)
Det gør ondt her.
It hurts here.
Gør det her ondt?
Does this hurt?
online adjective common
ONLINE · on-li-ne
online; on the internet
Hej, vi er online!
Hi, we're online!
Hun er aldrig online, ikke en gang i hendes ferie.
She is never online, even during her vacation.
onsdag en noun core
/ˈoːnˀsda/ · ONSDAG · ons-dag
Wednesday
I dag er det onsdag.
It's Wednesday.
Jeg skal klippes på onsdag.
I'm getting a haircut on Wednesday.
time

ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPDPQRSTUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN opdage – ophold 140
O
opdage verb rare
OPDAYE · op-da-ge
to discover, detect
"Hvem opdagede øen?"
"Who discovered the island?"
"Hvem opdagede Amerika?"
"Who discovered America?"
opdatere verb rare
OPDATERE · op-da-te-re
to update
For fremtiden vil Firefox opdatere plugins for dig.
In the future, Firefox will upgrade plugins for you.
opfattelse et noun general
OPFATTEL-SEH · op-fat-tel-se
conception, perception, understanding, apprehension
Den opfattelse er latterlig.
This notion is ridiculous.
Ifølge en landsdækkende meningsmåling i USA er det en udbredt opfattelse at muslimer er forbundet med terrorisme.
It is a prevalent belief, according to a nationwide poll in the United States, that Muslims are linked with terrorism.
technology
opfordre verb general
OPFORDRE · op-for-dre
to encourage (someone to do something)
Folk opfordrede til en øjeblikkelig invasion af Cuba.
People called for an immediate invasion of Cuba.
O
opfordring en noun general
OPFORDRING · op-for-dring
request; call; urging; invitation
Tom siger at han gjorde det på opfordring fra John.
Tom says he did it at John's behest.
opføre verb general
OPFURRE · op-fø-re
to behave a particular way
also: stage (a play); erect, build; add to a list, registry etc.
Hold op med at opføre dig som en baby.
Stop acting like a baby.
Ingen har til hensigt at opføre en mur.
Nobody intends to erect a wall.
culture
opgave et noun common
OPGAVE · op-ga-ve
task
Det er en simpel opgave.
It's a simple job.
Vores opgave er at genopføre muren.
Our task is to rebuild the wall.
work
ophold et noun general
OPHOLD · op-hold
stay; residence; sojourn; stopover
Vi gjorde ophold i Chicago.
We had a layover in Chicago.
Vi har en halv times ophold i Paris.
We have a stop for half an hour in Paris.

O
oplagt adjective general
/ɔplaɡt/ · OPLAGT · op-lagt
in a good mood
also: fit, in form; obvious; flagrant
Det er helt oplagt.
It is an open-and-shut case.
emotion
opleve verb common
OPLEVE · op-le-ve
to experience
also: to sense or feel; to enjoy an artistic performance; to believe or ascertain on the basis of personal experience
Han talte om sine oplevelser.
He talked about his experiences.
Det var en af de mest utrolige oplevelser i mit liv.
It was one of the most incredible experiences of my life.
oplevelse et noun common
OPLEVEL-SEH · op-le-vel-se
experience, adventure, sensation
also: experience
Han talte om sine oplevelser.
He talked about his experiences.
Det var en af de mest utrolige oplevelser i mit liv.
It was one of the most incredible experiences of my life.
oplyse verb general
/ɔplyːˀsə/ · OPLEWSE · op-ly-se
to light up, illuminate
also: to enlighten; to report, state
Betjenten bad mig om at oplyse mit navn.
The police officer asked me what my name was.
health
O
oplysning en noun rare
OPLEWSNING · op-lys-ning
information
also: Enlightenment, Aufklärung; literal illumination
Disse oplysninger er fortrolige.
This information is confidential.
Jeg har fået nogle nye oplysninger.
"I've got some new information."
bureaucracy
opmærksom adjective common
/ˈɔpˌmæɐ̯ɡsɒm/ · OPMEHRKSOM · op-mærk-som
alert, attentive, mindful, observant, observing, watchful
Vær opmærksom!
Pay attention!
Når man oversætter, skal man være opmærksom på »falske venner«!
When translating, one should be aware of "false friends"!
opmærksomhed en noun common
/ʌbˈmæɐ̯ɡsʌmˌheðˀ/ · OPMEHRKSOMHETH · op-mærk-som-hed
attention, acuity, attentiveness
Jeg prøvede uden held at aflede deres opmærksomhed.
I tried to divert their attention without success.
Det var tydeligt at Tom forsøgte at få Marys opmærksomhed.
It was obvious that Tom was trying to get Mary's attention.
opnå verb general
OPNAW · op-nå
to achieve, obtain, accomplish
also: to reach, arrive at
Hun anstrengte sig for at opnå sine mål.
She worked hard to reach her goals.

Ordbog DA→EN oppe – orden 142
O
oppe verb common
OPPE · op-pe
to try one's best; try to improve
Du er sent oppe!
You're up late.
Var du oppe hele natten?
Were you up all night?
oprette verb general
[ˈʌbˌʁadə] · OPRETTE · op-ret-te
to create
also: to establish
Der er oprettet venteliste.
A waiting list has been created.
food
oprindelig adjective rare
OPRINDELEE · op-rin-de-lig
original
»Propaganda« er oprindelig et latinsk ord, der betyder »noget, der skal udbredes«.
"Propaganda" is originally a Latin word which means "things which are to be spread."
opslag et noun general
/ɔpslaːˀɣ/ · OPSLAG · op-slag
entry
also: lookup; notice; advertisement
Det var et hårdt opslag.
It was a tough post.
O
opstå verb general
[ˈʌb̥ˌsd̥ɔˀ] · OPSTAW · op-stå
to arise
also: to emerge, reappear (from the dead).
Der kan opstå problemer.
Problems may arise.
optaget adjective common
OPTAYET · op-ta-get
busy; occupied; engaged; taken
Optaget!
Occupied!
Er du optaget?
Are you busy?
ord et noun common
/oːˀr/ · ORD
word
Jeg savner ord.
Words fail me.
orden en noun general
/ɔrˀdən/ · ORDEN · or-den
order, neatness
also: order; order; order
Alt i orden.
She's apples.
Her er alt i orden.
Everything's in order here.
food

ABCDEFGHIJKLMNORDPQRSTUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN ordentlig – overfor 143
O
ordentlig adjective general
/ɔr(də)nli/ · ORDENTLEE · or-dent-lig
correct, orderly, regular, well-ordered
also: neat, tidy; methodical, orderly, tidy; accurate, careful
"Spørg ordentligt!"
Ask properly.
Opfør dig ordentligt.
Behave yourself.
ordentligt adjective general
ORDENTLIGT · or-dent-ligt
accurately, awfully, carefully, correctly, decently, methodically, neatly, properly, regularly, steadily, thoroughly, tidily, well
Spørg ordentligt!
Ask properly.
Opfør dig ordentligt.
Behave yourself.
food drink transport
organisation en noun general
ORGANISATION · or-ga-ni-sa-tion
organization
Denne organisation er bundkorrupt.
That organization is corrupt to the core.
Denne organisation er gennemkorrupt.
That organization is corrupt to the core.
otte adjective common
/ˈɔːtə/ · OTTE · ot-te
eight
also: early morning
Hun er otte.
She is eight.
Hans søn er otte år.
His son is eight years old.
O
oven adverb general
OVEN
above
also: placed higher up than
Æblet ligger oven på bogen.
The apple is on top of the book.
Katten sidder oven på bordet.
The cat is sitting on top of the table.
overbevise verb rare
OVERBEVISE · over-be-vi-se
to convince; to persuade
Hvordan vil I overbevise Tom?
How are you going to convince Tom?
Jeg kunne ikke overbevise Tom.
I couldn't convince Tom.
overblik et noun common
OVERBLIK · over-blik
an overview, a perspective
Få overblik her.
Get an overview here.
overfor adverb common
/ɔvərfɔr/ · OVERFOR · over-for
opposite
Du løj overfor mig.
You lied to me.
Han løj overfor mig.
He lied to me.

Ordbog DA→EN overholde – overtage 144
O
overholde verb general
OVERHOLDE · over-hol-de
to comply, keep to, respect
Du skal overholde loven.
You must observe the law.
Man skal overholde loven.
You must observe the law.
overhovedet adverb common
OVERHOVETHET · over-ho-ve-det
at all, whatsoever
Han svarer overhovedet ikke.
He's not responding at all.
Kan du overhovedet lide mig?
Do you even like me?
overlæge et noun general
OVERLEHYE · over-læ-ge
some kind of doctor with authority over other doctors
Overlæge og stor viden om Tyskland.
Consultant and great knowledge of Germany.
health bureaucracy
overraske verb rare
[ˈɒwɐˌʁɑsɡ̊ə] · OVERRASKE · over-ra-ske
to surprise (cause (someone) to feel surprise)
Jeg ville overraske hende.
I wanted to surprise her.
Tom forsøgte at overraske Mary.
Tom tried to surprise Mary.
emotion
O
overraskelse et noun general
OVERRASKEL-SEH · over-ra-skel-se
surprise
also: astonishment
Hvilken overraskelse!
What a surprise!
Jeg har en overraskelse til dig.
I have a surprise for you.
overskud et noun general
OVERSKUD · over-skud
surplus
also: profit
Det giver overskud og energi.
It provides profit and energy.
oversvømmelse et noun rare
OVERSVURMMEL-SEH · over-svøm-mel-se
flood
Oversvømmelser i Vejle.
Flooding in Vejle.
overtage verb general
[ˈɒwʌˌtˢæˀ] · OVERTAYE · over-ta-ge
to take over (assume control of something)
"Kunsten har overtaget!"
"Art has taken over!"

O
overtrædelse et noun general
OVERTREHDEL-SEH · over-træ-del-se
infringement, violation, transgression
"Hvad var overtrædelsen?"
"What was the violation?"
overveje verb general
[ˈɒwɐˌʋɑjˀə] · OVERVAYE · over-ve-je
to consider, think about, contemplate
also: to weigh more
Jeg vil overveje det.
I'll think about it.
Du burde virkelig overveje at arbejde her.
You should seriously consider working here.
P
pakke en noun general
PAKKE · pak-ke
package
also: parcel; packet; pack
Kan du pakke den ind som en gave?
Can you gift wrap that?
Adressen på denne pakke er forkert.
The address on this parcel is wrong.
pandemi en noun general
PANDEMI · pan-de-mi
a pandemic
Som I allesammen ved, er vi midt i en global pandemi.
As you all know, we are in the midst of a global pandemic.
Efter den falske pandemi kan jeg ikke tro et ord af, hvad medierne siger.
After the fake pandemic I can't believe a word the media say.
P
par en noun core
[ˈpʰɑ] · PAR
pair
also: couple
De er et par.
They're a couple.
Jeg har tre par sko.
I have three pairs of shoes.
park en noun general
/paːrk/ · PARK
park
Er dette bussen til Park Ridge?
Is this the bus for Park Ridge?
Hvilken vej fører til Central Park?
Which way is Central Park?
parti et noun common
/parti/ · PARTI · par-ti
lot, quantity, batch
also: party; game (of e.g. chess or tennis); A prospective spouse perceived as an economical or social boon rather than a person; a
Du tager altid hendes parti!
You always pick her side.
Det parti lefler altid for middelklassen.
That party is always pandering to the middle class.
passager en noun rare
PASSAYER · pas-sa-ger
passenger
Ziri forventede ikke en passager.
Ziri wasn't expecting a passenger.

passe verb common
PASSE · pas-se
to look after
also: to be true; to fit
Jeg skal passe kaninen.
I have to look after the rabbit.
Rygtet lader til at passe.
The rumor seems true.
patient en noun general
[pʰaˈɕɛnˀd̥] · PATIENT · pa-tient
patient (person or animal who receives treatment from a doctor or other medically
Tom er min patient.
Tom is my patient.
Åh nej! Vores patient er død.
Oh no! Our patient is dead.
patrulje et noun general
PATRULYE · pa-trul-je
patrol
also: scouts; the surveillance of something
»Vi har en patrulje tæt på.
»We have a patrol close by.
pause en noun common
PAUSE · pau-se
pause
Tag en pause.
Take a break.
Hold en pause.
Have a break.
P
pege verb rare
/pajə/ · PEYE · pe-ge
to point
Det er ikke pænt at pege.
It is bad manners to point at people.
Det er uhøfligt at pege ad folk.
It is rude to point at others.
penge en noun core
/pɛŋə/ · PENYE · pen-ge
money
I har penge.
You have money.
Har du penge?
Do you have money?
money
pension en noun common
PENSION · pen-sion
a , boarding house
also: pension, periodic payments from a retirement fund; lodging and meals that is provided by such places
Jeg er gået på pension.
I'm retired.
Hvornår skal du på pension?
When will you retire?
home
per preposition common
PER
for each; for every
Fugle lærer at flyve per instinkt.
Birds learn to fly by instinct.
Vi har langt mere end bare biceps i armene, Per.
We've got a lot more than just biceps in our arms, Per.

perfekt adjective general
PERFEKT · per-fekt
perfect
also: perfectly
Perfekt!
Perfect!
Tom er perfekt.
Tom is perfect.
periode et noun common
PERIOTHE · pe-ri-o-de
period
also: era; term of office; time
Et årti er en periode på ti år.
A decade is a span of ten years.
Over en lang periode havde jeg for vane at gå tidligt i seng.
Over a long period of time, I had the habit of going to sleep early.
permanent adjective general
[pæɐ̯maˈnɛnˀd] · PERMANENT · per-ma-nent
permanent
Alle ønsker permanent fred.
Everybody wants permanent peace.
Denne pens blæk er permanent.
This pen's ink is indelible.
person en noun common
PERSON · per-son
person
also: character; figure; people
Den person er fuld.
That person is drunk.
Jeg er en modig person.
I'm a brave person.
P
personale et noun common
PERSONALE · per-so-na-le
personnel, staff
Katte har ikke ejere; de har personale.
Cats don't have owners, they have staff.
Hunde har ejere, men katte har personale.
Dogs have owners, but cats have staff.
family
personbil en noun general
PERSONBIL · per-son-bil
car; passenger car; private car
En lastbil bruger normalt mere benzin end en personbil.
A truck typically uses more gas than a car.
personlig adjective general
PERSONLEE · per-son-lig
personal
Det er et spørgsmål om personlig smag.
It's a question of personal taste.
family
personligt adjective general
PERSONLIGT · per-son-ligt
personlig
Han kom personligt.
He came in person.
Tag det ikke personligt.
Don't take it personally.

P
pige et noun common
/ˈpiːə/ · PIYE · pi-ge
girl
also: girl, woman; girl, girlfriend; girl, daughter
Er du en pige?
Are you a girl?
Hvem var den pige?
Who was that girl?
placere verb general
[pʰl̥aˈseˀɐ] · PLACERE · pla-ce-re
to place
also: to invest; to site
Algeriet er strategisk placeret mellem Europa og subsaharisk Afrika.
Algeria is strategically located between Europe and sub-Saharan Africa.
Forskerne sporede fuglene med små radiosendere placeret på deres rygge.
The scientists tracked the birds using small radio transmitters on their backs.
plads en noun core
/plas/ · PLADS
place
also: seat; position; berth
Tag plads!
Take a seat!
plan en noun common
PLAN
a plan
also: a level or plane
Har I en plan?
Do you have a plan?
Er der en plan?
Is there a plan?
P
planlægge verb general
PLANLEHGYE · plan-læg-ge
to plan
At planlægge brylluppet, før man frier, er at vende tingene på hovedet.
Planning the wedding before proposing is putting the cart before the horse.
plant verb rare
PLANT
to imp
Planter gror.
Plants grow.
Planter vokser.
Plants grow.
pleje verb general
/ˈplajə/ · PLAYE · ple-je
to care for; to nurse; to tend
also: to usually do; to be in the habit of (plejer at + infinitive)
Han plejede at drikke.
He used to drink.
Tom plejede at drikke.
Tom used to drink.
pleje en noun general
/ˈplajə/ · PLAYE · ple-je
care; nursing; tending
also: maintenance; upkeep (of objects or nature)
Hun arbejder inden for ældrepleje.
She works in elderly care.
Planten kræver omhyggelig pleje.
The plant requires careful tending.

P
plejehjem et noun common
PLAYEHYEM · ple-je-hjem
nursing home; care home
Min mor har arbejdet på et plejehjem.
My mom worked in a retirement home.
pludselig adjective common
PLUDSELEE · plud-se-lig
sudden
also: overnight; suddenly, all of a sudden
Han kom pludselig.
He came suddenly.
Tom frøs pludselig.
Tom suddenly felt cold.
point et noun common
[pʰoˈɛŋ] · POINT · po-int
a point in a game
Det er faktisk en god pointe.
"That's actually a good point."
politi et noun core
/poliˈtiː/ · POLITI · po-li-ti
police
Hjælp! Politi! Hjælp!
Help! Police! Help!
Det algeriske politi arresterede ham for ulovligt at krydse grænsen.
The Algerian police arrested him for illegally crossing the border.
P
politik en noun common
[pʰoliˈtˢiɡ̊] · POLITIK · po-li-tik
politics
also: a policy (course or principle of action adopted or proposed by an organisation)
Lad os tale politik!
Let's talk politics!
Politik har ikke min interesse.
I'm not interested in politics.
politiker en noun general
[pʰoˈlid̥iɡ̊ɒ] · POLITIKER · po-li-ti-ker
politician
Tom er politiker.
Tom is a politician.
Du taler som en politiker.
You talk like a politician.
politisk adjective common
[pʰoˈlid̥isɡ̊] · POLITISK · po-li-tisk
political
Status quo er en politisk dagsorden.
The status quo is a political agenda.
Fra og med 1950 begynder de europæiske lande indenfor rammerne af Det Europæiske Kul- og Stålfællesskab at nærme sig hinanden økonomisk og politisk for at sikre varig fred.
As of 1950, the European Coal and Steel Community began to unite European countries economically and politically in order to secure lasting peace.
populær adjective general
POPULEHR · po-pu-lær
popular, liked by many people
also: popular, prevalent among the people
Bob er populær i skolen.
Bob is popular at school.
Han er en populær japansk popstar.
He's a famous Japanese popstar.

Ordbog DA→EN position – primært 150
P
position en noun general
POSITION · po-si-tion
position; role; stance; standing
De fleste mennesker baserer deres politiske position på følelser fremfor beviser.
Most people base their political positions on feelings rather than evidence.
Takket være en ekstraordinær kvalitetstilgang har virksomheden opbygget et brandimage, der sikrer det en position på internationale markeder.
Thanks to an outstanding quality approach, the company has built up a brand image that ensures its position in international markets.
positiv et noun common
POSITIW · po-si-tiv
positive
Jeg er hiv-positiv.
I am HIV-positive.
Tom er blevet testet positiv for marihuana.
Tom has tested positive for marijuana.
post en noun general
POST
post position, job
Der er post til dig.
You've got mail.
Der er ingen post i postkassen.
There's no mail in the mailbox.
praksis en noun general
[ˈpʰʁ̥ɑɡ̊sis] · PRAKSIS · prak-sis
practice
En ting viser først sin værdi i praksis.
The proof of the pudding is in the eating.
I teorien er der ingen forskel mellem teori og praksis, men i praksis er der.
In theory, there is no difference between theory and practice. But, in practice, there is.
P
premierminister en noun general
PREMIERMINISTER · pre-mi-er-mi-ni-ster
prime minister
Premierministeren er leder af regeringen.
The prime minister is the head of the government.
Premierministeren besluttede at nedlukke Algier.
The prime minister decided to lock down Algiers.
politics
pres et noun common
PRES
pressure; stress
Der er pres på hornhinden fra indersiden af øjet.
There is pressure on the cornea from the inside of the eye.
Jeg har været under et stort pres på det seneste.
I have been under a lot of pressure lately.
presse en noun general
[ˈpʰʁ̥asə] · PRESSE · pres-se
a press (a machine for pressing things, like fruits, cloths and books)
also: a press (a machine for printing); the press (printed media and journalism); to press, squeeze
En fri presse er essentiel for demokratiet.
A free press is essential for democracy.
Jeg kan ikke presse denne appelsin. Den er tør.
I can't squeeze this orange. It's dry.
culture
primært adjective common
PRIMEHRT · pri-mært
primarily
Hans problem var primært psykisk.
His trouble was chiefly mental.

/priːs/ · PRIS

also: fare; cost; prize
Pris Herren!

privat adjective general
[pʁiˈvæˀd] · PRIVAT · pri-vat
private (not done in the view of others)
also: privately owned, privately held (not public)
Jeg er privat sproglærer.
I'm a private language teacher.

I'm a private language teacher.

problem
I er et problem.
You guys are a problem.
Du er et problem.
You're a problem.

PROBLEMATIK · pro-ble-ma-tik
problematic
Det er jo den samme problematik.

procent en noun core
[pʁ̥oˈsɛnˀd̥] · PROCENT · pro-cent

Hundrede procent.
One hundred percent.

PROCES · pro-ces

Det er en vanskelig proces.

/prodjuːsər/ · PROTHUCER · pro-du-cer
producer (one who produces an artistic production)
Galdeblæren producerer galde.
The gallbladder produces bile.
Algeriet producerer byggematerialer.
Algeria produces building materials.
producere verb general
/produseːrə/ · PROTHUCERE · pro-du-ce-re

also: To manufacture, to make.

They can make the same product much more cheaply.

P
produkt et noun rare
PROTHUKT · pro-dukt
product
I den seneste tid er efterspørgslen efter dette produkt steget hurtigere end udbuddet.
Recently the demand for this product has increased faster than the supply.
produktion en noun general
PROTHUKTION · pro-duk-tion
production
Fabrikken starter produktion næste år.
The factory will begin to produce next year.
USA's produktion af skifergas er en af de værste igangværende økologiske katastrofer.
United States shale gas production is one of the worst ongoing ecological disasters.
professionel adjective general
PROFESSIONEL · pro-fes-sio-nel
professional; skilled; expert
Mary er professionel poledancer.
Mary is a professional pole dancer.
Tom er professionel hockeyspiller.
Tom is a professional hockey player.
professor en noun common
PROFESSOR · pro-fes-sor
professor
Hvem er din professor?
Who is your professor?
Professor White udgav sin første bog sidste år.
Professor White published his first book last year.
P
program et noun general
PROGRAM · pro-gram
programme; program; schedule; agenda
Det program sendes hver anden uge.
That program is broadcast every other week.
Dette program sendes hver anden uge.
This program is broadcast every other week.
projekt et noun common
PROYEKT · pro-jekt
project
Vores projekt fejlede.
Our project failed.
Det er et formidabelt projekt.
It is a stupendous project.
præcis adjective common
/prɛˈsiːˀs/ · PREHCIS · præ-cis
precise, exact
also: precisely, specifically; exactly
Klokken er præcis ti.
It's ten o'clock sharp.
Tom kom præcis kl. 6.
Tom came at six sharp.
præge verb rare
[ˈpʁɛːjə] · PREHYE · præ-ge
to characterize, distinguish
also: to affect, alter (give something a certain trait); to imprint, emboss
1950’erne er præget af en kold krig mellem Øst og Vest.
The 1950s are characterized through a cold war between east and west.

præsentere verb general
PREHSENTERE · præ-sen-te-re
to present
Lad mig præsentere mig selv.
Let me introduce myself.
Lad mig præsentere mine forældre for dig.
Let me introduce my parents to you.
præsident en noun common
PREHSITHENT · præ-si-dent
president
also: chairman
Vi har en præsident.
We have a president.
Han var en sjov præsident.
He was a funny president.
prøve en noun common
/prøːvə/ · PRURVE · prø-ve
trial, test, examination
also: ordeal; rehearsal, audition; sample, specimen
Vil du prøve?
Do you want to try?
Lad os prøve!
Let's try!

psykisk adjective general
/sykisk/ · PSEWKISK · psy-kisk
mental
also: psychological; mentally; psychologically
Er du psykisk syg?
Are you mentally ill?
Har du et psykisk problem?
Do you have a mental problem?
P
publikum et noun common
PUBLIKUM · pu-bli-kum
audience
also: the public
Publikum holdt op med at klappe.
The audience stopped clapping.
Publikum var hovedsagelig kvinder og børn.
The crowd was mostly women and children.
punkt et noun general
/poŋˀt/ · PUNKT
dot
also: point (in space, on some scale or in some mathematical space)
Det er et lidt ømt punkt.
It's a bit of a sore point.
Byens højeste punkt ligger 592 meter over havniveau.
The city's highest point is 592 metres above sea level.
pædagog en noun rare
[pʰɛd̥aˈɡ̊oˀ] · PEHDAGOG · pæ-da-gog
A kindergarten teacher; a pedagogue
Og for os pædagoger.
And for us educators.
drink education culture
pænt adjective general
PEHNT
fairly, pretty; to some extent
Det er ikke pænt at pege.
It is bad manners to point at people.
Han fandt mig et pænt slips.
He found me a nice tie.

ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPÅBQRSTUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN påbud – påvirke 154
P
påbud et noun general
PAWBUD · på-bud
an injunction; a command to commit some action
De blev påbudt at lukke.
They were ordered to close.
pågældende adjective general
PAWGEHLDENDE · på-gæl-den-de
in question
Den pågældende er programmør.
They are a programmer.
Den pågældende er hverken en mand eller en kvinde.
They are neither a man nor a woman.
påpege verb rare
PAWPEYE · på-pe-ge
to point out, highlight
Nej, har nogle historikere påpeget.
No, some historians have pointed out.
pårørende en noun common
PAWRURRENDE · på-rø-ren-de
next of kin; person who is close (e.g. as a spouse, relative, close friend) to someone who has fallen ill, been in an accident, died etc.
De pårørende er underrettet.
The next of kin have been notified.
health social family
P
påske et noun general
[ˈpʰɔːsɡ̊ə] · PAWSKE · på-ske
Passover
also: Easter (Christian holy day)
God påske!
Happy Easter!
Glædelig påske!
Happy Easter!
påstand en noun rare
PAWSTAND · på-stand
assertion, claim, contention
also: allegation; claim
En påstand er ikke et bevis.
An assertion isn't a proof.
påstå verb rare
PAWSTAW · på-stå
to claim
also: to postulate; to claim without good grounds
Tom påstår, at han ikke slog nogen ihjel.
"Tom claims that he didn't kill anybody."
Tom påstår at hans retssag var manipuleret.
Tom insists his trial was rigged.
påvirke verb general
PAWVIRKE · på-vir-ke
to affect, to influence
Drikkevand med syreregn påvirker helbredet.
Acid rain in drinking water affects human health.
Det er nok det, der påvirkede deres beslutning.
That was probably what influenced their decision.
Danish–English Dictionary  ·  Tatoeba (CC-BY)  ·  Leipzig (CC-BY) enetverbadjadvother DA → EN
ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRACSTUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN racisme – redde 155
R
racisme en noun general
RACISME · ra-cis-me
racism; racial discrimination
Stalinisme er racisme.
Stalinism is racism.
Jeg ved at du var offer for racisme da du var barn.
I know that you were a victim of racism when you were a kid.
radikal et noun rare
/radikaːl/ · RATHIKAL · ra-di-kal
radical
"Termen \"TERF\", som står for \"transekskluderende radikalfeminist\", blev introduceret af en ciskønnet radikalfeminist."
"The term \"TERF,\" which stands for \"trans-exclusionary radical feminist,\" was coined by a cis radical feminist."
ramme en noun common
RAMME · ram-me
a frame
also: to affect; to hit (affect adversely); to frame
Jeg så flyet ramme bygningen.
I saw the plane hit the building.
Du var lige ved at ramme mig.
You almost hit me.
rapport en noun common
RAPPORT · rap-port
a report (information describing events)
Har du set den officielle rapport?
Have you seen the official report?
Jeg vil have dig til at sende denne rapport til Tom.
I want you to send this report to Tom.
R
rar adjective rare
/raːr/ · RAR
pleasant
also: kind; nice; rare, scarce
Du er både køn og rar.
You are both pretty and kind.
rask adjective rare
RASK
healthy
also: vigorous, energetic; quick
Jeg er blevet rask igen.
I have recovered again.
Tom blev aldrig helt rask igen.
Tom never fully recovered.
health
reaktion en noun general
REAKTION · re-ak-tion
a reaction
Hun vidste at hendes reaktion havde været dum.
She knew her reaction had been stupid.
Hans reaktion var ikke overraskende overhovedet.
His reaction wasn't surprising at all.
redde verb common
/reðə/ · REDDE · red-de
to save, rescue
Kan du redde Tom?
Can you save Tom?
Kan vi redde Tom?
Can we save Tom?

ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQREDSTUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN redskab – regn 156
R
redskab et noun rare
REDSKAB · red-skab
tool
Interlingua er et moderne redskab for international kommunikation.
Interlingua is a modern tool for international communication.
reducere verb general
RETHUCERE · re-du-ce-re
to reduce, lessen
also: to reduce the score and thereby closer to the opponent; make (something) more concentrated by letting it boil so that some of the liquid evaporates; to reduce fractions
Kan du reducere prisen en smule?
Could you reduce the price a little?
reel en noun general
/rɛɛl/ · REEL · re-el
real, proper
also: reliable, trustworthy, honest (about a person); real (being a real number)
Islamisk terrorisme er en reel trussel mod Algeriet.
Islamic terrorism is a real threat to Algeria.
Hvad skulle have været en målrettet militær operation, endte med at være en reel nedslagtning af civile.
What was supposed to be a targeted military operation deteriorated into wholesale slaughter of civilians.
regel en noun general
[ˈʁɛjˀəl] · REYEL · re-gel
rule
also: regulation; precept
Det er en dum regel.
It's a stupid rule.
Vi bør ændre den regel.
We should change that rule.
R
regering en noun common
REYERING · re-ge-ring
government; the executive body of a country's governmental system
De dannede en ny regering.
They formed a new government.
De har valgt en ny regering.
They have elected a new government.
region en noun core
REYION · re-gion
region
Kabylien er en meget smuk region beliggende i det nordlige Algeriet.
Kabylie is a very beautiful region located in northern Algeria.
registrere verb rare
REYISTRERE · re-gi-stre-re
to record
also: to register
Du bedes registrere dit kæledyr.
You must register your pet.
regn en noun common
[ˈʁɑɪ̯ˀn] · REN
rain
Der er regn på vej.
It is going to rain soon.
Det ser ud til regn.
It looks like rain.
weather

ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQREGSTUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN regne – repræsentant 157
R
regne verb general
/ˈrɑjnə/ · RENE · reg-ne
to rain
also: solve, work out; to estimate; to consider
Det vil snart regne.
It is going to rain soon.
I kan regne med mig.
You can count on me.
weather
rejse en noun common
[ˈʁɑjsə] · RAYSE · rej-se
journey
also: trip; voyage, passage, crossing; tour, cruise
God rejse!
Bon voyage!
rekord en noun general
REKORD · re-kord
a record (best performance or most remarkable event of its kind)
I går samlede vi næsten et halvt kilo glasskår på stranden indenfor en time, hvilket var ny rekord.
Yesterday we collected nearly half a kilo of pieces of broken glass on the beach within an hour, which was a record result.
I går samlede vi inden for en time næsten et halvt kilo glasskår på stranden, hvilket var ny rekord.
Yesterday we collected nearly half a kilo of pieces of broken glass on the beach within an hour, which was a record result.
rektor en noun general
/rɛktɔr/ · REKTOR · rek-tor
headmaster or headmistress, head teacher , principal
also: rector
Hun blev sendt til rektor.
She was sent to the principal's office.
body drink education
R
relation en noun general
RELATION · re-la-tion
relationship
Relationen går også den anden vej.
The relationship also goes the other way.
relativt adjective general
RELATIVT · re-la-tivt
relatively
Priserne på japanske biler er stadig relativt høje.
The prices of Japanese automobiles are still comparatively high.
ren en noun general
/reːˀn/ · REN
reindeer
also: a strip of unplowed land serving as a boundary between estates; clean; pure, mere, sheer
Tom er ren.
Tom is clean.
Jeg er ren.
I'm clean.
repræsentant en noun rare
REPREHSENTANT · re-præ-sen-tant
representative
Hun er formand for Repræsentanternes Hus.
She is the Speaker of the House of Representatives.

R
respekt en noun general
RESPEKT
respect
Respekt!
Respect!
Behandl hende med respekt.
Treat her with respect.
respirator en noun general
RESPIRATOR · re-spira-tor
respirator
"Skal jeg så i respirator?"
“Should I go on a ventilator?”
ressource et noun rare
/rɛsursə/ · RESSOURCE · res-sour-ce
resource
Vi har begrænsede ressourcer.
We have limited resources.
Verdens ressourcer er begrænsede.
"The world's resources are limited."
rest en noun rare
[ˈʁasd̥] · REST
remnant, remainder,
also: scraps of food; residue, remainder
"Jeg kan ta' resten."
I can take the rest.
Jeg kan tage resten.
I can take the rest.
R
restaurant en noun general
[ʁɛsd̥oˈʁɑŋ] · RESTAURANT · re-stau-rant
restaurant
Kan du anbefale en god restaurant?
Can you recommend a good restaurant?
Tom arbejder i en lokal restaurant.
Tom works at a local restaurant.
restriktion en noun rare
RESTRIKTION · re-strik-tion
restriction
Der er dog restriktioner.
However, there are restrictions.
resultat et noun common
RESULTAT · re-sul-tat
result
Dette er et godt resultat.
This is a good outcome.
Det er et fantastisk resultat.
This is an amazing result.
ret en noun common
/rɛt/ · RET
right; correctness; justice
also: dish; course (of a meal); law; court; legal case
Du har ret.
You are right.
Fiskeretten er husets specialitet.
The fish dish is the house speciality.

Ordbog DA→EN ret – ringe 159
R
ret adjective common
/rɛt/ · RET
right; correct; proper
also: straight; direct; true; genuine (den rette person = the right person)
Det er det rette svar.
That is the right answer.
Gå lige frem og drej til højre — det er den rette vej.
Go straight ahead and turn right — that is the correct way.
education
retning en noun common
/rɛtneŋ/ · RETNING · ret-ning
direction
also: movement, school; correction, marking, grading
I hvilken retning er han gået?
Which direction did he go?
I hvilken retning er han taget hen?
Which direction did he go?
retningslinje et noun rare
RETNINGSLINYE · ret-nings-linje
a guideline
Læs retningslinjerne her.
Read the guidelines here.
rette verb common
/rɛtə/ · RETTE · ret-te
to straighten, adjust
also: to aim, direct, turn, address; to correct, rectify; to mark, grade
Hold op med at rette mig!
Stop correcting me.
Tom har den rette indstilling.
Tom has the right attitude.
R
rettighed en noun rare
[ˈʁadiˌheðˀ] · RETTIGHETH · ret-tig-hed
rights, entitlements, liberties
Ingen har den rettighed.
No one has that right.
rigtig adjective core
/ˈrekti/ · RIGTEE · rig-tig
right
also: proper; correct; true
Tom er rigtig sej.
Tom is really cool.
Er udtalen rigtig?
Is the pronunciation correct?
ring en noun rare
/renɡ/ · RING
circle
also: halo; hoop; coil
Ring mig op.
Ring me up.
ringe verb common
RINYE · rin-ge
To call, to telephone
also: of (rather) limited size, quantity or extent etc.; of limited or insufficient value, quality, effect or importance etc.
Tom vil ringe.
Tom'll call.
Skal jeg ringe 112?
Should I call 112?
technology

ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRISSTUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN risikere – rundt 160
R
risikere verb general
RISIKERE · ri-si-ke-re
to risk
Når man kører over rødt, risikerer man at forårsage en ulykke.
When somebody runs a red light, they risk causing an accident.
Den som kæmper, risikerer at tabe. Den som ikke kæmper, har allerede tabt.
"He who fights may lose, but he who doesn't has already lost."
risiko en noun common
RISIKO · ri-si-ko
A risk
Den risiko tager jeg.
I'll take that risk.
Hun tog en stor risiko.
She took a big risk.
rolig adjective rare
ROLEE · ro-lig
calm, quiet
Vær rolig!
Be still.
Tom var rolig og afslappet.
Tom was calm and relaxed.
rolle et noun common
ROLLE · rol-le
a role, character, part
also: a role
Det spiller ingen rolle hvilket hold der vinder kampen.
It doesn't matter which team wins the game.
I dag spiller tysk ikke længere en rolle i videnskaben.
German doesn't play a role in science anymore today.
culture
R
rum et noun common
/romˀ/ · RUM
room
also: compartment; space; wide, spacious
Hold jeres rum rent.
Keep your room clean.
Mal dette rum med en pensel.
Paint this room with a brush.
home
rumme verb rare
RUMME · rum-me
to contain
also: to harbor or grasp (feelings, thoughts)
Bussen kan rumme halvtreds personer.
The bus takes fifty people.
runde en noun common
[ˈʁɔnd̥ə] · RUNDE · run-de
a round
also: a lap; a heat; a circuit
Jim blev talt ud i 7. runde.
Jim was counted out in the seventh round.
rundt adjective core
[ˈʁɔnˀd̥] · RUNDT
round, around
Fjol ikke rundt!
Don't mess around.
Tom fjollede rundt.
Tom was goofing around.

russisk adjective rare
/rusisk/ · RUSSISK · rus-sisk
Russian (of or pertaining to Russia)
also: Russian (the Russian language)
Han taler russisk.
He speaks Russian.
Hun taler russisk.
She speaks Russian.
rute en noun general
RUTE · ru-te
route; road; itinerary
Dette er den korteste rute til Paris.
This is the shortest route to Paris.
ryg en noun rare
[ˈʁœɡ̊] · REWG
back
also: spine; ridge
Ryg og rejs!
Get lost!
Jeg lå på min ryg.
I was lying on my back.
body
ryge verb general
/ryːə/ · REWYE · ry-ge
to smoke
also: to go, fall
Du kan ryge her.
You can smoke here.
Stop med at ryge.
Stop smoking.
R
rykke verb general
REWKKE · ryk-ke
to jerk, pull, tug
also: rykke ind: to move in ; to insert
Hunden satte efter et egern og rykkede hårdt i snoren.
The dog took off after a squirrel and yanked hard on the leash.
række en noun core
[ˈʁaɡ̊ə] · REHKKE · ræk-ke
row, line, rank; things arranged spatially
also: series; series; succession; sequence; things or events arranged in order of time; rank
Stå på række ved døren.
Line up at the door.
Vi blev stillet på række.
We were made to stand in line.
rød adjective general
[ˈʁœˀð] · RURD
red
also: red ,
Er den rød?
Is it red?
Er vinen rød?
Is the wine red?
røg en noun general
/røj/ · RURG
smoke
Tom røg.
Tom was smoking.
Hun sad og røg.
She sat and smoked.

rådgive verb rare
[ˈʁʌðˌɡiˀ] · RAWDYIVE · rå-d-gi-ve
to advise, counsel, guide
Tom er finansiel rådgiver.
Tom is a financial advisor.
rådgivning en noun general
[ˈʁʌðˌɡiwˀneŋ] · RAWDYIVNING · rå-d-giv-ning
advice
also: counselling
De får rådgivning og hjælp.
They get advice and help.
rådhus et noun rare
RAWDHUSE · rå-d-hu-se
town hall; city hall
Jeg har en ven på rådhuset.
I have a friend at City Hall.
Rådhuset ligger ikke langt herfra.
"The city hall isn't far from here."
rådighed en noun general
RAWDIGHETH · rå-dig-hed
presence of mind, resourcefulness (the ability to control something or someone)
Min bil står til din rådighed.
My car is at your disposal.
S
sag en noun common
/saːɡ/ · SAG
matter, affair
also: cause (grand mission); thing; case, lawsuit
Det er min sag.
That's my business.
Det er ingen sag.
It's a snap.
sagtens adverb general
SAGTENS · sag-tens
easily, with no difficulty
Jeg kan sagtens slå dig.
I can easily beat you.
salg et noun common
/salˀɣ/ · SALG
sale
Til salg.
For sale.
Er det til salg?
Is that for sale?
samarbejde et noun common
SAMARBAYDE · sam-ar-bej-de
cooperation
also: collaboration; partnership; cooperate
Tom ville ikke samarbejde.
Tom wouldn't cooperate.
På forhånd tak for dit samarbejde.
Thank you for your cooperation in advance.

S
samfund et noun common
/samfonˀ/ · SAMFUND · sam-fund
society
also: society, community; social intercourse
Vi bor i et berbisktalende samfund.
We live in a Berber-speaking society.
Vi lever i et berbisktalende samfund.
We live in a Berber-speaking society.
samle verb common
/samlə/ · SAMLE · sam-le
to collect
also: to gather; to assemble; to attract
Min hobby er at samle på mønter.
My hobby is collecting coins.
Min hobby er at samle på frimærker.
My hobby is collecting stamps.
samme adjective core
/samə/ · SAMME · sam-me
same
also: equal; said
Vi vil det samme.
We want the same thing.
Vi er i samme båd.
We're in the same boat.
sammen adverb core
SAMMEN · sam-men
together
Kom sammen!
Come together!
Bliv sammen!
Stay together.
social
S
sammenhæng en noun general
[ˈsɑmənˌhɛŋˀ] · SAMMENHEHNG · sam-men-hæng
connection
also: context
Det afhænger af sammenhængen.
It depends on the context.
sammenligne verb rare
[ˈsɑmənˌliˀnə] · SAMMENLINE · sam-men-lig-ne
to compare
Jeg sammenlignede min bil med hans.
I compared my car with his.
"Du sammenligner æbler og appelsiner!"
"You're comparing apples and oranges!"
sammenligning en noun general
[ˈsɑmənˌliˀneŋ] · SAMMENLINING · sam-men-lig-ning
a comparison
Til sammenligning kan lava fra vulkanudbrud være alt mellem 700 °C og 1.200 °C.
For comparison, lava from volcano eruptions can be anywhere between 700 and 1,200°C.
family
samråd et noun general
SAMRAWD · sam-råd
consultation
Samrådet begynder klokken 12.
The consultation will begin at 12 noon.

ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSAMTUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN samt – savn 164
S
samt conjunction core
/samt/ · SAMT
and, and also, plus, as well as
also: med samt – together with
Den kinesiske dyrekreds' tolv dyr kommer fra elleve dyrearter som findes i naturen: rotten, oksen, tigeren, haren, slangen, hesten, vædderen, aben, hanen, hunden og svinet samt dragen fra mytologien; de bruges som kalender.
The twelve animals of the Chinese Zodiac come from eleven types of animals we find in nature: the rat, the ox, the tiger, the hare, the snake, the horse, the ram, the monkey, the rooster, the dog, the pig, and the mythological dragon; they're used as a calendar.
samtale en noun general
/sɑmtæːlə/ · SAMTALE · sam-ta-le
conversation, talk, discourse
also: dialog, dialogue; interview; call (telephone conversation)
Han kan ikke føre en samtale.
He can't hold a conversation.
Vores samtale varede i timevis.
Our conversation lasted for hours.
education social
samtidig adjective core
/samtiːdi/ · SAMTITHEE · sam-ti-dig
contemporary, from the same time
also: simultaneous; synchronous; also, in addition, furthermore
Alle kirkeklokkerne begyndte at ringe samtidig.
All the church bells started ringing at the same time.
Brug ikke strygejernet, elkedlen og komfuret samtidig.
Don't use the iron, electric kettle, and electric stove at the same time.
time
samtlige adjective common
SAMTLIYE · samt-li-ge
all
Ja, og samtlige taler skal ind-
Yes, and all speeches must be
S
sandsynlighed en noun general
SANDSEWNLIGHETH · sand-syn-lig-hed
likelihood, probability
I morgen vil det med stor sandsynlighed regne.
Tomorrow there's a high probability it will rain.
Hvis man oversætter fra sit andetsprog til sit modersmål, i stedet for omvendt, er der mindre sandsynlighed for at man laver fejl.
If you translate from your second language into your own native language, rather than the other way around, you're less likely to make mistakes.
sandsynligvis adverb general
SANDSEWNLIGVIS · sand-syn-lig-vis
likely
Jeg taber sandsynligvis.
I'll probably lose.
Tom vinder sandsynligvis.
Tom will probably win.
sang en noun general
[sɑŋˀ] · SANG
song
also: singing
Jeg sang.
I was singing.
Fuglene sang.
The birds sang.
family
savn et noun rare
/savˀn/ · SAVN
need, loss, privation
also: want, lack
"Savner du mig?"
"Do you miss me?"
Jeg savner dig.
I miss you.

ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSAVTUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN savne – seksuel 165
S
savne verb rare
/savnə/ · SAVNE · sav-ne
to miss
also: to want
Vi vil alle savne dig, Tom.
We'll all miss you, Tom.
Vi kommer til at savne hende.
We'll miss her.
scene et noun general
/seːnə/ · SCENE · sce-ne
stage
Aldrig har jeg set sådan en smuk scene.
Never have I seen such a beautiful scene.
Merkel har stået i spidsen for Europas største økonomi i de sidste 16 år og har spillet en nøglerolle i Europa og på den globale scene.
Merkel has led Europe’s biggest economy for the past 16 years and has played a major role in Europe and on the global stage.
score et noun general
/skoːrə/ · SCORE · sco-re
A , a number of points earned.
also: a goal/point; land; steal
Tom scorede et verdensklassemål.
Tom scored a worldie.
se verb core
[ˈseˀ] · SE
to see
also: to look; to watch; to understand
Se, se!
Look, look!
Se der!
Look there.
S
sejle verb general
[ˈsɑjlə] · SAYLE · sej-le
to sail (to ride in a boat, especially sailboat)
Mit hus sejler.
My house is in disarray.
"Det skib er sejlet!"
"That ship has sailed!"
transport
sejr en noun common
/sajˀr/ · SAYR
victory
also: triumph; win
Det var en moralsk sejr.
It was a moral victory.
Træneren førte sit hold til sejr.
The coach steered his team to victory.
seks adjective core
/sɛks/ · SEKS
six
Vi har seks æg.
We have six eggs.
Klokken er næsten seks.
It's almost six o'clock.
seksuel adjective rare
/sɛksuɛl/ · SEKSUEL · seksu-el
sexual
Han var udsat for seksuel chikane.
He was sexually harassed.
Sami blev udsat for seksuel chikane.
Sami was sexually harassed.

S
sekund et noun rare
SEKUND · se-kund
second (SI unit of time, unit of angular measure, short, indeterminate amount of time)
also: second; seconde
Har du et sekund?
Can I have a second?
Jeg kunne ikke vente et sekund mere.
I couldn't wait another second.
time
selskab et noun common
[ˈsɛlˌsɡ̊æˀb̥] · SELSKAB · sel-skab
company, firm
also: social gathering; company, companionship
Han holdt mig med selskab.
He kept me company.
Jeg sætter virkelig pris på dit selskab.
I really appreciate your company.
selve adjective common
SELVE · sel-ve
very, itself, herself, himself
Jeg er selverhvervende.
"I'm self-employed."
selvfølgelig adjective common
[sɛlˈføljəli] · SELVFURLYELEE · selv-føl-ge-lig
natural, inevitable
also: of course, naturally, sure
Selvfølgelig.
Certainly.
Selvfølgelig!
Of course!
S
selvom conjunction common
SELVOM · selv-om
selv om
Selvom han er klog, er han ikke vis.
Though he is clever, he isn't wise.
Han spiller golf, selvom det regner.
He'll play golf even if it rains.
selvstændig adjective general
SELVSTEHNDEE · selv-stæn-dig
independent
also: entrepreneur, self-employed; independently
Jeg er selvstændig.
I'm self-employed.
sen adjective rare
[ˈseˀn] · SEN
late
also: belated, tardy; slow
Send Tom op.
Send Tom up.
Send Tom væk.
Send Tom away.
sende verb common
[ˈsɛnə] · SENDE · sen-de
to send
Jeg vil gerne sende en fax.
I'd like to send a fax.
Vi vil sende en eller anden.
We'll send somebody.

ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSENTUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN senere – side 167
S
senere adjective core
[ˈseːnɐɐ] · SENERE · se-ne-re
later
Vi ses senere!
See you later!
Gør det senere!
Do it later!
senest adjective common
SENEST · se-ne-st
supd
Jeg vil være hjemme senest 2:30.
I'll be home by 2:30.
Jeg vil være hjemme senest 14:30.
I'll be home by 2:30.
seng en noun general
/ˈsɛnɡ/ · SENG
bed
Red din seng!
Make your bed.
Tom er i seng.
Tom is in bed.
sent adjective common
SENT
sen
For sent, Tom.
Too late, Tom.
Er det for sent?
Is that too late?
S
september en noun common
/səptɛmbər/ · SEPTEMBER · sep-tem-ber
September , abbreviated sept.
Til september bliver jeg seksten.
I'll be sixteen in September.
I den forgangne september var hun i Paris.
Last September she was in Paris.
sexisme et noun general
SEXISME · se-xis-me
sexism
For alt er jo sexisme.
Because everything is sexism.
sidde verb common
[ˈseðə] · SIDDE · sid-de
to sit
also: to be; to fit; to sit up
Tom vil sidde bagi.
Tom wants to sit in the back.
Lad os sidde udenfor!
Let's sit outside.
side et noun common
/ˈsiːdə/ · SITHE · si-de
side; a bounding straight edge or surface
also: side; a region in a specified position with respect to something.; viewpoint an opinion or attitude; side; one group of competitors in a game or a war.
Denne side op!
This side up!
Hvis side er du på?
Whose side are you on?
body

ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSIDTUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN siden – sikkerhed 168
S
siden adverb core
/sidən/ · SITHEN · si-den
since, later (on), afterwards, in the time since then
also: since, ago (referring to the length of time between some past event and the (narrative) present); ago (referring to a point in time a definite amount of time in the past); since
Siden hvornår?
Since when?
Han bor ved siden af.
He lives next door to us.
time
sidst adjective common
SIDST
last
Hvor så du den sidst?
Where did you see it last?
Tom stod sidst i køen.
Tom was standing at the end of the line.
sidste adjective core
/sistə/ · SIDSTE · sid-ste
last
also: latest; latter; past
De kom sidste uge.
They came last week.
Tom døde sidste år.
Tom died last year.
sige verb core
[ˈsiːə] · SIYE · si-ge
to say
also: to tell; to mean; to be said, to be told
"Aha", vil de sige.
'Aha', they will say.
Kan du sige det igen?
Can you repeat that?
S
sigt en noun general
/sekt/ · SIGT
sight, visibility
also: view, term
Sigt. Skyd!
Aim. Fire!
Sigt ikke på mig.
Don't aim at me.
sigte et noun rare
/sektə/ · SIGTE · sig-te
intention, goal, purpose
also: aim; sieve; to aim
En verdensøkonomisk opsving er endnu ikke i sigte.
Recovery of the world economy is not yet in sight.
Vi syntes at det lå i kortene for ham at sigte efter at blive læge.
We thought it was in the cards for him to aim to become a doctor.
sikker adjective common
[ˈseɡ̊ɐ] · SIKKER · sik-ker
safe, secure
also: sure, certain
Er du sikker?
Are you sure?
Jeg er sikker.
I'm certain.
sikkerhed en noun common
SIKKERHETH · sik-ker-hed
safety, security
also: certainty; security, guarantee (for a loan); confidence belief in one's own abilities
Er I i sikkerhed?
Are you safe?
Du er i sikkerhed.
You're safe.
Danish–English Dictionary  ·  Tatoeba (CC-BY)  ·  Leipzig (CC-BY) enetverbadjadvother DA → EN
ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSIKTUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN sikkert – skade 169
S
sikkert adjective common
SIKKERT · sik-kert
probably
also: no doubt; steadily; confidently
Helt sikkert!
Certainly!
Tom er sikkert bange.
Tom is certainly afraid.
sikre verb core
SIKRE · sik-re
to ensure, make sure
also: to guarantee; to secure; to protect
Er I sikre?
Are you sure?
Det er vi ret sikre på.
We're quite certain of that.
simpelthen adverb common
SIMPELTHEN · sim-pelt-hen
simply
Det er simpelthen fantastisk!
It's simply fantastic!
Jeg inviterede ham simpelthen.
I simply invited him.
situation en noun common
SITUATION · si-tu-a-tion
situation; circumstances
Det er en vanskelig situation.
This is a difficult situation.
Vi er alle i den samme situation.
We are all in the same situation.
S
sjov en noun general
/sjɔv/ · SYOW
fun
also: funny
Er jeg sjov?
Am I fun?
Du er en sjov fyr.
You're a funny guy.
sjælden adjective general
SYEHLDENT · sjæl-dent
rare; uncommon; infrequent
Tom ler sjældent.
Tom seldom laughs.
Det sker sjældent.
That rarely happens.
skabe verb common
/skaːbə/ · SKABE · ska-be
to create, make
also: to fuss, make a scene
Kan man skabe noget af intet?
Can we create something out of nothing?
Han ønsker ikke at skabe problemer.
He doesn't want to cause trouble.
food
skade en noun common
/skaːðə/ · SKATHE · ska-de
damage, harm
also: to harm, damage; to hurt, injure; skate
Kom nogen til skade?
Did anybody get hurt?
Det vil skade afgrøden.
It will damage the crops.
body

S
skaffe verb general
[ˈsɡ̥ɑfə] · SKAFFE · skaf-fe
to procure, to get
also: to serve up, eat
Kan du skaffe mig noget mælk?
Could you get me some milk?
Jeg kan skaffe dig en hest og en sadel.
I can get you a horse and saddle.
skal en noun rare
/skalˀ/
shell, valve
also: skin, peel, rind; skull
Aalborg Stift skal have ny domprovst.
skat en noun general
/skat/ · SKAT
treasure
also: ; honey, sweetie; tax
Ja, min skat.
Yes, my love.
Godmorgen skat!
Good morning, sweetheart.
ske en noun common
[sɡ̊eˀ] · SKE
be
also: happen, occur; take place; come about
Alt kan ske.
Anything might happen.
Det er en ske.
That is a spoon.
S
skib et noun rare
/ˈskiːp/ · SKIB
ship, boat, vessel
also: nave, aisle
Det skib er sejlet!
That ship has sailed!
Er Tom på dette skib?
Is Tom on this ship?
skidt adjective general
SKIDT
bad, mean
also: it is of no consequence, never mind; dirt; something undesirable; shit
Skidt med det.
Never mind.
Skidt med det!
It doesn't matter.
skifte verb general
SKIFTE · skif-te
to change
also: to exchange, swap; to distribute, divide, partition; to shift, switch
Hvor skal vi skifte?
Where have we got to change?
Jeg ønsker at skifte job.
I want to change jobs.
sko en noun general
/sɡoːˀ/ · SKO
shoe
also: to shoe
I sælger sko.
You sell shoes.
Du sælger sko.
You sell shoes.

S
skole et noun common
/skoːlə/ · SKOLE · sko-le
school
also: to school, train
Gå i skole!
Go to school.
Er Tom i skole?
Is Tom at school?
education
skov en noun general
[ˈsɡ̊ɒwˀ] · SKOW
forest
Vi solgte vores skov.
We sold our forest.
Vi har solgt vores skov.
We sold our forest.
nature
skridt et noun common
/skrit/ · SKRIDT
step, footstep, pace
also: crotch, crutch, groin; measure, move
Det er et skridt i den rigtige retning.
It's a step in the right direction.
Det første skridt er altid det sværeste.
The first step is always the hardest.
skrive verb common
/skriːvə/ · SKRIVE · skri-ve
to write
Lad os skrive en bog.
Let's write a book.
Kan I lide at skrive?
Do you guys like writing?
S
skrue et noun general
SKRUE
a screw or bolt
also: propeller; pasta; to screw
Denne skrue er løs.
This screw is loose.
Den fyr har en skrue løs!
That guy has a screw loose!
skud et noun common
[ˈsɡ̊uð] · SKUD
shot (launching of a projectile)
also: shoot (the emerging stem and embryonic leaves of a new plant)
Tom hørte et skud.
Tom heard a gunshot.
Fadil hørte et skud nummer to.
Fadil heard a second shot.
skulle verb rare
[ˈsɡ̊ulə]
shall; must; to be supposed to
also: should; would; ought to (hypothetical/conditional)
Aarhus Festuge skulle være gennemført fra 28. august til 6. september.
skyde verb general
[ˈsɡ̊yːðə] · SKEWDE · sky-de
to shoot
also: to push, to shove
Jeg vil skyde dig.
I'll shoot you.
Tom kom til at skyde sig selv i foden.
Tom accidentally shot himself in the foot.

ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSKYTUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN skyld – slem 172
S
skyld en noun common
SKEWLD
blame, guilt responsibility for wrongdoing
also: fault responsibility for a mistake; sake; debt
For guds skyld.
For heaven's sake.
For himlens skyld.
For heaven's sake.
skyldes verb common
SKEWLDES · skyl-des
to be due to; to be caused by; to be attributable to
Ulykker skyldes uagtsomhed.
Accidents arise from carelessness.
Hvad skyldes trafikproblemet?
What's the hold up?
skyldig adjective common
/skyldi/ · SKEWLDEE · skyl-dig
guilty
also: owed; due
Jeg er ikke skyldig.
I'm not guilty.
Føler du dig skyldig?
Do you feel guilty?
skære verb general
SKEHRE · skæ-re
to cut to make a cut with a knife or a sword
Toms læge foreslog ham at skære ned på sukkeret.
Tom's doctor suggested that he cut down on sugar.
Jeg bad slagteren om at skære al fedtet fra kødet.
I asked the butcher to trim all the fat off of the meat.
S
skærpe verb rare
SKEHRPE · skær-pe
to sharpen, to make something sharp
also: to make stricter or harsher
Han ved hvordan man skærper knive.
He knows how to sharpen knives.
slag et noun common
/slaːˀɣ/ · SLAG
A hit, punch
also: A beat; A battle; A game, a round
"Hvor slagtede du dem?"
"Where did you slaughter them?"
"Hvad slags orm er der?"
"What kind of worms are there?"
slags en noun common
/slaːˀɣs/ · SLAGS
kind, sort, type
Hvad slags orm er der?
What kind of worms are there?
Det er en slags orange.
It's a kind of orange.
slem adjective rare
[ˈslɛmˀ] · SLEM
bad, ill-behaved, mean
also: serious in a bad way, grievous
Du har været en slem hund.
You've been a bad dog.
En slem forkølelse har afholdt mig fra at studere i denne uge.
A bad cold has kept me from studying this week.
health

ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSLETUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN slet – slås 173
S
slet adjective common
[ˈslɛd̥] · SLET
bad
also: evil; plain; at all
Slet dem.
Erase them.
Slet dem!
Delete them.
slippe en noun general
SLIPPE · slip-pe
alley
Tom vil ikke slippe væk.
Tom won't get away.
De ville ikke slippe os ind.
They wouldn't let us in.
slukke verb general
[ˈslɔɡ̊ə] · SLUKKE · sluk-ke
to put out (extinguish)
also: to quench, slake; turn off (to power down); switch off (to turn a switch to the "off" position in order to stop or disable a device)
Gider du slukke for tv'et?
Would you mind turning the TV off?
Vær så venlig at slukke lyset.
Please turn off the light.
slut en noun common
/slut/ · SLUT
over
also: finished; over and out, out; end
Det er slut.
It's over.
Krigen er slut.
The war is over.
S
slutning en noun rare
SLUTNING · slut-ning
end, finale
also: conclusion; ending
Filmens slutning var for banal.
The movie's ending was too banal.
slutte verb rare
[ˈslud̥ə] · SLUTTE · slut-te
to end, to finish
also: to conclude; to close; to enter into, engage
Krigen sluttede.
The war ended.
Frikvarteret slutter.
Recess is ending.
slå en noun common
/slɔːˀ/ · SLAW
bolt
also: to beat, hit, knock; to mow; to strike, defeat
Slå dem ihjel.
Kill them.
Du bør slå ordet op.
You'd better look up the word.
slås verb general
/slɔs/ · SLAWS
to fight [each other]
Dette græs skal slås.
This grass needs cutting.
Alle begyndte at slås.
Everyone started fighting.

Ordbog DA→EN smide – snart 174
S
smide verb general
SMITHE · smi-de
to throw, toss
Han vil smide dem under bussen.
He will throw them under the bus.
Sami ønskede ikke at smide Layla under bussen.
Sami didn't want to throw Layla under the bus.
smil et noun general
/smiːl/ · SMIL
smile
Smil og lær!
Smile and learn!
Smil til kameraet.
Smile for the camera.
smitte en noun common
[ˈsmed̥ə] · SMITTE · smit-te
infection
also: to infect
Der er ingen nye smittede.
There are no new cases of infection.
Tom smittede mig med skoldkopper.
Tom gave me the chicken pox.
smuk adjective rare
/smok/ · SMUK
beautiful, handsome, good-looking
also: fine; noble
Du er smuk.
You're beautiful.
Er hun smuk?
Is she beautiful?
body
S
smule en noun common
SMULE · smu-le
a little bit of something (especially, but far from exclusively, of food)
Tom er en smule døv.
Tom is a bit deaf.
Jeg drikker en smule.
I drink a little.
food
småt adjective general
[ˈsmʌd̥] · SMAWT
scarcely; barely
Når Mary sms'er, skriver hun det hele med småt, og hun sætter sjældent tegn.
Mary only uses lowercase letters when she texts, and she rarely uses punctuation.
snak verb general
SNAK
to imp
Snak ikke udenom.
Don't beat about the bush.
Lad os få en snak.
Let's have a chat.
snart adverb common
[ˈsnɑˀt] · SNART
soon
also: shortly; presently
De kommer snart.
They are coming soon.
Snart er det jul.
Soon it'll be Christmas.

ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSOCTUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN social – sort 175
S
social adjective common
[soˈɕæˀl] · SOCIAL · so-ci-al
sociable
Kontinenter er en social konstruktion.
Continents are a social construct.
Befolkningen kræver social retfærdighed.
The country demands social justice.
sol en noun general
/soːl/ · SOL
sun
also: solution; note
Katten slikkede sol.
The cat sunned itself.
Det franske ord »soleil« betyder »sol«.
The French word "soleil" means "sun".
weather
soldat en noun rare
SOLDAT · sol-dat
soldier
Ziri er soldat.
Ziri is a soldier.
Læreren blev soldat.
The teacher became a soldier.
sommer en noun common
/ˈsɔmɐ/ · SOMMER · som-mer
summer
Én svale gør ingen sommer.
One swallow does not make a summer.
Hvor vil du hen til sommer?
Where do you want to go this summer?
S
sommerferie en noun general
SOMMERFERIE · som-mer-fe-rie
summer holiday; summer vacation
Jeg har haft en god sommerferie.
I had a good summer vacation.
Hvad har du lavet i din sommerferie?
How did you spend your summer holiday?
sommerhus et noun general
SOMMERHUS · som-mer-hus
summerhouse, holiday home
Han tilbragte det meste af tiden i sit sommerhus.
He spent most of the time at his summer home.
home time
sommetider adverb rare
SOMMETITHER · som-me-ti-der
sometimes, occasionally
Sommetider laver jeg fejl.
Sometimes I make mistakes.
Tom går sommetider med hat.
Tom sometimes wears a hat.
time
sort en noun common
[ˈsoɐ̯d̥] · SORT
black
also: under the table; done in secret so as to avoid taxation; under the table; secretly, so as to avoid taxation; , kind
Den var sort.
It was black.
Det var sort.
It was black.

Ordbog DA→EN spansk – spille 176
S
spansk adjective rare
SPANSK
Spanish; of, or pertaining to the Spanish language or Spain
also: Spanish; the Spanish language; the language as an area of study
Jeg taler spansk.
I speak Spanish.
Kan han tale spansk?
Can he speak Spanish?
health
spare verb general
/spɛːr/ · SPARE · spa-re
to save
also: to economize; to save up
Jeg forsøger at spare penge.
I'm trying to save money.
Lad os spare på vores begrænsede vandresurser.
Let's conserve our limited water resources.
speciel adjective general
SPECIELT · spe-ci-elt
special; particular; peculiar
Denne maskine er specielt bygget til at fungere i ekstremt vejr.
This machine is specially engineered to work in extreme weather.
Selv nu, mange år efter den kolde krig, er der fortsat meget bitterhed mellem tyskere og russere, specielt i områder som blev okkuperet af Sovjetunionen.
Even now, many years after the Cold War, there is still much bitterness between Germans and Russians, especially in areas which were occupied by the Soviet Union.
spids en noun rare
SPIDS
pointed, sharp
also: tapering; pinched; tart
Tom spidsede en blyant.
Tom sharpened a pencil.
Vulcanere har spidse ører.
Vulcans have pointy ears.
S
spil et noun common
SPIL
A game.
also: A box of equipment and such used for a game.; A specific part of a game, such as a round or level.; Conditions or limits of a game.
Sikke et spil!
What a game!
Livet står på spil.
Life is at stake.
spilde verb rare
[ˈsbilə] · SPILDE · spil-de
to spill
also: to waste (often recklessly)
Lad aldrig en krise gå til spilde.
Never let a crisis go to waste.
Lad aldrig en god krise gå til spilde.
Never let a good crisis go to waste.
health
spildevand et noun general
SPILDEVAND · spil-de-vand
wastewater
Spildevandskrigen er brudt ud.
The Wastewater War has broken out.
drink nature
spille verb common
/spelə/ · SPILLE · spil-le
to play
also: to gamble, play a game; to move quickly; to flicker, sparkle
Lad os spille poker.
Let's play poker.
Han kan spille tennis.
He can play tennis.

ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSPITUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN spiller – sprog 177
S
spiller en noun common
/spelər/ · SPILLER · spil-ler
player
also: gambler; gamer
Spiller du golf?
Do you play golf?
Tom spiller ikke.
Tom doesn't play.
spise et noun common
/spiːsə/ · SPISE · spi-se
food
also: dish; to eat
Ønsker I at spise?
Do you want to eat?
Jeg vil spise dig.
I want to eat you.
food
spor et noun common
/spoːr/ · SPOR
track
also: trail; scent; trace
Tom forsvandt uden at efterlade sig et spor.
Tom disappeared without leaving a trace.
Vi har nogle andre spor, vi skal følge op på.
We have some other leads to follow up.
sport en noun general
SPORT
sport
Kan hun lide sport?
Does she like sports?
Fodbold er en sport.
Soccer is a sport.
S
sprede verb general
[ˈsb̥ʁɛːðə] · SPRETHE · spre-de
to spread
Hold op med at sprede disse løgne!
Stop spreading these lies!
Tom var skyldig i at sprede løgne om Mary.
Tom was guilty of spreading lies about Mary.
spredning en noun general
SPREDNING · spred-ning
dispersion; the degree to which the elements of a set deviates from its mean
also: standard deviation
Håndvask anbefales for at forhindre spredning af smitsomme sygdomme såsom coronavirussen.
Hand washing is recommended to prevent the spread of contagious diseases like a coronavirus.
spredt adjective general
[ˈsb̥ʁɛˀd̥] · SPREDT
scattered
Virussen blev spredt af medierne.
The virus was spread by the media.
Tøj lå spredt ud over hele gulvet.
Clothes were strewn all over the floor.
sprog et noun general
/sprɔːˀɣ/ · SPROG
language
Jeg kan fire sprog.
I know four languages.
Han taler fem sprog.
He speaks five languages.
Danish–English Dictionary  ·  Tatoeba (CC-BY)  ·  Leipzig (CC-BY) enetverbadjadvother DA → EN
ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSPÆTUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN spændende – stak 178
S
spændende adjective common
/spɛnənə/ · SPEHNDENDE · spæn-den-de
exciting, thrilling
Fodbold er en spændende sport.
Soccer is an exciting sport.
Køreturen til søen var meget spændende.
The drive to the lake was very exciting.
health
spærre en noun rare
/spɛrə/ · SPEHRRE · spær-re
barrier
also: lock; bar; block
Den kvinde spærrede vejen for mig.
That woman blocked my way.
Området var spærret af med gult afspærringsbånd.
The area was cordoned off with yellow tape.
spørge verb general
[ˈsb̥ɶɐ̯wə] · SPURRYE · spør-ge
to ask, inquire
also: to find out about, to learn about
Lad os spørge nogen!
Let's ask someone!
Må jeg spørge hvorfor?
May I ask why?
spørgsmål et noun common
/ˈspœɐ̯sˌmɔˀl/ · SPURRGSMAWL · spørgs-mål
question
Godt spørgsmål!
Good question!
Godt spørgsmål.
Good question.
S
stadig adjective core
/staːdi/ · STATHEE · sta-dig
constant
also: settled; continuous; incessant
Er du stadig syg?
Are you still sick?
Sover Tom stadig?
Is Tom still sleeping?
stadigvæk adverb general
STATHIGVEHK · sta-dig-væk
still (in the same way as before)
Du er stadigvæk ung.
You're still young.
I er stadigvæk unge.
You're still young.
health
stadion et noun general
STATHION · sta-dion
a stadium (sporting venue)
"Tog du til stadionet?"
"Did you go to the stadium?"
stak en noun general
STAK
stack
Han stak af.
He did a runner.
Pigen stak af.
The girl ran away.

ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTATUVWXYZÆØÅ

S
stamme en noun rare
STAMME · stam-me
trunk, log (of a tree)
also: stem; tribe; stock
Tom begyndte at stamme.
Tom began to stutter.
Lad være med at stamme!
Don't stutter!
nature
stand en noun common
[ˈsd̥anˀ] · STAND
position, social status, station
also: class, rank; occupation, trade, profession; estate
Denne bil er i god stand.
This car is in good condition.
Jeg er i stand til at tale.
I'm able to speak.
social
standse verb rare
[ˈsd̥ansə] · STANDSE · stand-se
to stop, halt
also: to stop
Bilen standsede.
The automobile stopped.
Bilen standsede ikke.
"The car didn't stop."
start en noun common
START
start
Start nu.
Start now.
Start med enkle sætninger.
Start with simple sentences.
S
starte verb common
/startə/ · STARTE · star-te
to start
Lad os nu starte spillet.
Now let's begin the game.
Tom kunne ikke starte sin bil.
Tom wasn't able to start his car.
culture
stat en noun general
STAT
A state (polity).
I 2014 kan Skotland blive en uafhængig stat.
In 2014 Scotland may become an independent state.
Kurderne med anslået fyrre millioner mennesker er verdens største folkeslag uden egen stat.
The Kurds, numbering an estimated forty million people, are the world's largest nation without a state of their own.
station en noun common
[sd̥aˈɕoˀn] · STATION · sta-tion
station major stopping place for busses or trains
also: station a building which is the center for an institution, in particular a police station; station a company broadcasting radio or television
Jeg tager til Osaka station.
I am going to Osaka station.
Hvor er den nærmeste station?
Where is the nearest station?
statsminister en noun common
STATSMINISTER · stats-mi-ni-ster
a prime minister
Mit mål i livet er at blive statsminister.
My goal in life is to be Prime Minister.
politics

S
status en noun general
[ˈsdæːtus] · STATUS · sta-tus
a company's statements of assets and liabilities
Status quo er en politisk dagsorden.
The status quo is a political agenda.
Nok har en ronin ikke samme status som en daimyo, men han har den frihed, som den anden aldrig får.
A ronin may not have the status of a daimyo, but he has the freedom that the latter may never have.
sted et noun core
[ˈsd̥ɛð] · STED
place
also: spot; passage, text; homestead
Af sted!
Go!
stemme en noun common
[ˈsd̥ɛmə] · STEMME · stem-me
voice (sound uttered by the mouth, especially that uttered by human beings in speech or song)
also: vote; voice; tune
Lad os stemme.
Let's vote.
Vi vil stemme.
We want to vote.
family
sten en noun general
/steːn/ · STEN
stone
Gem dig bag den sten.
Hide behind that rock.
Det var de vises sten.
This was the Philosopher's Stone.
S
stige en noun common
[ˈsd̥iːə] · STIYE · sti-ge
ladder
also: rise; ascend; increase
Skynd dig at stige ind!
Hurry up and get in.
Han ville blot låne en stige.
He just wanted to borrow a ladder.
stigning en noun common
STINING · stig-ning
ascent
also: rise; increase; gradient
Der var en jævn stigning i indbyggertallet.
There was a steady increase in population.
Der har været en stigning i engrospriserne.
There's been an increase in wholesale prices.
stikke verb rare
[ˈsd̥eɡ̊ə] · STIKKE · stik-ke
to stick
also: to put; to thrust; to shove
Lad os stikke af sammen.
Let's run away together.
Sami blev ved med at stikke hånden i en hvepserede.
Sami continued to stir up the hornets' nest.
stil en noun general
/sdiːl/ · STIL
style
also: manner; essay, paper
Stil dig i kø.
Get in the queue.

stille et noun common
[ˈsd̥elə] · STILLE · stil-le
still
also: quiet; calm; silent
Ti stille!

health
stilling en noun common
STILLING · stil-ling
position
also: post (position in employment)
Han søger en god stilling.
He is looking for a good job.
Har du søgt en stilling i dette firma?

stjæle verb rare
/stjɛːlə/ · STYEHLE · stjæ-le
to steal
De ønskede at stjæle bilen.
They wanted to steal the car.
Han tog dem i at stjæle æbler.
He caught them stealing apples.
drink
stof et noun general
/ˈsdʌf/ · STOF
cloth, fabric
also: substance; matter; drug
Køb seks meter af det stof.
Buy six meters of that cloth.

stol en noun rare
/stoːl/ · STOL
chair
Stol på Tom!
Trust Tom.
Stol på mig.

stolt adjective general
STOLT
proud
Jeg er stolt af dig.
I'm proud of you.
Jeg er stolt af jer.
I'm proud of you guys.
stop et noun general
STOP
imp
Stop!
Stop!
Stop ham!
Stop him.
stoppe verb common
[ˈsd̥ʌb̥ə] · STOPPE · stop-pe
to stop, halt
also: to stop; to cram; to darn
Kan du stoppe Tom?
Can you stop Tom?
De kan ikke stoppe mig.

stoppet verb general
STOPPET · stop-pet
to stoppe
Min næse er stoppet.
My nose is stuffy.
Er Mike stoppet med at drikke?
Has Mike quit drinking?
stor adjective core
/stoːˀr/ · STOR
large
also: great; magnificent; important; capital, upper case; fat
Hvor stor?
How big?
Du er stor.
You are big.
straf en noun general
[ˈsd̥ʁɑf] · STRAF
punishment, penalty
Denne gang undgår du ikke din straf.
This time, you won't avoid your punishment.
Tom sagde at han syntes at Marys straf var passende.
Tom said he thought Mary's punishment was appropriate.
straks adverb general
[ˈsd̥ʁɑɡ̊s] · STRAKS
at once, immediately, straight away, right away, instantly
also: presently, shortly; as soon as
Kom straks herhen!
Come over here right now.
S
stramme verb rare
STRAMME · stram-me
to tighten, make tight or stringent
Så spring ud som strammer.
So jump out as a tightener.
strand en noun common
STRAND
beach
also: shore, seashore; seaside
Tom var alene på den tomme strand.
Tom was alone on an empty beach.
Er der en offentlig strand her i nærheden?
Is there a public beach around here?
strategi en noun common
/stʁatɛɡiː/ · STRATEYI · stra-te-gi
a strategy
Jeg forstår ikke Toms strategi.
I don't understand Tom's strategy.
Vores COVID-19-strategi er den grønne kurve.
Our COVID-19 strategy is the green curve.
stribe en noun general
STRIBE · stri-be
A stripe
Skynd dig! Ser du den røde stribe på himlen? Om nogle minutter stiger solen, og da må du dø!
Quick! Quick! do you not see the rosy streak in the sky? In a few moments the sun will rise and then you must die!

S
strid en noun general
/striːd/ · STRID
rough
also: rapid; stiff; stubborn
Denne ret må ikke påberåbes ved anklager, der virkelig hidrører fra ikke-politiske forbrydelser eller fra handlinger i strid med De forenede Nationers formål og principper.
This right may not be invoked in the case of prosecutions genuinely arising from non-political crimes or from acts contrary to the purposes and principles of the United Nations.
strøm en noun general
[ˈstχœmˀ] · STRURM
current
also: flow, stream, spate; energy
Glødepærer bruger strøm.
Lightbulbs consume electricity.
Glødelamper bruger strøm.
Lightbulbs consume electricity.
studerende en noun common
[sd̥uˈd̥eˀɐnə] · STUTHERENDE · stu-de-ren-de
student
Hun er studerende.
She's a student.
Han er studerende.
He's a student.
education
studie en noun general
STUTHIE · stu-die
studio
also: study
Observationen af tyngdebølger vil starte en ny fase i studiet af universet.
The observation of gravitational waves will start a new phase in the study of the universe.
S
stund en noun general
[ˈsdɔnˀ] · STUND
an undetermined amount of time, a while
also: a moment
Jeg vil gerne sidde her for en stund.
I'd like to sit here for a while.
Hun tog en pause, og jeg afløste hende for en stund.
She took a break, and I took over for a while.
time
stykke et noun common
[ˈsd̥øɡ̊ə] · STEWKKE · styk-ke
piece
also: to divide into pieces
Det er et stykke kage.
It's a piece of cake.
Giv mig et stykke kridt.
Give me a piece of chalk.
styr et noun common
STEWR
handlebar(s)
also: rudder; control
Få styr på dig selv!
Get a grip on yourself!
Ingen lod til at have styr på det.
Nobody seems to be in control.
styre et noun general
/styːrə/ · STEWRE · sty-re
government, regime
also: rule; management; to head, govern, rule, manage, control
Han kunne ikke styre hesten.
He couldn't control the horse.
Jeg kan næsten ikke styre mig.
I can barely control myself.

styrelse et noun rare
STEWREL-SEH · sty-rel-se
board, management, administration, council
Der er ikke brug for nye styrelser.
There is no need for new boards.
styrke en noun common
STEWRKE · styr-ke
strength
also: optical strength; the quality of being prescription; to strengthen, invigorate; to refresh, strengthen oneself
Canada vil styrke forholdet til pålidelige allierede.
Canada wants to strengthen its relationships with reliable allies.
Canada vil styrke forholdet til troværdige allierede.
Canada wants to strengthen its relationships with trustworthy allies.
stærk adjective common
STEHRK
strong
also: hot, spicy
Han er stærk.
He's strong.
Hun er stærk.
She is strong.
størrelse et noun general
/ˈstœrəlsə/ · STURRREL-SEH · stør-rel-se
size
also: dimensions; height; extent
De har alle samme størrelse.
They're all the same size.
Har I jeans i min størrelse?
Do you have jeans in my size?

størst adjective general
/størst/ · STURRST
supd

Which is larger, the sun or the earth?

Which is larger, Japan or England?
støtte en noun common
/støtə/ · STURTTE · støt-te
support
also: support, grant; pillar, statue
Du har min fulde støtte.
You have my full support.
Algeriet fortjener tillid og støtte.
Algeria deserves trust and support.

[ˈsd̥ɔˀ] · STAW
to stand
also: to be, appear , to say, to be written; standstill, halt
Stå tidligt op.

Tid til at stå op.
Time to get up.
succes en noun common
/sykse/ · SUCCES · suc-ces
success (achievement of one's aim or goal)
Vi har succes.
We're successful.
Du er en succes.

svar et noun common
/svɑːˀ/ · SVAR
answer
also: reply; response; retort, repartee
Svar mig.
Answer me.
Svar ikke.
Don't answer.
svare verb general
/svaːrə/ · SVARE · sva-re
to answer, reply, respond
also: to retort; to respond, counter; to correspond
Jeg nægter at svare.
I refuse to answer.
Jeg kunne ikke svare.
I couldn't answer.
svensk adjective rare
/ˈsvɛnˀsk/ · SVENSK
Swedish
also: the Swedish language

Speak Swedish!
Svensk er let.

/svɛkə/ · SVEHKKE · svæk-ke
to weaken
also: to impair
Min fiasko svækkede ikke min selvtillid.
My failure did not weaken my self-confidence.
svær en noun common
/svɛːˀr/ · SVEHR
rind
also: crackling; difficult, hard, stiff, tough; heavy, massive, solid
Al begyndelse er svær.
Every beginning is difficult.
Dansk udtale er lidt svær.
Danish pronunciation is a little bit difficult.
syd et noun common
/syd/ · SEWD
south
also: south, southwards,
Mod syd.

Fuglene fløj mod syd.
The birds flew to the south.
sydlig adjective rare
SEWDLEE · syd-lig
southern
also: southerly

Pskov is in the south of Russia.
Pingviner lever næsten udelukkende på den sydlige halvkugle.
Penguins live almost exclusively in the Southern Hemisphere.

/syːˀ/ · SEWG
sick, ill
also: diseased; wicked

sygdom en noun common
/syːdɔm/ · SEWGDOM · syg-dom
illness, disease, ailment
Tom har Alzheimers sygdom.
Tom has Alzheimer's.
Toms far har Alzheimers sygdom.

sygehus et noun common
/syːɣəhuːˀs/ · SEWYEHUS · sy-ge-hus
hospital

health
