/syːəplajˀərskə/ · SEWYEPLAYERSKE · sy-geple-jer-ske
nurse
Er du sygeplejerske?
Are you a nurse?

symptom
De gule veste i Frankrig er et symptom.
syn et noun general
SEWN
vision, faculty of sight

Tom har et dårligt syn.

[ˈsynəs] · SEWNES · sy-nes
to think

synspunkt et noun rare
SEWNSPUNKT · syns-punkt
point of view
Jeg deler ikke hendes synspunkt.
I don't share her point of view.
Jeg forventer ikke at de vil støtte mit synspunkt.
I don't expect that they will support my view.
system et noun general
/systeːm/ · SEWSTEM · sy-stem
system
Det er et godt system.
It's a good system.
Jeg forstår ikke dette system.
I don't understand this system.
syv adjective common
[ˈsywˀ] · SEWW
seven
Katte har syv liv.
Cats have seven lives.
Væk os klokken syv.
Wake us up at seven o'clock.
sælge verb common
/sɛlɣə/ · SEHLYE · sæl-ge
to sell
Hun vil sælge sin bil.
She wants to sell her car.
Jeg vil sælge mit hus.
I'm going to sell my house.

sænke verb general
/sɛnkə/ · SEHNKE · sæn-ke
to lower, to reduce
also: to sink; to fall, descend
Tom sænkede farten ved stopskiltet, men han stoppede faktisk ikke.
"Tom slowed down for the stop sign, but he didn't actually stop."
særdeles adverb general
SEHRDELES · sær-de-les
extremely, particularly
Det er særdeles interessant.
That's extremely interesting.
Min metode er overraskende simpel, men særdeles effektiv.
My method is surprisingly simple, but highly effective.
culture
særlig adjective common
/ˈsæɐ̯li/ · SEHRLEE · sær-lig
special; particular
I dag er en særlig dag.
Today is a special day.
Mary er ikke særlig feminin.
Mary isn't very feminine.
culture
sæson en noun common
/sɛˈsɔŋ/ · SEHSON · sæ-son
the time in which something is done
"Hvilken sæson var det?"
“What season was it?”

S
sæt et noun general
/sɛt/ · SEHT
set
also: start
Sæt jer!
Take a seat.
Sæt farten ned.
Please slow down.
sætte verb core
[ˈsɛd̥ə] · SEHTTE · sæt-te
to put, place, set
also: to fix, stake; to plant; to suppose, posit
Hvor kan vi sætte os?
Where can we sit down?
Lad os sætte os på bænken.
Let's sit down on the bench.
sø en noun general
SUREN · sø-en
lake; loch; sea (in place names)
Hvor dyb er søen?
How deep is the lake?
Søen er meget dyb.
The lake is very deep.
søge verb common
SURYE · sø-ge
to search, look
also: to seek, to look for; to apply for to submit oneself as a candidate; to apply for to file a formal request for something
Jeg måtte søge ly under et træ.
I had to take shelter under a tree.
Hvis jeg var dig, ville jeg søge jobbet.
If I were you, I would apply for the job.
S
søn en noun common
SURN
son a male person in relation to his parents
De har en søn.
They have a son.
Han er min søn.
He is my son.
søndag en noun core
/ˈsœnˀda/ · SURNDAG · søn-dag
Sunday
I morgen er det søndag.
It is Sunday tomorrow.
weather time
sørge verb common
/sœrɣə/ · SURRYE · sør-ge
to grieve, mourn, lament
also: to take care of; look after; make sure
Jeg vil sørge for at Tom har alt han har brug for.
I'll make sure Tom has everything he needs.
sådan adjective core
SAWDAN · så-dan
such
also: like this, like that, like these, like those
Sådan er livet.
That's how life is.
Danish–English Dictionary  ·  Tatoeba (CC-BY)  ·  Leipzig (CC-BY) enetverbadjadvother DA → EN
ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSÅKTUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN såkaldt – tag 189
S
såkaldt adjective common
SAWKALDT · så-kaldt
so-called
Månen er en såkaldt naturlig satellit.
The Moon is a so-called natural satellite.
Solen er som stjerne en såkaldt gul dværg.
As a star, the sun is a so-called yellow dwarf.
således adverb common
SAWLETHES · så-le-des
thus
Vi kan blive tre hundrede år, men når vi så hører op at være til her, bliver vi kun skum på vandet, har ikke engang en grav hernede mellem vore kære. Vi har ingen udødelig sjæl, vi får aldrig liv mere, vi er ligesom det grønne siv, er det engang skåret over, kan det ikke grønnes igen! Menneskene derimod har en sjæl, som lever altid, lever, efter at legemet er blevet jord; den stiger op igennem den klare luft, op til alle de skinnende stjerner! Ligesom vi dykker op af havet og ser menneskenes lande, således dykker de op til ubekendte dejlige steder, dem vi aldrig får at se.
We sometimes live for three hundred years, but when we cease to exist here, we become only foam on the surface of the water and have not even a grave among those we love. We have not immortal souls, we shall never live again; like the green seaweed when once it has been cut off, we can never flourish more. Human beings, on the contrary, have souls which live forever, even after the body has been turned to dust. They rise up through the clear, pure air, beyond the glittering stars. As we rise out of the water and behold all the land of the earth, so do they rise to unknown and glorious regions which we shall never see.
sårbare adjective general
SAWRBARE · sår-ba-re
vulnerable; fragile; at risk
Vi er for sårbare.
We're too vulnerable.
såsom conjunction general
SAWSOM · så-som
such as (introducing an example)
Håndvask anbefales for at forhindre spredning af smitsomme sygdomme såsom coronavirussen.
Hand washing is recommended to prevent the spread of contagious diseases like a coronavirus.
Der findes mange alternativer til komælk, såsom gedemælk, mandelmælk, kokosmælk, sojamælk, hampemælk og rismælk.
There are many alternatives to cow milk, such as goat milk, almond milk, coconut milk, soy milk, hemp milk and rice milk.
S
såvel conjunction general
SAWVEL · så-vel
as well as; both...and (såvel...som = both...and)
Han, såvel som du, er en god golfspiller.
He, just like you, is a good golfer.
Jeg kendte såvel Tom som Mary meget godt.
I knew both Tom and Mary very well.
T
tab et noun general
[ˈtˢæˀb̥] · TAB
loss
also: casualty
Tab dig på tre uger.
Get thinner in three weeks.
Tom har lidt mange tab.
Tom has suffered many losses.
tabe verb general
[ˈtˢæːb̥ə] · TABE · ta-be
to lose
Tom hader at tabe.
Tom hates to lose.
Jeg er vant til at tabe.
I'm used to losing.
tag et noun general
/taːˀɣ/ · TAG
roof
also: hold, grasp, grip; stroke; handling, control
Taget lækker, når det regner.
The roof leaks when it rains.
Han fik et godt tag i håndtaget.
He got a good grip on the handle.
Danish–English Dictionary  ·  Tatoeba (CC-BY)  ·  Leipzig (CC-BY) enetverbadjadvother DA → EN
ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTAGUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN tage – tegne 190
T
tage verb core
/ta(ːˀ)/ · TAYE · ta-ge
to take, get, pick up
also: to catch, hold; to charge; to go somewhere
Lad os tage toget.
Let's take a train.
Vi vil tage toget.
We are going by train.
tak interjection general
/tak/ · TAK
thanks
also: thank you, thanks; jag; point
Tak.
Thank you.
Tak!
Thank you!
takt en noun general
TAKT
tact; timing; beat; rhythm; pace
Ro i takt, alle sammen!
Everybody row in concert!
I takt med at havis og gletsjere smelter, stiger den globale vandstand.
As more sea ice and glaciers melt, the global sea level rises.
tal et noun common
/tal/ · TAL
number
also: figure; digit; numeral
Tal fransk.
Speak French.
Tal svensk!
Speak Swedish!
T
tale en noun core
[ˈtˢæːlə] · TALE · ta-le
speech, talk, address, discourse
also: to make a speech; to speak, talk
Ikke tale om!
No way!
Han kan tale.
He can talk.
education social
tanke en noun general
/tankə/ · TANKE · tan-ke
thought
also: idea; intention; tank up, fill up
Vær venlig at tanke op.
Fill it up, please.
Det var min første tanke.
That was my first thought.
tegn et noun common
[tˢɑjˀn] · TEN
sign
also: token; character; freeman, thane
Tegn en cirkel.
Draw a circle.
Tegn med blyant.
Draw with a pencil.
tegne verb rare
[ˈtˢɑjnə] · TENE · teg-ne
to draw
also: to design; to represent; appear, seem, promise
Tom elsker at tegne.
Tom loves drawing.

T
teknisk adjective general
TENISK · tek-nisk
technical
Teknisk set er det her ulovligt i Algeriet.
Technically, this is illegal in Algeria.
teknologi en noun general
TENOLOYI · tek-no-lo-gi
technology
Tom forstår sig ikke på moderne teknologi.
Tom doesn't understand modern technology.
Udviklingen indenfor digital teknologi skrider frem i et halsbrækkende tempo.
Digital technology is advancing at a breakneck pace.
telefon en noun common
/teləfoːn/ · TELEFON · te-le-fon
telephone
also: phone number, telephone number , abbreviated tlf.; call, phone call, telephone call
Har I en telefon?
Do you have a phone?
Oplad din telefon.
Charge your phone.
technology
temperatur en noun rare
TEMPERATUR · tem-pe-ra-tur
temperature (all senses)
Jeg tog hans temperatur.
I took his temperature.
Min mor har taget min temperatur.
My mother took my temperature.
weather
T
tendens en noun general
/tɛndɛns/ · TENDENS · ten-dens
tendency
also: trend; inclination; bias
Folk har en tendens til at undervurdere deres fremtidige behov.
People have a tendency to underestimate their future needs.
Magt har en tendens til at korrumpere, og fuldstændig magt korrumperer fuldstændigt.
Power tends to corrupt, and absolute power corrupts absolutely.
test en noun common
/ˈtɛsd/ · TEST
A , assessment or examination.
Dna-test viste at han var uskyldig.
DNA tests showed he was innocent.
DNA-test viste at han var uskyldig.
DNA tests showed he was innocent.
education
teste verb common
/ˈtɛsdə/ · TESTE · te-ste
to test, examine
also: to test, undergo testing, receive test results
Hun blev testet for hiv.
She got tested for HIV.
Han blev testet for hiv.
He got tested for HIV.
education
the en noun common
THE
tea
Denne video er the shit.
This video is the shit.
The Beatles gav fem koncerter i 1996 i Tokyo.
The Beatles gave five concerts in Tokyo in 1996.

Ordbog DA→EN tid – tildele 192
T
tid en noun core
TID
time
Hvad tid?
What time?
Har vi tid?
Do we have time?
tide noun general
TITHE · ti-de
time; period; era (archaic/literary)
Jeg ankom i tide.
I arrived in time.
Det er på tide at lære dansk.
It's time to learn Danish.
tidlig adjective general
TIDLEE · tid-lig
early
Kom i morgen tidlig.
Come tomorrow morning.
Du bør gå tidlig hjem.
You had best go home early.
tidspunkt et noun common
[ˈtˢiðsˌpʰɔŋˀd̥] · TIDSPUNKT · tids-punkt
time, moment, date, point in time
Vi burde gå ud på et tidspunkt.
We should go out sometime.
Hun var nitten på det tidspunkt.
She was nineteen at that time.
time
T
tilbage adverb core
/teˈbaːjə/ · TILBAYE · til-ba-ge
back
also: left, remaining; backwards
Kom tilbage!
Come back.
Ingen kom tilbage.
Nobody came back.
body
tilbud et noun common
/telbuð/ · TILBUD · til-bud
an offer
also: an offer where a ware has a reduced price
Er den her på tilbud?
Is this on sale?
Tom afviste vores tilbud.
Tom refused our offer.
tilbyde verb common
TILBEWDE · til-by-de
to offer
Kan jeg tilbyde jer et lift?
Can I offer you guys a ride?
Lad os se hvad Tom har at tilbyde.
Let's see what Tom has to offer.
tildele verb rare
TILDELE · til-de-le
to allocate, allot, assign
also: to award
Den ene tildeler ham et hårdt slag i ansigtet.
One of them gives him a hard slap in the face.

ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTILUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN tilfreds – tilknytte 193
T
tilfreds adjective common
TILFREDS · til-freds
satisfied
Han var aldrig tilfreds.
He was never content.
Hooker var yderst tilfreds.
Hooker was extremely pleased.
tilfælde et noun core
TILFEHLDE · til-fæl-de
case, occurrence
also: accident, coincidence
Han er et håbløst tilfælde.
He's a hopeless case.
Ring 119 i tilfælde af brand.
In case of fire, call 119.
tilfældighed en noun rare
TILFEHLDIGHETH · til-fæl-dig-hed
randomness, chance
also: accident
Det er ikke en tilfældighed.
It's not a coincidence.
Tom sagde at det bare var en tilfældighed.
Tom said that it was just a coincidence.
tilføje verb rare
[ˈtˢelˌfʌjˀə] · TILFURYE · til-fø-je
to add, append
Kan du tilføje mig?
Could you add me?
Jeg foretrækker at oversætte gamle sætninger frem for at tilføje nye.
I prefer translating old sentences to adding new ones.
technology
T
tilgang en noun general
TILGANG · til-gang
growth, increase
also: approach; a way of trying to solve a problem or understand something; access
Toms tilgang til sprogindlæring er at smide alt op på væggen og se hvad der hænger ved.
Tom's strategy for language learning is to throw everything against the wall and see what sticks.
tilgængelig adjective rare
available, accessible, obtainable
Anden sæson er også tilgængelig.
tilkalde verb rare
TILKALDE · til-kal-de
to call for; to summon; to send for
Vi måtte tilkalde låsesmeden.
We had to call the locksmith.
Nogen burde tilkalde en læge.
Someone ought to get a doctor.
tilknytte verb rare
TILNEWTTE · til-knyt-te
to attach
also: to affiliate; to connect
De kan læses i de tilknyttede artikler.
They can be read in the associated articles.

Ordbog DA→EN tillade – tilsyneladende 194
T
tillade verb general
/ˈteˌladə/ · TILLATHE · til-la-de
to allow, permit
Fortæl dem at jeg ikke vil tillade at Mary forlader landet.
Tell them I won't allow Mary to leave the country.
bureaucracy
tilladelse et noun general
TILLATHEL-SEH · til-la-del-se
permission, permit, consent
Behøver vi tilladelse fra Tom?
Do we need permission from Tom?
Hvem gav dig tilladelse til at gøre det?
Who gave you permission to do that?
bureaucracy
tillid en noun common
/teliːd/ · TILLID · til-lid
confidence
also: trust; reliance
Tom har forrådt min tillid.
Tom betrayed my confidence.
Han misbrugte vores tillid.
He abused our trust.
tilstand en noun common
/ˈtelˌstan/ · TILSTAND · til-stand
state
also: condition; condition
Hans tilstand ændrede sig til det værre.
His condition changed for the worse.
Dette program kan ikke køres i DOS-tilstand.
This program cannot be run in DOS mode.
T
tilstrækkelig adjective general
TILSTREHKKELIGT · til-stræk-ke-ligt
sufficient; adequate; enough
Tom sagde at ingen ord tilstrækkeligt kunne beskrive hans følelse af fortvivlelse og sorg over at miste sin bedste ven.
Tom said that no words could adequately describe his feelings of devastation and grief on losing his best friend.
tilsvarende adjective general
TILSVARENDE · til-sva-ren-de
corresponding; equivalent; proportionate
Efterspørgslen efter stoffer i USA vil altid skabe et tilsvarende udbud.
American demand for drugs will always create their supply.
tilsyn et noun general
TILSEWN · til-syn
supervision
Barnet sover tilsyneladende.
The child is apparently sleeping.
Han vidste tilsyneladende alt om mig.
Apparently, he knew everything about me.
tilsyneladende adverb common
TILSEWNELATHENDE · til-sy-ne-la-den-de
seemingly, apparently
also: apparent
Barnet sover tilsyneladende.
The child is apparently sleeping.
Han vidste tilsyneladende alt om mig.
Apparently, he knew everything about me.
family technology

Ordbog DA→EN tiltag – titel 195
T
tiltag et noun common
TILTAG · til-tag
initiative, step, measure
Kærlighed er som månen; når den ikke er i tiltagen, er den i aftagen.
Love is like the moon; when it does not wax, it wanes.
Kærlighed er som månen; når den ikke er i tiltagende, er den i aftagende.
Love is like the moon; when it does not wax, it wanes.
tiltale et noun rare
[ˈtelˌtæːlə] · TILTALE · til-ta-le
indictment
also: the act of addressing or getting addressed by someone; to satisfy, please, make an impact (on someone); to indict, accuse
Den måde tiltaler landmanden.
This way appeals to the farmer.
tiltrække verb general
TILTREHKKE · til-træk-ke
to attract
also: to draw (pull)
Mary er tiltrækkende.
Mary is attractive.
Du er en tiltrækkende pige.
"You're an attractive girl."
time en noun common
TIME · ti-me
hour
also: lesson, class
Vi har kun en time.
We've only got an hour.
Jeg har en halv time.
I've got half an hour.
T
ting en noun core
/tenˀɡ/ · TING
thing
also: assembly, parliament
Nyd de små ting!
Enjoy the little things!
Hvor er mine ting?
Where are my things?
tirsdag en noun core
/ˈtiɐ̯ˀsda/ · TIRSDAG · tirs-dag
Tuesday
Det er tirsdag i morgen.
It's Tuesday tomorrow.
I morgen er det tirsdag.
Tomorrow is Tuesday.
time
tit adverb general
TIT
often
also: frequently
Jeg læser tit.
I often read.
Jeg er her tit.
I'm often here.
titel en noun rare
TITEL · ti-tel
A title.
Tom er i gang med at skrive en bog, men den har endnu ikke en titel.
Tom is writing a book, but it does not have a title yet.

Ordbog DA→EN tjekke – tradition 196
T
tjekke en noun general
TYEKKE · tjek-ke
czech (a person from the Czech Republic (Czechia) or of Czech descent)
Jeg vil gerne tjekke ud.
I'd like to check out.
Hvornår skal vi tjekke ud?
When should we check out?
tjene verb general
[ˈtɕɛːnə] · TYENE · tje-ne
to serve
also: to earn
Vil du tjene mange penge?
Do you want to earn a lot of money?
Ingen kan tjene to herrer.
No man can serve two masters.
tog et noun core
/tɔː(ˀ)ɣ/ · TOG
train
also: expedition
Du tog fejl.
You were mistaken.
Han tog hævn.
He took revenge.
weather transport
tom adjective general
[ˈtˢʌmˀ] · TOM
empty
Tom lo.
Tom was laughing.
Tom ler.
Tom's laughing.
T
ton et noun general
/tʌn/ · TON
tonne, metric ton (unit of weight, equivalent to 1000 kilograms)
Dette køleskab vejer et ton.
This fridge weighs a ton.
top interjection general
TOP
summit, peak
also: hairpiece; top (uppermost part, lid, cap, cover, garment worn to cover the torso, child’s spinning
Der er et yndigt land, det står med brede bøge nær salten østerstrand, nær salten østerstrand. Det bugter sig i bakke dal, det hedder gamle Danmark, og det er Frejas sal, og det er Frejas sal! Vort gamle Danmark skal bestå så længe bøgen spejler, sin top i bølgen blå, sin top i bølgen blå.
There is a lovely country; it stands with wide beeches near a salty eastern beach, near a salty eastern beach. It winds through rolling hills, its name is old Denmark, and it is Freja’s hall, and it is Freja’s hall! Our old Denmark must remain as long as the beech mirrors its top in the blue waves, its top in the blue waves.
torsdag en noun core
/ˈtɒːˀsda/ · TORSDAG · tors-dag
Thursday
I dag er det torsdag.
Today is Thursday.
Er det torsdag i dag?
Is today Thursday?
time
tradition en noun general
/tradiˈsjoːn/ · TRATHITION · tra-di-tion
tradition
Den her berberiske tradition uddøde for længe siden.
This Berber tradition has died out long ago.

ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTRAUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN traditionel – trivsel 197
T
traditionel adjective rare
/tradisjonɛl/ · TRATHITIONEL · tra-di-tio-nel
traditional
Kimchi er en traditionel koreansk ret.
Kimchi is a traditional Korean dish.
En e-bog ligner meget en traditionel bog. Den skal have et omslag, en indholdsfortegnelse, særskilte kapitler og et indeks.
An e-book is very much like a traditional book. It should have a cover, a table of contents, separate chapters and an index.
culture
trafik en noun common
TRAFIK · tra-fik
traffic
also: illegal trade; trafficking (sense 2)
Der er rigtignok en masse trafik i dag.
There sure is a lot of traffic today.
Al trafik blev standset for at lade den præsidentielle bilkortege passere.
All traffic was halted to let the presidential motorcade pass.
travlt adjective common
TRAVLT
busy; hectic; with a lot to do
Vi har travlt.
We're in a hurry.
Har du travlt?
Are you in a hurry?
tre adjective core
[ˈtˢʁ̥ɛˀ] · TRE
three
Næsten tre.
Nearly three.
Tre is, tak.
Three ice-creams, please.
T
tredje adjective common
/ˈtreðjə/ · TREDYE · tred-je
third
3 i tredje er 27.
3 to the third power is 27.
Tom er på tredje sal.
Tom is on the third floor.
tredjedel en noun general
TREDYETHEL · tred-je-del
a third (1/3) (a fraction, each of three equal parts)
En tredjedel af ni er tre.
One third of nine is three.
En tredjedel er mindre end en halv.
A third is less than a half.
culture
trist adjective general
TRIST
sad
Jeg er trist.
I'm sad.
Hvorfor så trist?
Why so glum?
emotion
trivsel en noun general
TRIVSEL · triv-sel
well-being
also: growth, prosperity
Et mindre fald i elevernes trivsel.
A minor decrease in student well-being.

T
tro en noun common
/troːˀ/ · TRO
belief
also: confidence; trust; faith
Tro du mig.
Believe you me.
Du må tro mig.
Believe you me.
trods en noun common
/ˈtrɔs/ · TRODS
defiance
also: despite
Jeg gik ud på trods af regnen.
I went out in spite of the rain.
Trods bekymringerne var vejret pragtfuldt.
Despite the concerns, the weather was magnificent.
tror verb rare
to pres
Alligevel tror Engell på en aftale.
true verb rare
/truːə/ · TRUE
to threaten
"Truede du ham?"
"Did you threaten him?"
Tom truede os.
Tom threatened us.
food
T
tryg adjective general
/trøɡ/ · TREWG
safe
also: secure; easy; confident
Jeg følte mig tryg i hans stærke arme.
I felt safe in his strong arms.
trygge adjective general
[ˈtˢʁ̥œɡ̊ə] · TREWGYE · tryg-ge
tryg
Vi føler os meget trygge her.
We feel very safe here.
tryghed en noun general
TREWGHETH · tryg-hed
safety, security, comfort
Min egen tryghed.
My own safety.
trygt adjective general
TREWGT
tryg
Så alle kan handle trygt.
So everyone can shop safely.

T
træ et noun general
/trɛːˀ/ · TREH
tree
also: wood, timber
Træ brænder.
Wood burns.
Et træ er grønt.
A tree is green.
nature
træde verb general
/trɛːðə/ · TREHDE · træ-de
to step
also: to tread; to thread
Du bør træde tilbage.
You should resign.
Du er nødt til at træde tilbage.
You need to resign.
træffe verb general
[ˈtˢʁ̥afə] · TREHFFE · træf-fe
to meet, hook up
also: to hit; meet, see
Jeg ønskede at træffe dig.
I wanted to meet you.
Hvilke foranstaltninger bør vi træffe?
What action should we take?
social
træk en noun common
/trɛk/ · TREHK
pull
also: touch, feature, trait; move; migration, flight
Træk dit sværd.
Draw your sword.
Træk en streg her.
Draw a line here.
T
trække verb common
/trɛkə/ · TREHKKE · træk-ke
to pull, drag, haul, tow
also: to draw; to withdraw; to steep
Jeg kan ikke trække vejret!
I can't breathe.
Jeg kunne ikke trække vejret.
I couldn't breathe.
træn et noun rare
/trɛŋ/ · TREHN
baggage, train
Jeg træner.
I work out.
Jeg træner judo.
"I'm practising judo."
weather transport
træne verb general
/trɛːnə/ · TREHNE · træ-ne
to train, coach
also: to practise; to train
Det er for varmt i dag til at træne.
It's too hot to exercise today.
Marys læge rådede hende til at træne.
Mary's doctor advised her to exercise.
weather transport
trænge verb rare
/trɛŋə/ · TREHNYE · træn-ge
to need, want
also: to press, force; to advance, force; to push, intrude
Det lykkedes os at trænge ham op i en krog.
We managed to corner him.
Det lykkedes os at trænge ham op i et hjørne.
We managed to corner him.

T
træning en noun common
TREHNING · træ-ning
training, practice
Og træning skal der til.
And training is required.
weather transport
træt adjective general
/trɛt/ · TREHT
tired
also: fed up, frustrated
Så træt!
So tired!
Er du træt?
Are you tired?
trådt verb rare
[ˈtˢʁ̥ʌd̥] · TRAWDT · trå-dt
stepped; trodden
Du har lige trådt på en hvepserede.
You have just stepped on a hornet's nest.
tung adjective rare
[ˈtˢɔŋˀ] · TUNG
heavy
also: dark, gloomy, heavy (about skies etc.); heavy, arduous; important, major
Kassen er tung.
The box is heavy.
Stenen er tung.
The stone is heavy.
T
tur en noun common
/tuːr/ · TUR
turn
also: trail; walk, stroll; outing, excursion
God tur!
Have a nice trip.
Gå en tur.
Take a walk.
turde verb rare
[ˈtˢoːɐ] · TURDE · tur-de
to dare
also: ought to; might
Den stakkels ælling vidste hverken, hvor den turde stå eller gå, den var så bedrøvet, fordi den så så styg ud og var til spot for hele andegården.
The poor duckling did not know where to go, and was quite miserable because he was so ugly and laughed at by the whole farmyard.
turist en noun rare
TURIST
a tourist
Jeg er turist.
I am a tourist.
Jeg er en turist.
I am a tourist.
tusinde et noun general
TUSINDE · tu-sin-de
thousand
also: thousandth; a thousand (a unit of ca. 1000 pieces)
Jeg vil gerne låne omkring tre hundrede tusinde dollar.
I'd like to borrow about three hundred thousand dollars.
Det ville kede mig hvis jeg skulle tælle til fem hundrede og fireogtyve tusinde tre hundrede og tooghalvfjerds.
I would be bored if I had to count to five hundred twenty-four thousand three hundred seventy-two.
Danish–English Dictionary  ·  Tatoeba (CC-BY)  ·  Leipzig (CC-BY) enetverbadjadvother DA → EN
ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUSUVWXYZÆØÅ
Ordbog DA→EN tusindvis – type 201
T
tusindvis adverb general
TUSINDVIS · tu-sind-vis
thousands ( / of)
Wow! Tusindvis af fisk!
Wow! Thousands of fish!
Tusindvis af udlændinge rejser til Japan hvert år.
Thousands of foreigners visit Japan every year.
tvinge verb rare
/tveŋə/ · TVINYE · tvin-ge
to force, compel
also: to force; to compel
Kan du tvinge dig selv til at sove?
Can you will yourself to fall asleep?
Ingen kan tvinge os til at gøre det.
No one can force us to do that.
tvivl en noun common
/tvivl/ · TVIVL
doubt (uncertainty)
Uden tvivl!
Without a doubt!
Er du kommet i tvivl?
Are you having second thoughts?
tværs adverb common
/tvɛrs/ · TVEHRS
across, abeam
Tom fik et føl på tværs.
Tom threw a hissy fit.
Tom tog på en biltur tværs over Australien.
Tom went on a road trip across Australia.
T
tværtimod adverb rare
TVEHRTIMOD · tvær-ti-mod
on the contrary, au contraire
Desværre ikke; tværtimod.
No, unfortunately; on the contrary.
Jeg var tværtimod overrasket over hendes ærlighed.
I was rather taken aback by her honesty.
tyde verb rare
[ˈtˢyːðə] · TEWDE · ty-de
to interpret
also: decipher, read
Hans håndskrift er umulig at tyde.
His handwriting is impossible to decipher.
tydelig adjective common
TEWDELIGT · ty-de-ligt
clear; distinct; obvious
Tal tydeligt.
Speak clearly.
Jeg kan se tydeligt nu.
I can see clearly now.
type et noun general
TEWPE · ty-pe
a type (kind, sort)
Du er ikke min type.
You're not my type.
Hun er ikke min type.
She's not my type.

T
typisk adjective common
TEWPISK · ty-pisk
typical
Den typiske brasilianer har en forfærdelig musiksmag.
The Brazilian average Joe has awful taste in music.
tysk adjective general
/tysk/ · TEWSK
German
Jeg er tysk.
I am German.
Taler du tysk?
Do you speak German?
tyveri et noun general
TEWVERI · ty-ve-ri
thievery, theft (act of stealing property)
also: stealing; burglary
Han er skyldig i tyveri.
He is guilty of stealing.
Hun beskyldte dem for tyveri.
She accused him and her of stealing.
drink
tælle en noun rare
TEHLLE · tæl-le
tallow
also: to count; to number
Kan du tælle?
Do you know how to count?
Kan du tælle på fransk?
Can you count in French?
T
tænke verb common
/tɛnkə/ · TEHNKE · tæn-ke
to think
also: to believe; to intend
Lad mig tænke.
Let me think.
Jeg vil tænke over det.
I'll think about it.
tæt adjective core
/tɛt/ · TEHT
close
also: dense, tight, thick, heavy; watertight, waterproof, air-tight; closely
Det var tæt på!
That was a close call.
Tågen er meget tæt.
The fog's very dense.
tøj et noun common
/ˈtɔj/ · TURY
fabric, material
also: cloth, clothes, clothing; tool, mechanism; means of transport, mode of transport
Skift dit tøj.
Change your clothes.
Tom har tøj på.
Tom is wearing clothes.
tåle verb rare
/ˈtɔːlə/
to tolerate; to endure
also: to bear; to put up with
Jeg tåler ikke lugten af cigaretrøg.
"I can't stand the smell of cigarette smoke."

Ordbog DA→EN uanset – uddele 203
U
uanset preposition common
UANSET · uan-set
notwithstanding, whatever
also: despite (that), even though; regardless (of the circumstances)
Uanset hvad, taber du.
Either way, you lose.
Jeg vil stå ved din side, uanset hvad.
I will stand by you, no matter what.
udarbejde verb rare
UTHARBAYDE · ud-ar-bej-de
to prepare, produce, develop
Rapporten er udarbejdet af ph.
Authors of the report:
udbrede verb rare
UDBRETHE · ud-bre-de
to propagate
also: to propagate, promulgate
Hold op med at udbrede disse løgne!
Stop spreading these lies!
udbrud et noun general
UDBRUD · ud-brud
an eruption (of a volcano or the like)
also: an outburst of emotions; an outbreak (of a war, infectious disease, or the like); an act of breaking out of something
Bárðarbunga er i udbrud i Island.
The Bárðarbunga is erupting in Iceland.
Bárðarbunga er i udbrud på Island.
The Bárðarbunga is erupting in Iceland.
U
udbud et noun general
UDBUD · ud-bud
supply
also: Something being available for choosing, such as on a market; an option, offer.
Efterspørgslen efter stoffer i USA vil altid skabe et tilsvarende udbud.
American demand for drugs will always create their supply.
uddanne verb rare
UDDANNE · ud-dan-ne
to educate
also: to pursue a degree; to educate oneself
Uddannelse i denne verden skuffer mig.
Education in this world disappoints me.
Uddannelse er det bedste middel mod vold.
Education is the best antidote against violence.
uddannelse et noun common
UDDANNEL-SEH · ud-dan-nel-se
education
Uddannelse i denne verden skuffer mig.
Education in this world disappoints me.
Uddannelse er det bedste middel mod vold.
Education is the best antidote against violence.
uddele verb rare
UDDELE · ud-de-le
to distribute, to hand out to multiple parties
Tom tilbragte eftermiddagen med at uddele pjecer.
Tom spent the afternoon handing out leaflets.
Tom tilbragte eftermiddagen med at uddele foldere.
Tom spent the afternoon handing out leaflets.
body culture

Ordbog DA→EN ude – udenrigsminister 204
U
ude adverb core
/uːdə/ · UTHE
out
also: outside
Vi er ude!
We're out!
Hun er ude nu.
She is out now.
udelukke verb rare
/uːdəlɔkə/ · UTHELUKKE · ude-luk-ke
to shut out, exclude
also: bar, exclude; to ban; to debar
Det er udelukket.
That is out of the question.
Det er helt udelukket.
That is out of the question.
udelukkende adverb general
/uːdəlɔkənə/ · UTHELUKKENDE · ude-luk-ken-de
only, solely, exclusively
also: entirely; simply
Jeg skriver udelukkende på mit modersmål.
I write exclusively in my mother tongue.
Pingviner lever næsten udelukkende på den sydlige halvkugle.
Penguins live almost exclusively in the Southern Hemisphere.
uden preposition core
/ˈuðən/ · UTHEN
without
also: except for, but, save
Te uden is.
Tea without ice.
Uden tvivl!
Without a doubt!
U
udendørs adverb common
UTHENDURRS · uden-dørs
outdoors
also: outdoor, [[outdoors
I dag må vi sove udendørs.
Today, we have to sleep outdoors.
I dag er vi nødt til at sove udendørs.
Today, we have to sleep outdoors.
home
udenfor adverb common
UTHENFOR · uden-for
outside
also: excluded
Tom er udenfor.
Tom is outside.
Tom var udenfor.
Tom was outside.
udenlandsk adjective rare
/uːðənlanˀsk/ · UTHENLANDSK · uden-land-sk
foreign
Han har en udenlandsk bil.
He has a foreign car.
udenrigsminister en noun general
UTHENRIGSMINISTER · uden-rigs-mi-ni-ster
minister of foreign affairs
Tro det eller ej; det siges at aggressorens udenrigsminister engang var en respekteret diplomat.
Believe it or not, they say the aggressor's foreign minister was once a respected diplomat.
politics

Ordbog DA→EN udfordring – udgøre 205
U
udfordring en noun common
UDFORDRING · ud-for-dring
a challenge
Er det en udfordring?
Is that a challenge?
For de fleste arkitekter repræsenterer sådan en skyskraber en designmæssig udfordring.
For most architects, such a skyscraper represents a design challenge.
udføre verb general
UDFURRE · ud-fø-re
to perform, to carry out, to execute, to do
also: to produce, manufacture; to export
Hvem kan udføre dette arbejde?
Who can do this work?
Tom reddede Marys liv ved at udføre Heimlich-manøvren.
Tom saved Mary's life by performing the Heimlich maneuver.
bureaucracy transport
udgang en noun rare
UDGANG · ud-gang
exit, way out
also: end; outcome, issue; ending
Denne dør er den eneste udgang.
This door is the only exit.
Dette er ikke den eneste udgang.
This isn't the only exit.
udgangspunkt et noun common
UDGANGSPUNKT · ud-gangs-punkt
starting point
Som udgangspunkt.
That was the first thing I wished to say.
culture
U
udgave et noun common
UDGAVE · ud-ga-ve
An edition (newspaper, book, article etc.)
also: A version
Jeg læste det i dagens udgave.
I read it in today's edition.
Er faxen en moderne udgave af telegrafen?
Is the fax a modern form of the telegraph?
culture
udgift en noun rare
/udɡift/ · UDYIFT · ud-gift
expense
also: spending, expenditure
Tom skar ned på sine udgifter.
Tom cut down on his spending.
Algeriets sociale udgifter er stadig meget høje.
"Algeria's social spending is still very high."
udgive verb rare
UDYIVE · ud-gi-ve
to publish, release
ABBA udgiver et nyt album efter fyrre år.
ABBA is releasing a new album after forty years.
En fransk oversættelse af denne bog blev udgivet i 2013.
A French translation of this book was published in 2013.
udgøre verb rare
UDGURRE · ud-gø-re
to form, make up, constitute
also: to add up to a certain proportion of something; to amount to, be in total; to represent, be synonymous with
Amerikanere er bekymrede for at illegale migranter kommer til at udgøre en trussel mod nationens kultur og økonomi.
Americans are worried that illegal migrants are threatening the nation's culture and economy.
bureaucracy

U
udland et noun rare
[ˈuðˌlanˀ] · UDLAND · ud-land
abroad, foreign countries; everywhere that is not this country, taken as a whole
Tom var i udlandet.
Tom was abroad.
Min bror bor i udlandet.
My brother lives abroad.
transport
udlevere verb rare
UDLEVERE · ud-le-ve-re
to hand over; to extradite; to deliver
Til den store åbningsfest planlagde butiksejerne at udlevere gratis gaver til de første tredive kunder i køen.
For their grand opening, the store owners were planning giveaways for the first thirty customers in line.
udlænding en noun rare
[ˈuðˌlɛnˀeŋ] · UDLEHNDING · ud-læn-ding
foreigner
Du er udlænding.
You're a foreigner.
Du er en udlænding.
You're a foreigner.
udmelding en noun general
UDMELDING · ud-mel-ding
announcement
also: statement; message; withdrawal
Udmeldingen møder kritik.
The announcement is met with criticism.
U
udnytte verb general
UDNEWTTE · ud-nyt-te
to exploit
also: expedient use
Jeg vil ikke lade Tom udnytte mig.
I won't let Tom exploit me.
Vi bør udnytte de begrænsede naturresurser vi har, bedst muligt.
We should make the best of the limited natural resources we have.
udover adverb common
UTHOVER · ud-over
beyond; above; in addition to; besides
Der var ingen i lokalet udover Mary og John.
There was no one in the room besides Mary and John.
Tom spildte rødvin udover Marys hvide kjole.
Tom spilled red wine all over Mary's white dress.
udsat adjective common
UDSAT · ud-sat
vulnerable, exposed
Elizabeth blev udsat for tåregas.
Elizabeth got maced.
Han var udsat for seksuel chikane.
He was sexually harassed.
udseende et noun general
/udseːənə/ · UDSEENDE · ud-se-en-de
appearance, look, looks
Den her sjovt-udseende bil må være britisk.
This funny-looking car must be British.
Bedøm ikke et menneske ud fra dets udseende.
Don't judge a person by his appearance.
technology

U
udsende verb rare
UDSENDE · ud-sen-de
to broadcast
also: to transmit; to dispatch
Hør mere i dagens udsendelse.
Hear more in today's broadcast.
transport
udsigt en noun common
[ˈuðˌseɡ̊d̥] · UDSIGT · ud-sigt
view, vista
also: prospect
Sikken herlig udsigt!
What a beautiful view.
Sikke en flot udsigt.
What a beautiful sight!
udskifte verb rare
UDSKIFTE · ud-skif-te
to replace, substitute, change, swap
also: to sub out (a player)
Glem ikke at udskifte kattegruset.
Don't forget to change the cat litter.
Jeg er nødt til at udskifte det lysstofrør.
I must replace that fluorescent lamp.
udskyde verb general
UDSKEWDE · ud-sky-de
to delay, hold up, postpone
Det er på tide at holde op med at udskyde alting.
It's time to stop procrastinating.
Det er på tide at holde op med at udskyde tingene.
It's time to stop procrastinating.
U
udstilling en noun general
UDSTILLING · ud-stil-ling
exhibition; display; show
Museet har en udstilling af gamle våben.
The museum has an exhibit of ancient weapons.
udstyr et noun general
UDSTEWR · ud-styr
equipment
also: outfit, kit; accessories; trousseau
Laboratoriet har det nyeste udstyr.
The laboratory has the most up-to-date equipment.
Dette sygehus har en masse nyt udstyr.
This hospital has a lot of new equipment.
udsætte verb rare
UDSEHTTE · ud-sæt-te
to subject to
also: to postpone, to suspend
Han besluttede sig for, at udsætte sin afrejse.
He decided to postpone his departure.
Gør aldrig det i dag som du kan udsætte til i morgen.
Never do today what you can put off till tomorrow.
udtale et noun general
UDTALE · ud-ta-le
pronunciation
also: to say, to declare, to state; to express; to pronounce
Er fransk udtale svært?
Is French pronunciation difficult?
Dansk udtale er lidt svær.
Danish pronunciation is a little bit difficult.

udtryk et noun common
/udtrøk/ · UDTREWK · ud-tryk
expression
also: expression, token; phrase, locution; term
Det er et fast udtryk.
This is a set expression.
Det er et virkelig godt udtryk.
This is a very good phrase.
udvalg et noun general
UDVALG · ud-valg
committee
also: selection
Du er den udvalgte.
You are the chosen one.
Jeg er den udvalgte.
I am the chosen one.
udvalgt adjective general
UDVALGTE · ud-valg-te
selected; chosen; handpicked
Du er den udvalgte.
You are the chosen one.
Jeg er den udvalgte.
I am the chosen one.
udvide verb general
UDVITHE · ud-vi-de
to widen
also: to expand; to broaden; to enlarge
Lokalpolitikere modsatte sig udvidelsen af EU.
Local politicians opposed the enlargement of the European Union.
Esperanto er en enorm udvidelse af min horisont.
Esperanto is a huge expansion of my horizon.
U
udvikle verb common
/ˈuðˌveɡlə/ · UDVIKLE · ud-vik-le
to develop
also: to evolve
Handel hjælper lande med at udvikle sig.
Trade helps nations develop.
Vil Algeriet udvikle sig til et velkonsolideret demokrati?
Will Algeria become a consolidated democracy?
udvikling en noun common
/uðveɡleŋ/ · UDVIKLING · ud-vik-ling
development
also: generation; advance, progress; trend
Han er ekspert i bæredygtig udvikling.
He is an expert in sustainable development.
Økonomisk udvikling er vigtig for Afrika.
Economic development is important for Africa.
uge en noun core
UYE
week
Giv mig en uge.
Give me a week.
De kom sidste uge.
They came last week.
uheld et noun general
/uhɛlˀ/ · UHELD
bad luck
also: misfortune; unluckiness; mishap
Mange uheld skyldes uagtsomhed.
Many accidents arise from carelessness.
Tom spiste rottegift ved et uheld.
Tom accidentally ate some rat poison.

ukendt adjective general
[ˈuˌkʰɛnˀd̥] · UKENDT
unknown, unfamiliar
also: obscure
Dødsårsagen er ukendt.
The cause of death is unknown.
Antallet af sårede er ukendt.
The number of injured is unknown.
ulempe et noun rare
ULEMPE · ulem-pe
disadvantage, drawback
also: discomfort, inconvenience
"Hvilke ulemper er der?"
"What are the disadvantages?"

ulovlig adjective general
ULOVLEE · ulov-lig
illegal
Tom lavede en ulovlig U-vending.
Tom made an illegal U-turn.
ulykke et noun general
/uløkə/ · ULEWKKE · ulyk-ke
accident
Der skete en ulykke.
There was an accident.
Der er sket en ulykke.
There has been an accident.
U
umiddelbart adjective common
UMIDDELBART · umid-del-bart
immediately, happening straight away
also: immediately, adjacently, being separated by nothing
Umiddelbart er.
Immediately is.
technology
umulig adjective rare
UMULEE · umu-lig
impossible
Du er umulig at håndtere.
You are impossible to deal with.
Hans håndskrift er umulig at tyde.
His handwriting is impossible to decipher.
underskud et noun general
[ˈɔnʌˌsɡuð] · UNDERSKUD · un-der-skud
deficit
Vi ser det ikke som et underskud.
We don't see it as a deficit.

UNDERSTREYE · un-der-stre-ge
to underline

Det faktum bør du understrege.

Lægen undersøger ham lige nu.
The doctor is examining him right now.
undersøge verb common
UNDERSURYE · un-der-sø-ge
to examine, go over, look over, scrutinize
also: to test; to investigate
Jeg vil gerne have en mand til at undersøge mig.
I would like to have a man examine me.
Jeg vil gerne have en kvinde til at undersøge mig.
I would like to have a woman examine me.
education
undersøgelse et noun common
UNDERSURYEL-SEH · un-der-sø-gel-se
examination, investigation, test
also: inquiry
Undersøgelsen er i gang.
The investigation is under way.
Undersøgelsen er endnu ikke afsluttet.
"The investigation hasn't yet been concluded."
education
undervejs adverb common
UNDERVAYS · un-der-vejs
on the way; underway; in progress; en route
Tom var undervejs.
Tom was on the way.
Projektet er undervejs.
The project is underway.
U
undervisning en noun common
UNDERVISNING · un-der-vis-ning
education, schooling
also: teaching, instruction, lessons
I august er der ingen undervisning.
There's no school in August.
education
undgå verb common
[ˈɔnˌɡ̊ɔˀ] · UNDGAW · und-gå
to avoid, evade, escape
Undgå dem.
Avoid those.
Undgå Tom.
Avoid Tom.
undre verb rare
[ˈɔnd̥ʁɐ] · UNDRE · un-dre
to surprise
also: to be a surprise, be a wonder; to wonder, be surprised
Det ville undre mig hvis Tom gjorde det.
It would surprise me if Tom does that.
undtagelse et noun general
UNDTAYEL-SEH · und-ta-gel-se
exception
Disse regler gælder for alle uden undtagelse.
Those regulations all apply to everyone without a single exception.
Udmærket, vi vil gøre en undtagelse i dit tilfælde.
Very well, we'll make an exception in your case.

ung adjective common
[ˈɔŋˀ] · UNG

unusual

utrolig adjective general
/utroːˀli/ · UTROLEE · ut-ro-lig
incredible, unbelievable

Du er utrolig!
You're incredible.

Det var dit valg.

Drik vand.

Du har vand.

VANSKELIGT · van-ske-ligt

Det er vanskeligt.

Fransk er vanskeligt at lære.
French is difficult to learn.
vant et noun general
VANT
usual, familiar
Jeg er vant til at tabe.
I'm used to losing.
Tom er vant til arbejdet.
Tom is used to the work.
vare en noun general
/vaːrə/ · VARE · va-re
article
also: commodity; item; goods, merchandise, wares
Jeg bad dig om at vare din mund!
I told you to watch your mouth!

It's going to take days.

varm adjective general
VARM
warm, hot
Teen er varm.
The tea is hot.
Hans hud var varm.

body

washing

[(ˈ)ʋeð] · VED
at, by
also: in case of, in the event of; upon, on, onto; nearby
Hvem ved?
Who knows?
Vi ved alt.
We know everything.
vedkommende et noun common
VEDKOMMENDE · ved-kom-men-de
part
Vedkommende er nonbinær.
They're non-binary.
Er vedkommende lykkelig?

[ˈʋeðˌtˢæˀ] · VEDTAYE · ved-ta-ge
to pass, decide
Torsdag er ny lov vedtaget.

vegne noun general
[ˈʋɑjnə] · VENE · veg-ne
behalf
Har du ledt alle vegne?

Der var mennesker alle vegne.
There were people everywhere.
vej en noun core
[ˈʋɑjˀ] · VAY
road (strip of land made suitable for travel, the path or route one takes)
also: way; route
Denne vej!
Right this way.
Gå din vej!
Go away!

vejledning en noun general
/vajleːˀðneŋ/ · VAYLEDNING · vej-led-ning
guidance, instruction
also: manual, guidelines; supervision
Tom har brug for vejledning.
Tom needs guidance.
Jeg har brug for noget vejledning.
I need some guidance.
culture
vejr et noun common
/ˈvɛːˀr/ · VAYR
weather
also: breath; wind, compass point
Sikke et vejr.
What weather!
Det er tåget vejr.
The weather is foggy.
food weather

/ˈvɛl/ · VEL
well
also: I suppose, maybe; is it the case
Det er ikke dine, vel?
These aren't yours, are they?
Tom havde vel en grund.
Tom must've had a reason.
velfærd en noun general
/vɛlfɛːˀr/ · VELFEHRD · vel-færd

also: prosperity, wealth
Der skulle skabes velfærd.
Well-being had to be created.

/ˈvɛlˌkɔmˀən/ · VELKOMMEN · vel-kom-men

Welcome.

Prosperity and climate are inextricably linked.

V
ven en noun general
/vɛn/ · VEN
friend
also: boyfriend
Hej, min ven!
Hi, friend!
Du er min ven.
You are my friend.
social
vende verb common
[ˈʋɛnə] · VENDE · ven-de
to turn (all senses)
also: to turn over (all senses); to discuss, debate
Kan du vende pladen?
Can you flip over the record?
Tom så Mary vende hjem alene.
Tom saw Mary return home alone.
venstre adjective core
VENSTRE · ven-stre
left
Drej til venstre.
Turn to the left.
Min venstre hæl er betændt.
My left heel is inflamed.
vente verb common
/vɛntə/ · VENTE · ven-te
to wait
also: to expect
Kan du vente?
Can you wait?
Vi bør vente.
We ought to wait.
V
verden en noun core
[ˈʋaɐ̯d̥n̩] · VERDEN · ver-den
world
Hej verden!
Hello world!
Hej, verden!
Hello world!
vest et noun general
/vɛst/ · VEST
west
also: toward the west, westwards; A .
Solen går altid ned i vest.
The sun always sets in the west.
Tårnet hældte lidt mod vest.
The tower leaned slightly to the west.
vestlig adjective rare
VESTLEE · ve-st-lig
western
also: westerly
Tyfonen bevægede sig i vestlig retning.
The typhoon moved in a westerly direction.
via preposition common
/ˈviːæ/ · VIA
; by way of; passing through.
also: , by means of, using.
Han tog til London via Paris.
He went to London via Paris.
Det kan være ensomt at arbejde hjemmefra via computeren.
Working from home via computer can be lonely.

V
vide verb common
/viːðə/ · VITHE · vi-de
to know
Jeg vil vide alt.
I want to know everything.
Hvad vil de vide?
What do they want to know?
viden en noun common
/viːðən/ · VITHEN · vi-den
knowledge
Han har viden og erfaring.
He has knowledge and experience.
Fantasi er vigtigere end viden.
Imagination is more important than knowledge.
video en noun common
VITHEO · vi-deo
video recorder
Tom så en YouTube-video.
Tom watched a YouTube video.
Denne video er the shit.
This video is the shit.
videre adjective core
/vidərə/ · VITHERE · vi-de-re
further
also: farther, further; on; go on
Kør videre.
Drive on.
Kom videre!
Stop beating a dead horse.
V
vidne et noun general
/ˈviðnə/ · VIDNE · vid-ne
witness
also: to testify, give evidence
Tom vil måske vidne.
Tom might testify.
Sami ville ikke vidne.
Sami didn't want to testify.
vidt adjective common
[ˈvid̥] · VIDT
wide
also: widely; far
Jeg gik for vidt.
I went too far.
Han gik for vidt.
He went too far.
vigtig adjective common
[ˈve̝ɡ̊d̥i] · VIGTEE · vig-tig
important
Du er vigtig!
You're important!
Den er vigtig.
It's important.
vigtigste adjective common
VIGTIGSTE · vig-tig-ste
supd
Hvad er det vigtigste for dig?
What's most important to you?
Det vigtigste er at du forstår mig.
The most important thing is that you understand me.

V
vild adjective general
[ˈʋilˀ] · VILD
wild
also: fierce, ferocious; savage, barbarian; desire, will
Jeg for vild.
I got lost.
Jeg fór vild.
I got lost.
vildt et noun general
VILDT
game wild animals for hunting
also: venison meat from wild animals
Du ser helt vildt lækker ud.
You look absolutely smoking hot.
Hun er vildt forelsket i ham.
She's madly in love with him.
vilje et noun general
/viljə/ · VILYE · vil-je
will
Det var ikke med vilje.
It wasn't on purpose.
Du gjorde det med vilje!
You did it on purpose!
health
vilkår et noun general
VILKAWR · vil-kår
term, condition, stipulation, circumstance
Ikke på vilkår!
No way!
V
villa en noun general
VILLA · vil-la
a villa detached house with garden around, intended for living, often larger
Villaen harmonerede med landskabet.
The villa was harmonious with the scenery.
ville verb rare
/vilə/
to want to, be willing to
also: shall, will; should, would
Alex kender mange i Haslev, som gerne ville lykønske ham.
vind en noun general
/ʋend/ · VIND
crooked, warped
also: wind; trend; development; flow
Der blæste en kold vind.
A cold wind was blowing.
Der er en kold vind fra nord.
There's a cold wind from the north.
vinde en noun common
/venə/ · VINDE · vin-de
reel
also: win; gain; achieve
Vi skal vinde.
We have to win.
Jeg vil vinde.
I want to win.

vindue et noun general
/vendu/ · VINDUE · vin-due
window

Open a window.
Vindue eller gang?

vinter en noun general
/ˈvenˀdɐ/ · VINTER · vin-ter
winter
Det var vinter.
It was winter.
Jeg hader vinter.
I hate winter.
virke et noun general
VIRKE · vir-ke
function properly
also: to work, to labor; exert influence upon; seem, appear
Har du set det virke?
Have you seen it work?
Mit tv er holdt op med at virke.
My TV has quit working.
virkelig adjective common
VIRKELEE · vir-ke-lig
real
also: actual; really, truly
Virkelig?
You don't say.
Nej virkelig?
Really?
V
virkelighed en noun general
VIRKELIGHETH · vir-ke-lig-hed
reality
Mød den forvrængede virkelighed!
Meet the twisted reality!
Toms frygt blev til virkelighed.
Tom's fear came true.
virksomhed en noun common
VIRKSOMHETH · virk-som-hed
company, enterprise, corporation
Min virksomhed er succesfuld.
My company is successful.
Tom solgte sin virksomhed til Mary.

virus
Kunne det være en virus?
Could it be a virus?
Kunne det være et virus?
Could it be a virus?
vis en noun common
[ˈʋiˀs] · VIS
manner, way
also: wise; sure, certain; certain, a
Vis mig!
Show me.
Vis mig det.
Show me that.

V
vise en noun common
[ˈʋiːsə] · VISE · vi-se
song, ballad, ditty
also: to show; to prove; show off
Gud vil vise dig vejen.
God will show you the way.
Jeg kan vise dig billederne.
I can show you the pictures.

vokse verb general
/vɔksə/ · VOKSE · vok-se
to grow
also: to grow into, become; to wax
Kan appelsintræer vokse i troperne?
Can orange trees grow in the tropics?
Teaktræet er et løvfældende træ der kan vokse til en højde på 40 meter.
The teak is a deciduous tree that can grow to a height of 40 metres.
voksen en noun general
/vɔksən/ · VOKSEN · vok-sen
adult, grown-up
also: full-grown
Tom er voksen.
Tom is an adult.
Tom er en voksen mand.
Tom is a grown man.
vold en noun common
/vɔlˀ/ · VOLD
violence
also: force; assault and battery; embankment
Vold avler mere vold.
Violence breeds more violence.
Blod og vold fascinerer dem.
Blood and violence fascinate them.
V
voldsom adjective general
VOLDSOM · vold-som
forceful, violent (in the sense: involving extreme force, not necessarily implying any physical conflict)
Det regner voldsomt.
It is raining heavily.
Der var et voldsomt regnvejr.
A heavy rain fell.
voldtægt en noun general
[ˈʋʌlˌtˢɛɡ̊d̥] · VOLDTEHGT
rape
Angriberens soldater var intet andet end tyve, mordere og voldtægtsforbrydere i uniform.

VORES · vo-res
our, ours
Det er vores.
It's ours.

[ʋuɐ̯ˈd̥eˀɐ] · VURDERE · vur-de-re
to assess, evaluate
Det vurderer vi løbende.
We assess this on an ongoing basis.

vurdering en noun common

vægt en noun general
[ˈʋɛɡ̊d̥] · VEHGT
weight
also: scales; Libra
Hvad er Toms vægt?
What's Tom's weight?
Tom lyver om sin vægt.
Tom is lying about his weight.
væk adverb core
[ˈvɛɡ̊] · VEHK
gone
also: away
Væk!
Away!
vække verb rare
/vɛkə/ · VEHKKE · væk-ke
to wake up
also: to arouse, cause; to awaken
Undskyld, jeg kom til at vække dig.
I'm sorry for waking you up.
Tom bad mig vække ham klokken halv syv.
Tom asked me to wake him at six-thirty.
V
vækst en noun general
/vɛkst/ · VEHKST
growth
also: growth, increase; plant
Sarkozy forsvarede en model for bæredygtig vækst.
Sarkozy defended a model of sustainable growth.
Det vi republikanere skal stå for, er vækst i økonomien. Vi bør gøre kagen højere.
What we Republicans should stand for is growth in the economy. We ought to make the pie higher.
vælge verb common
[ˈʋɛljə] · VEHLYE · væl-ge
to choose
also: to select; to elect
Jeg vil vælge en.
I will choose one.
Jeg vil lade dig vælge.
I'll let you decide.
vælger en noun common
VEHLYER · væl-ger
voter
Jeg vælger dig.
I choose you.
Poeter vælger de bedste ord.
Poets select the best words.
vælte verb rare
/vɛltə/ · VEHLTE · væl-te
to overturn, roll
also: to roll, wallow
Giraffen kan ikke svømme fordi dens tyngdepunkt ligger så højt at den ville vælte.
The giraffe cannot swim because its centre of gravity is so high that it would topple over.

værd et noun general

værdi en noun general
/vɛrˈdiːˀ/ · VEHRDI · vær-di
value
also: worth, merit; monetary value, worth

This article is of no value.

supd

væsentlig adjective general
[ˈvɛˀsənd̥li] · VEHSENTLEE · væ-sent-lig
essential
also: important; considerable, substantial; significantly
Denne kandidat er væsentligt bedre end sin forgænger.
This candidate is significantly better compared to his predecessor.
Man ser kun klart med hjertet. Det væsentlige er usynligt for øjet.

våben et noun general
/vɔːbən/ · VAWBEN · vå-ben
a weapon

Til våben!

[ˈyðʌliʌʌ]
additional, further
also: furthermore, additionally

EWDERST
extremely; utterly; outermost

That's extremely interesting.

[ˈɛlˀsd̥] · EHLDST

ændre verb common

change

what a pity; annoying; unfortunate

ærgre verb rare
/ˈɛrɡrə/ · EHRGRE · ærg-re
to annoy

ø et noun rare
[ˈøˀ] · UR
island

We live on an island.
Jeg ejer den ø.
I own that island.
ødelægge verb rare
/øːðəlɛɡə/ · URDELEHGYE · øde-læg-ge
to destroy
also: to ruin
Vi vil ødelægge deres huler.
We'll destroy their dens.
Min eks ønsker at ødelægge mit nye forhold.
My ex wants to ruin my new relationship.
øge verb common
/øːjə/ · URYE
to increase, enhance
also: to increase
Øgede CO2-niveauer bidrager væsentligt til generel mangel på næringsstoffer i vores afgrøder.
Increased CO₂ levels are significantly contributing to a general lack of nutrients in our crops.
Øgede CO₂-niveauer bidrager væsentligt til generel mangel på næringsstoffer i vores afgrøder.
Increased CO₂ levels are significantly contributing to a general lack of nutrients in our crops.
øget verb common
URYET
to increase
Med øget hastighed.
With increased speed.
Ø
øje et noun common
/ɔjə/ · URYE
eye
also: to see, watch
Hold øje med ham.
Keep an eye on him.
Hold øje med Tom.
Keep your eye on Tom.
body
øjeblik en noun general
/øjəblek/ · URYEBLIK · øje-blik
blink of an eye, moment
Lige et øjeblik!
Just a moment.
Vent et øjeblik.
Wait a moment.
body
økonomi en noun common
URKONOMI · øko-no-mi
economy
also: economics; financial circumstances
De forsøger at lamme Ruslands økonomi.
They're trying to cripple Russia's economy.
Den amerikanske økonomi har lidt under en recession.
The American economy suffered a recession.
økonomisk adjective common
URKONOMISK · øko-no-misk
economic, financial
also: economical
Økonomisk udvikling er vigtig for Afrika.
Economic development is important for Africa.
Jeg er økonomisk uafhængig af mine forældre.
I am economically independent of my parents.

[ønsɡə] · URNSKE · øn-ske

øst et noun common
[ˈøsd̥] · URST
east
also: east, eastwards

Japan is to the east of China.

/ˈøːvrə/ · URVRE · øv-re
upper

/øvri/ · URVREE · øv-rig
other, remaining

URVRIGT · øv-rigt
besides; moreover

/ɔːbən/ · AWBEN
open
also: frank, candid
Døren er åben.

AWBENBART · åben-bart
apparently

Vi kom åbenbart lige i rette tid.

AWBNING · åb-ning

also: a beginning

/ɔːˀr/ · AWR

She is thirty-one.

for years (and years)

yearly, annually

/ɔrsaːˀɣ/ · AWRSAG · år-sag
cause, reason

It winds through rolling hills, its name is old Denmark, and it is Freja’s hall, and it is Freja’s hall! Our old Denmark must remain as long as the beech mirrors its top in the blue waves, its top in the blue waves.
Der er et yndigt land, det står med brede bøge nær salten østerstrand, nær salten østerstrand. Det bugter sig i bakke dal, det hedder gamle Danmark, og det er Frejas sal, og det er Frejas sal! Vort gamle Danmark skal bestå så længe bøgen spejler, sin top i bølgen blå, sin top i bølgen blå.

Our old Denmark must remain as long as the beech mirrors its top in the blue waves, its top in the blue waves.

top
There is a lovely country; it stands with wide beeches near a salty eastern beach, near a salty eastern beach. It winds through rolling hills, its name is old Denmark, and it is Freja’s hall, and it is Freja’s hall! Our old Denmark must remain as long as the beech mirrors its top in the blue waves, its top in the blue waves.
Der er et yndigt land, det står med brede bøge nær salten østerstrand, nær salten østerstrand. Det bugter sig i bakke dal, det hedder gamle Danmark, og det er Frejas sal, og det er Frejas sal! Vort gamle Danmark skal bestå så længe bøgen spejler, sin top i bølgen blå, sin top i bølgen blå.
